title: Best-one London
review_text: I normally have no issue with this store, it’s small and has limited selection but is what you would expect from a brick lane convenience store.  When I went today (mid Friday) it was randomly closed with no note, and nobody appeared for 10 minutes while I waited. Unfortunately it is the only my Hermes parcel drop off place nearby, so I was more than frustrated by this closure during my only free time this week.

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text: Lovely staff and well stocked. Good selection of products especially with the premade food section and very reasonable prices. Would definitely recommend anyone to stop by as they also have a good selection of different snacks and drinks too. But especially the staff is genuinely so polite and helpful!!!

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text: Dropped luggage off for the day. Staff were friendly and helpful and the store layout was a nice size. Had a look around and brought some drinks before leaving.

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text:
My family and I have been loyal local customers for years, but recently I’ve had to stop coming due to a senior member of staff at this branch. Whenever I come to collect a parcel, he gives attitude, makes me wait unnecessarily, and lectures customers on the “best times” to collect — even when I've follow that advice. No matter what time I come, there’s always an issue. If you offer a parcel collection service, please don’t be annoyed when customers actually use it!

I’m never one to complain, but today, after months of avoiding this place, I had to collect a parcel because it was my only local option. Even before arriving, I felt anxious about dealing with this individual — and unfortunately, my instincts were right. He was huffing and puffing again, giving me another lecture about collection times. Usually, I just let it go, but after a long day at work, a dentist appointment, and trying to fit this in during my lunch break, it was genuinely upsetting to be met with such a bad attitude when I’d done nothing wrong and was patiently waiting my turn.

This experience has really put me off returning, which is a shame given how long my family and I have supported this business.

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text: Very reasonable prices, & well stocked! We stopped by this store to buy water and snacks during our stay at a nearby hotel. The store is well-stocked and prices were more than reasonable compared to other similar stores nearby.

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text:
Brilliant supermarket with very fair prices and a very friendly member of staff. Really helpful and very hardworking. Treated everyone in the store with a really helpful attitude.

Brilliant.

title: NISA LOCAL, Chalton Street, London
review_text:
Unhelpful and rude staff. Just went to drop off my package and they refused to help me and did not provide a real response, not even looking at me and looking away to their colleagues when I walked up to the counter.

When I tried to speak to their other colleague, he just stated "not now, come back later" and proceeded to ignore me.

I didn't even ask a question, and they just brushed me off entirely, and went to serve another customer.

It wasn't even that busy! I just walked out because I couldn't deal with this rudeness - I'm not sure if this was racially motivated, but I am of Asian descent and they probably assumed I couldn't speak good English, which is not an excuse to ignore then or not let them speak!!

I've read other reviews and it seems like other people have the same issue. Seems like they haven't changed - or don't care enough to. AVOID!

title: Field Store
review_text: The most unhappiest most depressing shop owner. I tried to say hello and be polite and he looked at me like I stabbed him in his foot or something. We had a lovely day out and on our way home we stopped here and this shop keepers attitude really put a dampener on the rest of my day. I vowed never to go in there after a few times of seeing what he’s like but this time had no choice we had too. NEVER EVER AGAIN! If he smiled even a little bit he would have many customers.

title: Field Store
review_text: I asked my husband to buy me some crackers and orange juice on the way home so he stopped by the field store.  The Ritz crackers he got me smelled like bug spray and moth balls.   On top of that the store doesn’t even have any fresh juices or anything close.  I’ve also been to this place to drop off collect plus parcels and they don’t take any package that is in a box or that is slightly larger than 30x30x30.  The angry thing is I had called collect plus before and they advised me that the store should take my parcel and that it was within the dimensions.  I had the collect plus representative call them and the store owner just lied about the whole incident.   Don’t come here unless you want to eat eat poison products and be greeted by an angry store owner!

title: Simply Fresh
review_text:

The staff are polite and helpful, and checkout is quick even during busy times. Perfect for grabbing something on the go. Highly recommend if you’re nearby!

title: Lords City Store
open_state: Closes soon · 9 pm · Opens 7 am Mon
review_text:
Wow. Ok, I don’t know where to start.

DO NOT SUPPORT THIS BUSINESS BY ALL COST.

The customer service is frankly terrible. I accompanied my Chinese friend into the store and was met by an attitude from one of the shopkeeper, who is of ethnic background. My friend was trying to retrieve an Inpost parcel that has been sat at their shop for 10 days, relentlessly going in trying to receive it and instead met with neglect and racism from the situation multiple times. This man mocked her intelligence to me saying “your friend doesn’t understand” and “she’s lying, the parcel is not here”. This standard of service is unbelievable and extremely xenophobic!!

I recommend to not support this off licence.

title: My Shop Local
review_text: This has got to be one of the worst corner stores I have ever visited. This is right next to a school so I figured out their technique. title: My Shop Local
review_text: Every single time I go in it feels like they making me such a huge a huge favour. Their customer service is probably the worst I've experienced from a corner shop. The only reason I have seen myself forced to shop is due to proximity /location but I happily try my best to walk to actual supermarkets just because I can't handle their rudeness and lack of manners. Prices are high and fruits and vegetables are rarely in good conditions but what really bothers me is their customer service and the atmosphere.

title: R S General Store
review_text:
I wandered to the Nisa on a quiet corner near Murray grov, following the glowing promise from Google Maps that the shop would be open. My little cat who walks with me and softly stomps like a distant  thunder cloud trotted beside me, her tail a curious antenna tuning into the neighbourhood.

But when we arrived, the shutters were already drawn, and the lights inside were dark,
For a moment it felt as if time had folded strangely, like a tiny cosmic hiccup. I pressed my palm to the cool metal door and listened, hoping for the hum of a fridge or the distant rustle of tayto packets, but everything was still.

My cat looked up at me with that patient feline expression that says, “the universe is unpredictable; this is simply its rhythm.” And I suppose she was right

title: R S General Store
review_text:
This nisa is unhygienic, overpriced and unsafe.

From the out of date food they try and sell as reduced, to the moulding fruits and veg.

The crowded aisles are badly organised with item stacked on tops of each which can easy fall and hurt a customer.

In addition the food is overpriced and the fact that the staff are not helpful and can not be bothered to put labels on items or on the self.

Also the staff are there watching movies or on a call or even eating whilst serving customers.

This shop definitely needs an inspection because the conditions are vulgar and this shop need to be shut down or fined.

title: R S General Store
review_text:
Extremely extremely rude staff. The rudest I've ever had the displeasure to come across anywhere in the world.

I wanted to buy a punnet of strawberries this evening but each of all the remaining punnets had rotten strawberries in them so I picked one and headed to the till to ask politely if I could swap the very CLEARLY ROTTEN (black and furry) strawberries in this punnet with some non rotten ones in another punnet. The rudest older man I've ever met was at the till and refused, was condescending, impossibly rude and chauvinistic in his response to my request. He ordered me to get another punnet rather than swap the current one which I declined to do as THEY WERE ALL AFFECTED WITH ROTTEN FRUIT. I insisted on just having the 3 rotten ones taken out and swapped, he took a second punnet and proceeded to pick out the most rotten ones out of them to add to my punnet. I repeat, he selected the most rotten fruit in the second punnet and proceeded to rudely flick them into my basket with my other produce. When I picked these out and told him I didn't wish to buy rotten fruits he rudely dismissed me and started to scan the ROTTEN BOX OF STRAWBERRIES in a bid to force me to buy rotten fruits. At this stage of the twilight zone I found myself in, I decided to leave the strawberries and not buy them at all to which he then once again responded to me by making a disrespectful sound under his voice that I heard. After that I refused to buy anything else and told him to cancel the purchases. He offered not a single apology but doubled down on it by rudely telling me to go away.

I will never EVER EVER BUY FROM THIS SHOP EVER AGAIN. I used to go there at least twice a week since moving to the area and while I know that my contribution to their bottom line is at best minimal to insignificant, due to my pride I refuse to give my hard earned money to a shop that actively sees and treats me lower than a backyard farm animal. Even backyard farm animals aren't fed rotten foods.

title: R S General Store
review_text: Old guy at the till (owner?) keeps trying to charge me double for products (prices change daily when he is there and you never get charged the price that was on the shelf) or add cheeky something (GBP 3-4!!!) to my final bill. He never gives receipts and when I ask for one, i usually get one where prices are amended on 80% of items. Stealing from loyal customers - absolutely ridiculous and counterproductive as I won't go back.

title: The convenience Store
address: 63 St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8LE
review_text: I strongly recommend not using this store as a UPS drop off point. My return label stated clearly that it was a "No-Box and No-Label" return, meaning that any UPS drop off point would package, label, and ship the items for free. The store simply did not. Only refusing would have been fine - there are always other drop off points - but instead the man working offered me an old Evian box filled with empty crisp packets and lottery tickets, along with a roll of tape, and assured me that if I kept the items together in the box, UPS themselves package the items properly and ship them. They do not. Please use another drop off point.

I called into this shop when I saw the Palestinian flag outside as I've just returned from the beautiful country.
Amazing to see in the depths of London a shop showing solidarity with Palestine. I immediately contacted the Belata refugee camp and told them about my experience.

I can't express how important this support is for Palestinian people 🙏🙏🙏

title: Retail 24
review_text:
Like other reviewers, I've also had a parcel disappear after dropping it off here. I can't say whether the staff or Hermes is at fault, but the tracking number never showed my parcel being picked up from this location after I dropped it off. The items are not stored securely, so anybody could have taken it from the shop.

I would not recommend mailing valuable items or Amazon returns from this location. This led to an insane amount of back-and-forth with Amazon for me, and ultimately I got my money back, but I wouldn't want anyone else to go through the experience I went through.

title: Shop On The Hill
review_text:
Hey to all. I’ve lived in many London addresses over past years, I’ve now settled in N19 for the past 11 years and I’m very grateful to have The Shop On The Hill as my local convenience Store. I’m blessed as they are not only my local but actually great people to get to know as a longstanding customer.

From the experienced professional and personable owner and his son as well all the pleasant caring staff, they are all simply amazing, extremely helpful and add a personal touch every time I visit the shop.

Best equipped to suit all my personal needs, amazing range of variety’s to suit all customers who visit

Personable, very friendly, professional, caring and most of all always willing to assist

Thank you guys

title: Shop On The Hill

title: Shop On The Hill
review_text: I have been a customer for years. Good honest and friendly service. The quality of products is outstanding and they never over charge their customers. They have so many other products which you sometimes cannot find in other shops and its the best place to go when you are hosting a feast for friends and family. Highly recommend to all. Traditional Mediterranean foods at good prices. You can find most of what you need. Staff are also very helpful which is always good. 5***** store all day long.

title: Shop On The Hill
review_text:
Great shop, friendly staff and good selection. Fresh fruit and veg out front.

I was prompted to write this after reading Romain's review. The man doesn't make any sense - he suggests the shopkeepers increase their prices depending on the person they are serving but provides no evidence for this. Instead he offers you the fact that he found products cheaper elsewhere - boohoo! If you like elsewhere so much then why do you not go shop there. For what it's worth, I think Express Supermarket is fairly standard for prices.

title: Shop On The Hill
review_text: Very friendly family owned business. They have been around for years and I have been coming since I was a child. My favourite part of the store is their wine selection which is impressively vast. Their prices are competitive and have everything you need which is such a convenience for me and my family. Thank you.

title: The Whisky Exchange - Great Portland Street Shop
place_title: The Whisky Exchange - Great Portland Street Shop
review_text:
I’ve always come here to shop for rare whiskey to bring back to Japan and enjoyed looking at the variety available especially on the floor below. Today, asked if I could go downstairs and then told no because the stock there are expensive and more than £200. First, why are you judging me on what I can and can’t afford?

I reluctantly ended up selecting from the choice available on the ground floor but staff were chatting loudly amongst themselves, uninterested in helping. I ended up buying a kilkerran, which wasn’t wrapped properly (didn’t even bother to tape the wrap, my two year old would wrap better. Imagine bringing this to a place like Japan and handing this to your boss 😂)

It’s a shame they have good stuff and I’ve enjoyed the conversations I’ve had with some of the staff (most times female) but I won’t be coming here again 😅

Visited 11 am 22/09.

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: Brilliant shop! Went  as I collect the pins from all different Hard Rock locations. Went on Saturday and was busy but well worth it! Even explored The Vault and learnt so much! Was super cool to see all the memorabilia and the guy telling us everything was funny and informative. He even took pics of us to mimic Ringo and have fun exploring round answering any questions. Eould deffo go again and even grabbed more merch there! My brother even loved it as his 1st time in one and got some shirts and bandanas even blanket deal! Brilliant day out! 💜💜

title: Tate Modern Shop
review_text: Amazing gift shop! So much for all ages too. They have great prints and so many small items of art that you can buy. They have a very good selection of books. Some books are about the art in the museum some are about creating art as well. There is a section for kids as well in the back. If you are looking for souvenirs then this is a good place to go!

title: Tate Modern Shop
review_text: This store won't come out of my head. So many amazing books, and other very cool gifts. I'd return every time I'm in London.

title: Tate Modern Shop
review_text:
THEY DO NOT GIFT WRAP. I actually really like this shop and they have a great selection of carefully curated and beautiful things to choose from. However, almost nothing is unique and you can get pretty much everything at 20-40% discount elsewhere. To shop there is to pay a premium for the experience which I don’t mind since it would go toward supporting the Tate.

However, when they told me they do not gift wrap, I was quite surprised and to be honest very disappointed. To have a piece of Tate brought home to my family and to tell them all about the strange and wonderful art within would have been something special. Without the gift wrapping to differentiate, whatever I would have brought I might as well have bought them on Amazon and save the luggage space and money.

title: Selfridges Corner Shop
review_text:
Elton John has taken over Selfridges Corner Shop (Selfridges Oxford St London, front right hand side of the store) till tge 8th of May 2023.

Along with the merchandise for sale, the real joy of this pop-up are Elton's iconic stage costumes from the early 1970s. Yes, 50yrs ago.

These bring some much needed colour to Oxford St and just to see them makes you smile, whether you like the music or not.

One of Selfridges better corner shop experiences. Go take a look. It's free.

title: Selfridges Corner Shop
review_text:
A daring new exploration of fashion, art, retail & technology as fashion house Paco Rabanne takes its inspiration from the late Op Artist, Victor Vasarely by displaying both his work (first UK Exhibition in 50 years) as well, as the purchasable products inspired by it entitled "Universe"

Sculpture, objects & video visuals of Vasarely are blened with costumes, dresses & accessories from the fashion label and all linked via technologgy and the inevitable NFTs produced for sale too from £2,000 - £100,000 which can be puchased at the till with no need for Crypto Currency (which seems to miss the point of NFTs).

Much of the funds raised will got to supporting the Varseley Foundation & ensures Victor's collection is appropriately archived and protected for generations to come.

Opened 13th January 2022 - Closes 31st March 2022

title: Selfridges Corner Shop

title: St John's Wood General Store
address: 17 St John's Wood High St, London, NW8 7NG
review_text:
St. John’s Wood General Store is an upmarket grocery shop with friendly staff & a very wide range of high-quality products. 👍 When I browsed through the shelves, I saw quite a few things I had never seen before - even in the chocolate section which I always explore in detail at any shop I visit! 🍫😁
Located right on St. John’s Wood High Street, it’s not surprising that they focus on premium products for premium prices 💷. If you regularly shop at Panzer’s around the corner, checking out the General Store might give you some additional options!

title: the Design Museum Shop
review_text: The gift sop presents a great way of showing off how design/art can be incorporated into every day life. Many of the gadgets are are useful and all of them are fashionable. They also make great gifts for all ages, everything from colourful toys for kids to utility chop sticks for the unable at an affordable price. It also offers great quality print art. Furthermore the inventory is always changing, so you will discover something new every time you visit.

I ended up arguing with this man as my little baby just grabbed one of the items and he started shouting to me saying that we need to get out, I told him many times that I would put the item back but he wouldn’t stop shouting to us, I was about to call police but thought it was not worth it waste any time on that guy, I DEFINITELY DONT RECOMMEND TO COME TO THIS SHOP TO BUY ANYTHING, there is thousands of souvenirs shops better than this.

title: My Shop Local
review_text: Absolutely wonderful local shop! It's got a phenomenal variety of products to choose from!! From baby products to birthday cards, fresh vegetables to flowers and anything in-between....the staff are always welcoming and will say hello as you enter and it's just had a full revamp and it looks great! I will say the prices are a tad higher then usual places but the selection on offer makes up for it!

title: My Shop Local
review_text: States this is a post office shop and they sell packages here. They do nothing of the sort, completely misleading.

title: My Shop Local
review_text:
Beware, bloke behind the till scammed me last week. Took for 4 items and charged me and then said he hadn’t put one through and charged me again for it.
It wasn’t until it finally came through on my phone a bit later it confirmed what I had suspected.
A lot of tenants in that area, I will be warning all of them.

title: My Shop Local
review_text: I had a great experience at this corner shop! It has a good variety of essentials, and the prices are very reasonable. The staff was extremely helpful, taking the time to show me everything I needed. Excellent customer service—thank you! I’ll definitely be coming back when im next in the area!

title: My Shop Local
review_text: I live next door to My Shop, unlike supermarkets where there is virtually no communication with customers, here there is, after a while they get to know their customers, and say, how are you. Their stock is considerable, they are open from seven in the morning till eleven p.m. It is a universal shop, regarding the staff, it is a shame that the world cannot be like this.

title: My Shop Local
review_text:
I bought a Oreo ice cream sandwich 😋 and it was SO DELICIOUS
recommended

title: My Shop Local
review_text: This has got to be one of the worst corner stores I have ever visited. This is right next to a school so I figured out their technique. Since children from the school want snacks and such, they go to the nearest shop… which is this one. But the thing is, everything in this shop is really expensive, so the children spend more than they should in this shop. The staff/person at the till always gives me a moody/rude vibe. He is not that friendly to customers and he looks like he just wants to grab our money and get out of there.

title: My Shop Local
review_text: Every single time I go in it feels like they making me such a huge a huge favour. Their customer service is probably the worst I've experienced from a corner shop. I feel intimidated and not welcome. The only reason I have seen myself forced to shop is due to proximity /location but I happily try my best to walk to actual supermarkets just because I can't handle their rudeness and lack of manners. Prices are high and fruits and vegetables are rarely in good conditions but what really bothers me is their customer service and the atmosphere.

title: My Shop London
review_text: I came during the week I saw the bald man owner and the cashier both of them were talking in Turkish about pest control. They have Mice and Rats from the underground were they kept food. Wasteman you think customers won’t know !!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

title: My Shop London
review_text: Genuinely the friendliest people I’ve ever met. Doesn’t matter what time or day they are the so welcoming. Never forceful but have everything you ever need. I’ve been in the day time for bread and milk and they double check you have what you need, and I’ve been there post 11pm where they shove the tunes on if they know you’ve been out drinking and have a dance with you. Whatever the occasion, this is the best corner shop I’ve ever had and I cannot understand anyone that rates them  less than 5 stars. The best shop ever <3

title: My Shop London
review_text: Nice staff just be prepared to pay extra for pretty much everything

title: My Shop London
review_text: This shop is not open 24 hours, I think it's open until 1or 2am. The staff are always helpful and friendly and they have a great supply of alcohol at reasonable prices. They also offer a collection point for delivery through companies such as Amazon , ASOS, very and many others. Another bonus of this shop is that they have a cash point which is free to use. EDIT - you now have to pay to use the cash machine 🙄😡

title: My Shop London

title: My Shop London
review_text: One of the best convenience stores in London, the staff are always polite, helpful and courteous, they're open late and have a fabulous selection of treats, cigarettes, the basics like bread and milk and a fantastic choice of booze and quality prices. This is the place to go, believe me! Top lads!

title: My Shop London

title: My Shop London

This is normal and we were about to send email to delivery company. But he start talking in his language with his colleague in front of us and shouting.  Rude

title: Retail 24
review_text: I’ve only ever had negative experiences here. Their parcel service is exceptionally bad. They leave stuff laying around it’s so unsecure and they have you dig up your stuff from giant boxes..They don’t accept heavy/large items saying it’s because the drivers don’t take them but the same drivers take them from other shops nearby. They’re unwilling to help, can’t show you a single policy for refusing your items..their stuff is overpriced but that’s general for these stores.

title: Retail 24
review_text:
Like other reviewers, I've also had a parcel disappear after dropping it off here. I can't say whether the staff or Hermes is at fault, but the tracking number never showed my parcel being picked up from this location after I dropped it off. The items are not stored securely, so anybody could have taken it from the shop.

I would not recommend mailing valuable items or Amazon returns from this location. This led to an insane amount of back-and-forth with Amazon for me, and ultimately I got my money back, but I wouldn't want anyone else to go through the experience I went through.

title: The Stock Shop

review_text:
Polite friendly staff, However they refuse to accept cash, No wonder shoplifting is rife in this country, There's the tax dodgers which only accept cash and then shops like this who make folks life difficult by saying card only. Not good if your stuck in hospital with only cash on your person..
Maybe learn to operate a cash register 🤔
Or is it a case of the staff are not trusted ?

title: The Corner Shop in Stepney
address: 130 Aylward St, Stepney Green, London, E1 0QW
review_text:
I called into this shop when I saw the Palestinian flag outside as I've just returned from the beautiful country.
Amazing to see in the depths of London a shop showing solidarity with Palestine. I immediately contacted the Belata refugee camp and told them about my experience.
They were so touched by your visible support and have translated my message to colleagues in the Yaffa Centre within the camp.
I can't express how important this support is for Palestinian people 🙏🙏🙏

review_text: Worst experience ever! The man at the till was really rude to a woman who was just asking for prices, he literally was kicking her out of the shop, even when I asked him if they have baby clothes he said no to me and I found lots of baby items inside. I ended up arguing with this man as my little baby just grabbed one of the items and he started shouting to me saying that we need to get out, I told him many times that I would put the item back but he wouldn’t stop shouting to us, I was about to call police but thought it was not worth it waste any time on that guy, I DEFINITELY DONT RECOMMEND TO COME TO THIS SHOP TO BUY ANYTHING, there is thousands of souvenirs shops better than this.

title: Retail Express
review_text:
Very frustrating experience, would not recommend. I am a very polite and calm spoken person and when I received a call from the manager of the store to say my order had arrived and explained I couldn’t collect straight away due to me working 6 days in the hospital, I was yelled at and threatened to collect or he would get rid of them and send them back to UPS. I explained I didn’t know when my packages would arrive and apologised for the inconvenience for them but was completely disrespected. So unprofessional and actually quite scary to be spoken in such a way. Not even my worst patients spoke to me like that lol.

Anyways, save your time and go elsewhere :)

Have a good day!

title: MS City Store

Nothing wrong with the size requirements for the shipping label paid for it. Showed them the details online but got nothing but excuses and them saying they don't want to accept it and said to go elsewhere. The parcels are just they have taken seem to be littered all over the shop floor. Went the the Tyson St Co-op instead and they took it with no issues

title: MS City Store
review_text:
There are 2 MS City Stores. One on Goswell Road and  another on 320 St John's Street - which has only a brown "off license" sign. But they have the same owners.

The store on St John's Street offers Randox tests pick up.

The person there was very nice and friendly, checked that I went to the other store already and scanned the collection bar code and dispensed the tests for me even though for him the scan was alwaready showing invalid code.

Thank you very much!

title: Lords City Store
review_text:
Wow. Ok, I don’t know where to start.

DO NOT SUPPORT THIS BUSINESS BY ALL COST.

The customer service is frankly terrible. I accompanied my Chinese friend into the store and was met by an attitude from one of the shopkeeper, who is of ethnic background. My friend was trying to retrieve an Inpost parcel that has been sat at their shop for 10 days, relentlessly going in trying to receive it and instead met with neglect and racism from the situation multiple times. This man mocked her intelligence to me saying “your friend doesn’t understand” and “she’s lying, the parcel is not here”. This standard of service is unbelievable and extremely xenophobic!!

I recommend to not support this off licence.

title: Field Store
review_text: The most unhappiest most depressing shop owner. I tried to say hello and be polite and he looked at me like I stabbed him in his foot or something. We had a lovely day out and on our way home we stopped here and this shop keepers attitude really put a dampener on the rest of my day. I vowed never to go in there after a few times of seeing what he’s like but this time had no choice we had too. NEVER EVER AGAIN! If he smiled even a little bit he would have many customers.

title: Field Store
review_text: I asked my husband to buy me some crackers and orange juice on the way home so he stopped by the field store.  The Ritz crackers he got me smelled like bug spray and moth balls.   On top of that the store doesn’t even have any fresh juices or anything close.  I’ve also been to this place to drop off collect plus parcels and they don’t take any package that is in a box or that is slightly larger than 30x30x30.  The angry thing is I had called collect plus before and they advised me that the store should take my parcel and that it was within the dimensions.  I had the collect plus representative call them and the store owner just lied about the whole incident.   Don’t come here unless you want to eat eat poison products and be greeted by an angry store owner!

title: Shoppers Stop
review_text: Terrible. I bought a heater from them that didn't work and then they refused to give me my money back. Had two men screaming at me and claiming that they don't give refunds, despite UK law stipulating this. They nearly gave me a credit note but then refused when I asked for the name of the manager. This store is clearly a front for illegal activity. I have reported them to Trading Standards and the Citizens Advice Bureau, plus my credit card company to avoid them defrauding other customers.

title: R S General Store
review_text:
I wandered to the Nisa on a quiet corner near Murray grov, following the glowing promise from Google Maps that the shop would be open. My little cat who walks with me and softly stomps like a distant  thunder cloud trotted beside me, her tail a curious antenna tuning into the neighbourhood.

But when we arrived, the shutters were already drawn, and the lights inside were dark,
For a moment it felt as if time had folded strangely, like a tiny cosmic hiccup. I pressed my palm to the cool metal door and listened, hoping for the hum of a fridge or the distant rustle of tayto packets, but everything was still.

My cat looked up at me with that patient feline expression that says, “the universe is unpredictable; this is simply its rhythm.” And I suppose she was right

title: R S General Store
review_text:
Extremely extremely rude staff. The rudest I've ever had the displeasure to come across anywhere in the world.

I wanted to buy a punnet of strawberries this evening but each of all the remaining punnets had rotten strawberries in them so I picked one and headed to the till to ask politely if I could swap the very CLEARLY ROTTEN (black and furry) strawberries in this punnet with some non rotten ones in another punnet. The rudest older man I've ever met was at the till and refused, was condescending, impossibly rude and chauvinistic in his response to my request. He ordered me to get another punnet rather than swap the current one which I declined to do as THEY WERE ALL AFFECTED WITH ROTTEN FRUIT. I insisted on just having the 3 rotten ones taken out and swapped, he took a second punnet and proceeded to pick out the most rotten ones out of them to add to my punnet. I repeat, he selected the most rotten fruit in the second punnet and proceeded to rudely flick them into my basket with my other produce. When I picked these out and told him I didn't wish to buy rotten fruits he rudely dismissed me and started to scan the ROTTEN BOX OF STRAWBERRIES in a bid to force me to buy rotten fruits. At this stage of the twilight zone I found myself in, I decided to leave the strawberries and not buy them at all to which he then once again responded to me by making a disrespectful sound under his voice that I heard. After that I refused to buy anything else and told him to cancel the purchases. He offered not a single apology but doubled down on it by rudely telling me to go away.

I will never EVER EVER BUY FROM THIS SHOP EVER AGAIN. I used to go there at least twice a week since moving to the area and while I know that my contribution to their bottom line is at best minimal to insignificant, due to my pride I refuse to give my hard earned money to a shop that actively sees and treats me lower than a backyard farm animal. Even backyard farm animals aren't fed rotten foods.

title: Selfridges Corner Shop
review_text:
A daring new exploration of fashion, art, retail & technology as fashion house Paco Rabanne takes its inspiration from the late Op Artist, Victor Vasarely by displaying both his work (first UK Exhibition in 50 years) as well, as the purchasable products inspired by it entitled "Universe"

Sculpture, objects & video visuals of Vasarely are blened with costumes, dresses & accessories from the fashion label and all linked via technologgy and the inevitable NFTs produced for sale too from £2,000 - £100,000 which can be puchased at the till with no need for Crypto Currency (which seems to miss the point of NFTs).

Much of the funds raised will got to supporting the Varseley Foundation & ensures Victor's collection is appropriately archived and protected for generations to come.

Opened 13th January 2022 - Closes 31st March 2022

title: The British Museum
review_text:
I had 45 mins to spare and as I was just around the corner, and had never been, I decided to pop in.

Vast, impressive buildings, a short queue to have bags checked by security. I wandered around the first floor and headed to Europe exhibits - very impressive.

They have regular exhibitions which are advertised well inside the building. There are at least two small cafes and one bigger restaurant , all looked good, I didn’t use them though. Plenty of toilets but again can’t comment on the cleanliness etc as didn’t use. The museum was very clean in all areas.

Plenty of staff in hand for questions.

It was midweek, a Tuesday , at 2pm and was very busy with school children and tourists but I didn’t feel crowded.

I hope to return again on a specific visit do the day.

title: The British Museum
review_text:
Absolutely wonderful place to visit.

We only had very limited time in London but this was somewhere we wanted to visit as we wanted to keep our costs low. The museum is free to enter but donations are welcomed.

Firstly, the place is enormous. Various exhibitions are available with literally hundreds of things to look at, probably even thousands.

It goes without saying that the place is very popular and often very busy so if you don't like crowds, do some research about your visit before you go.

We spent a lot of time looking at the Egyptian exhibitions as my wife is very interested in that particular part of history but we did also wander around the Greek, Roman and middle Eastern parts as well.

Both of us agreed that we would come back to visit but would definitely spend a whole day there as we felt rushed.

There are places to grab a drink and a snack inside the museum as well, which is very welcome.

It is worth noting that while backpacks are allowed, luggage is not. The shops nearby do offer a luggage storage solution.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
review_text:
This is one of the museums you can visit for free. It's quite large, so you can easily spend the whole day there. The exhibits are very impressive. The ceramics, silver, and jewelry sections are beautiful. There's a lovely café inside, perfect for a coffee or lunch break. The museum's gift shop offers a wide variety of options. It's very easy to get to. It's 5 minutes from South Kensington station. If you wish, you can enter the museum through the underground entrance.
Also, the Natural History Museum, the Science Museum and Imperial College London are right in front, and Harrods is a 10-minute walk away.

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
review_text:
We were in there for five hours and we’re still unable see all the rich cultural treasure the place has to offer.

The best cafe area I’ve seen in a long time and a lovely outside space for a break and some sunshine.

Recommend the diverse history from huge ancient Roman pillars to tiny exquisite jewellery, and ancient relics to modern-day fashion and interactive theatre.

Happy to get lost in there for the day.

title: Twist Museum
review_text: Twist Museum is a really fun place to visit. It’s great for kids, and there are lots of interesting illusions and interactive things to try. You can spend quite a long time in there exploring and taking pictures. Definitely a good place for a family day out.

title: the Design Museum
review_text: Not a very huge museum but still inspiring and thought provoking.  The exhibited items cover various design categories.  What impressed me most are the deep thinking behind, like the basic principles, purposes of design concepts, etc.  The "Platform " at the first floor regularly brings the master pieces of a designer to the visitors.  Sustainability is also a core concept of the museum, which is deeply planted in the selections and illustrations of the displayed items.  Visit of the regular displayed items is free but the topical exhibitions are worth paying.

title: London Transport Museum
review_text: Such a fascinating museum with so many interactive exhibits and videos, and also stamps to collect. I learnt so much at the museum and it makes you really appreciate the transport we have here in London and just how much went into building the underground and maintaining it today. Would highly recommend to anyone - both tourists and locals!

title: Hunterian Museum
address: The Royal College of Surgeons of England 38, 43 Lincoln's Inn Fields, London, WC2A 3PE
review_text:
A very positive experience, the staff are welcoming and helpful. I was happy to oblige them with a small donation. Afterall, they are getting paid! I just walked in on the day.
The museum itself is located in a beautiful building with other interesting landmarks nearby. And the contents are almost shocking! Well worth a visit to learn about the history if medicine, with too many examples of bones and animals to comprehend.
I won't post too many photos, lest I should spoil the experience.

title: The Cartoon Museum
review_text:
Small, quirky, welcoming museum that can be toured in 30 minutes. Restrooms, gift shop. I appreciated the historic British satirical cartoons with explanations of what element of life back then was being satirized.

You must go to the nearby Fitzrovia chapel nearby afterwards!

title: Bow Street Museum of Crime and Justice
address: 28 Bow St, Martlett Ct, London, WC2E 7AW
place_title: Bow Street Museum of Crime and Justice
review_text:
The Bow Street Museum of Crime and Justice is smaller than I imagined, with only a small part of the original police station and court still preserved or accessible, but it’s still worth a visit if you’re interested in London’s criminal history. I went with my family, including my 8yo nephew, and the kids’ investigation challenge really kept him engaged while we explored the displays.

There’s some fascinating information on the cases and people connected to Bow Street, and seeing the old cells up close gives a real sense of the building’s past. It’s not somewhere you’ll spend hours, but it’s a nice stop if you’re nearby, especially with the Blue Light Card discount, which makes it even better value

title: The Cinema Museum
address: 2 Dugard Way, Renfrew Rd, London, SE11 4TH
review_text: We had the most wonderful guided tour of The Cinema Museum in February (we were a group of 8 employees from the Historical Association office near by). The volunteer guides were really knowledgeable and friendly and the museum is full of the most fascinating items and interesting history. We met two of the museum's resident cats and ended the visit with a cinema reel of old adverts and footage, which was so fun to watch! We were also served coffee, tea and biscuits and the experience was both interesting and relaxing. Booking the tour was easy to do and I would recommend the experience to work teams and groups of people who are interested in learning about cinema, visiting micro-museums, exploring places of local heritage and looking for a fun cultural activity for an afternoon.

title: London Canal Museum
review_text: Nice museum.  Obviously not as extensive as the National Waterways museums in Gloucester and Ellesmere Port (do visit).  Lots of reading panels and artifacts. Combined with a pub crawl of the local area it's a good day out. Allow around two hours to have a good read of all the panels.  Toilets are upstairs and it's disabled friendly as they have a wheelchair type lift for upper floor.  Was originally an ice warehouse, part of which is in the middle of the museum.

title: Foundling Museum
review_text:
Highly recommend.  A very informative if not sad display of objects associated with The Foundling Hospital and resulting charties connected to it.
Plenty to see and the top floor has an excellent exhibition on Handle the composer. He was a governor and benefactor of the Foundling Hospital.
Attended a afternoon lecture there tge same day on music and symbolism in Durch 17th century art. There is a lift to each floor which is helpful. A small gift shop
Nearby are various places to eat within a 2 minute walk in the modernist Brunswick Shopping centre.

title: Islington Museum
address: Patrick Coman House, 245 St John St, London, EC1V 4NB
review_text: A small and informative museum, definitely worth a visit. It displays information on Islington's history and changing society; local radicals and dissenters; from fashion to leisure and prisons in the borough. Showcases facts, anecdotes and memorabilia from locals through the years. Staff member at reception helpful and welcoming. There is a permanent exhibition area and an area for temporary exhibitions. Note it closes for one hour at lunchtime (at time of writing 13:00-14:00).

title: Museum of Methodism
review_text:
The Museum of Methodism, located at Wesley's Chapel in London, offers a comprehensive insight into the history, impact, and evolution of Methodism, a Christian denomination founded by John Wesley in the 18th century. The museum is a significant cultural and religious site that preserves artifacts, documents, and exhibits related to Methodism’s influence on Britain and the world.

## Key Features of the Museum of Methodism

### **Location and Historical Significance**
- The museum is housed in the crypt of Wesley's Chapel, built by John Wesley in 1778.
- Wesley's Chapel is often referred to as the "Mother Church of World Methodism" and remains an active place of worship.
- Adjacent to the chapel is John Wesley's House, a well-preserved Georgian townhouse where Wesley lived for over a decade. This house provides insights into his modest lifestyle and the Georgian era.

### **Exhibits and Artifacts**
The museum features several permanent displays that narrate the story of Methodism:
- **"The Warmed Heart"**: Focuses on John Wesley's spiritual conversion, showcasing his Field Bible in a contemplative space.
- **"Mr Wesley’s New Chapel"**: Traces the history of Wesley's Chapel using maps dating back to 1746.
- **"Connecting the Connexion"**: Illustrates Wesley's organizational system for Methodist societies, including membership tickets and prints of Francis Asbury, who played a key role in Methodism’s separation from the Church of England.

Other notable artifacts include:
- John Wesley's death mask
- His wooden pulpit from The Foundery Chapel
- Original letters and archival documents
- Commemorative pottery and paintings

### **Interactive Features**
Visitors can access interactive displays via tablet computers, which provide access to John Wesley’s sermons, Charles Wesley’s hymns, burial records, and other historical documents. Audio-visual presentations further enhance understanding of Methodism’s development.

### **John Wesley's House**
This Georgian townhouse offers a glimpse into Wesley’s daily life. Key features include:
- His study chair
- An electrical machine he used for health experiments
- A prayer room considered the "Power House of Methodism"

### **Community Engagement**
Wesley’s Chapel continues to serve as a hub for worship and community activities. It hosts regular services, concerts, seminars, and educational workshops. The chapel grounds also include John Wesley’s tomb and Bunhill Fields cemetery nearby, which holds graves of notable nonconformist figures like William Blake and Daniel Defoe.

## Visitor Information
- **Admission**: Free (donations welcome)
- **Opening Hours**: Monday–Saturday (10 AM–3 PM). On Sundays, tours are available after morning worship.
- **Location**: 49 City Road, London (near Old Street Underground Station)

The Museum of Methodism is not only a place for those interested in religious history but also for visitors curious about its role in social reform, abolition movements, education, and temperance advocacy. It offers an enriching experience that connects faith with historical context.

title: Museum of Methodism
review_text:
This is a place I wanted to visit for some time.
I have been for a long time fascinated by the work of the Wesley brothers and George Whitfield and how Britain was so much transformed for the better by God through these men.
There is hope for a lost generation. God can do it again.

title: Arsenal Museum
review_text: My partner and I purchased this tour for my step daughter as part of her Christmas gift. We decided to take a few days off work and make a little city break of it with my stepson. This meant we booked travel and hotels in advance. The gift website advised to check the tour website for any planned interruptions of the tour which I did before booking anything else. We booked about 2 months prior to our visit. Checking all details 2 days before our trip only to see on the arsenal tour website to expect planned interruptions on the day of our visit up until 1pm. Got there on the day at 2.30pm and the interruptions were still in place. We couldn't visit the changing rooms, the dug out or go onto the pitch. I was absolutely gutted for my step daughter. The chap on the till was very apologetic but said our only option would be to visit on another day which wasn't possible. We did the tour in about 10minutes and to be honest it wasn't overly impressive, especially without being to see the spaces unavailable such as the changing rooms. When you purchase this tour as gift, purs was through virgin experience, you can NOT pre-book your visit so if youre local I assume you can check first thing on the day you plan to visit, it you need to book other arrangements around your tour good luck because something interrupts it you will get to see very little. Also as a little heads up, at the start of the tour they take your picture. Unbeknown to us, this picture is then placed against different backdrops for you to purchase at the end of your tour so either strike a good pose, or maybe just let the kids be in the picture so you can see them against the different backdrops. The Arsenal museum was worth a visit though.

title: Fashion and Textile Museum
review_text: Wonderful range of exhibitions. Situated in an interesting location - many good cafes, delis etc in the street, and an easy walk from London Bridge station and The Borough Market.check out the local history stories on the notice boards in the little park on Bermondsey Street, a short stroll up the road from the Museum.

title: Camera Museum
review_text:
Small quirky museum that you can tour in 20 minutes. Cafe inside, too.
It was fun seeing the historic cameras and the nostalgic ones from my childhood.
It has personal collection vibes.
Lots of Stairs.
Definitely go to the nearby stunner, Fitzrovia Chapel (Mon-Wed 11-5, and one Sunday a month).
Also Randolph Caldecott house is nearby (of picturebook award notoriety).

title: Alexander Fleming Museum
review_text:
Free museum displaying the original laboratory where the first trace of the  legendary medicine - penicillin - was discovered. The museum is largely ran by local volunteers, who will guide you through the space and share interesting stories about Alexander Fleming and how penicillin became a life-saving medicine from it's initial lab discovery. The museum is also an education centre to promote public awareness of the issue of anti-biotic resistance by explaining the principles of anti-biotic phenomena.

Be prepared to climb 3 floors from the ground floor, as the museum does not have a lift or wheel-chair access route.

title: Museum of Homelessness
address: The Manor House Lodge, Seven Sisters Rd, Finsbury Park, London, N4 2DE
review_text: My visit to the MoH was amazing.  I felt welcome as soon as I arrived and was treated like part of their big family.  I was offered a drink and chatted to.  The exhibition I attended was fun, practical and very educational.  The museum is packed with information and positivity.  The team were on hand to ask questions and I had a panic attack whilst there as I suffer from PTSD, I was cared for immediately and extremely well. What an incredible learning journey.  What an awesome and delightful group of people, with enormous hearts, committed to making the world a fairer and happier place for everyone, whoever you are.  Well worth a visit and please support their work by becoming a regular doner and or vounteer.  As I left, I was given a gift of lip balm made by the team at MoH, how kind of a homeless community to give a gift to everyone that visits them to learn about their work, where else do you get that?  What an inspirational place.  Thank you MoH for your life saving work, they are the only organisation that record the names of homeless people who die on the streets, they believe everyone has a value whoever you are.  If I could have bottled their enthusiasm and passion for their work and brought it home with me I would have.  Do yourself and your family or colleagues a favour, make a visit to MoH.  Thank you MoH for the work you do, please don't ever stop, we need more people like you in the world.

title: Museum of Homelessness
address: The Manor House Lodge, Seven Sisters Rd, Finsbury Park, London, N4 2DE
review_text: What a radical museum! I went to the show 'How To Survive the Apocalypse' at the weekend, and was blown away at the thoughtfulness and creativity across the board. Objects donated by people from the local homeless community were brought alive by storytellers, there were a couple of projected films, and a tour of the space and beautiful gardens. Everything felt very respectful and produced in line with what the community wanted represented, but it was also really joyful and funny at times. I would recommend it to anyone, as it is one of the most innovative museum experiences I've ever had.

title: Peek Freans Museum
review_text:
Appropriately housed off the labyrinthine passageways in the last remaining building of the former “Biscuit Town” precinct.
Less a museum than a focused obsession, commitment and labour of love, celebrating the product, process personnel, the history and ethos of a significant local employer.
An archives with a formidable collection of artefacts, and ephemera referencing different aspects of the defunct enterprise, presented in a refreshingly informal way. ( my personal favourite the meticulously logged “customer complaints” journal.)
A special shout-out to curator Frank - with over 30 years experience at Peek Frean - who invests his time, knowledge, expertise, and personal anecdotes as personal guide.
The unconventional and (perhaps) quirky methodology evident here largely makes for an informative, bespoke and memorable outing.
Recommended unreservedly.
Phone to secure a visiting appointment.

title: Lord's MCC Cricket Museum
address: Lord's Cricket Ground, St John's Wood Rd, London, NW8 8QN
review_text: I’m so glad we went to The "Monthly Interactive Music Session" at Lord's is a community music sessions with local partners like Mind , The singer Mr Maciek O’Shea,  fantastic And singing older, beautiful songs

title: The Gingerbread City by Museum of Architecture
address: Unit 5, Coal Drops Yard, Stable St, London N1C 4DQ
place_title: The Gingerbread City by Museum of Architecture
review_text:
An interesting place to visit for festive fun for the whole family. The displays making up the exhibitions were exquisitive and highly imaginative. Some of them were mind boggling   displaying wonderful skills and passion by the creators. There was something to draw the attention of the old and young alike. The polite staff were on hand to give guidance and support around the museum. The venue is easily accessible from any direction in London since it is served by the nearby Kings Cross station.
My only criticism is about the venue. I feel that it is too small and felt overcrowded when busy, which prevents one from freely enjoying the wonderful creations on display.

title: Crime Museum
review_text: Great place feel they could benefit from having a cafe half way round.Your in there 3-4 hours easy . When your a older person it’s a long time to be standing

title: Victoria & Albert Museum (Stop R)
review_text: I visited the Victoria and Albert Museum a relatively long time ago now in 2008. The Museum would be  interesting to anyone interested in design. There is a large collection of furniture and classic designs from designers and craftsman such as Thomas Chippendale. There is a large central courtyard that is a nice place to stop for a break as you make your way through the museum halls and exhibits.

title: Museum Superstore
review_text:
My experience in this store was amazing to say the least!

Great customer service, incredibly helpful and the prices are so so so reasonable. Clean and organised.

I was staying in a hotel a few minutes away but i would always come here, they’re open early and close late and have everything and if they dont have what you need on the spot, they can get it for you in minutes.

The best grocery/souvenir store in the area!

title: Museum Superstore
review_text: Great shop, just a short walk from the British Museum. The owner is a very nice guy, hospitable, kind and friendly person. Must visit if you are nearby.  He gave me a discount  on the things i bought.

title: Imperial War Museum (Stop K)
review_text: Has interesting stuff, tells slot about local history if you go to the right section

title: Science Museum Imax Cinema
address: Exhibition Rd, South Kensington, London, SW7 2DD
review_text:
The view is decent from all seats.. admittedly, they could be a bit more comfortable, especially for frequent 3+ hour long IMAX movies.
Anyway, it's great to have an IMAX screen in the neighborhood, I'm grateful for it. One more awesome thing about the Science Museum.

title: Astor Museum Hostel
review_text:
People are lovely, I stayed for 6 days. Everything was perfect, place is cleaned every day. The kitchen is well-equipped
It's also open 24h/7 which is perfect ! I arrived really early before the sun even rised and I still had a warm welcome. They also always do their best to suits you.

Safe place for queer and black, Latina/o people.

The only issues are;
The walls has echo so when someone snores or the bedroom above aren't calm it's harder to fall asleep but when you're used to hostel or are a heavy sleeper it's fine
The kitchen open at 8 and closes at 10, a bit too short and late  if you ask me but they have to clean everything between 10-11AM so it's understandable

Globally it's a really great place with amazing people and law prices

title: Tower of London
review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Tower of London 🏰👑✨

An absolute must-visit in London. Every time we come here, it feels like stepping straight into history. 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦

From the moment you walk through the gates, you can feel the weight of the past — kings, queens, prisoners, and stories that shaped Britain. The Crown Jewels are breathtaking 💎👑 — truly something you have to see in person.

The Yeoman Warder (Beefeater) tour was brilliant — entertaining, informative, and full of humour. Even the kids were listening! 😄

Walking along the ancient walls with views of Tower Bridge nearby makes the experience even more special. 🌉

History, drama, power, and beauty all in one place. A powerful reminder of the past and a fantastic day out for the whole family.

#TowerOfLondon #LondonHistory #CrownJewels #FamilyAdventure #LondonMustSee

title: Victoria and Albert Museum
review_text: Truly amazing mainly free museum and exhibitions that take you though the past for hundreds of years with huge amounts of antiquities right through to the modern day. Some every day items become accessible in ways you couldn't imagine.

title: the Design Museum Shop
review_text: The gift sop presents a great way of showing off how design/art can be incorporated into every day life. Many of the gadgets are are useful and all of them are fashionable. They also make great gifts for all ages, everything from colourful toys for kids to utility chop sticks for the unable at an affordable price. It also offers great quality print art. Furthermore the inventory is always changing, so you will discover something new every time you visit.

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: A really enjoyable visit and I love the items in the shop made from tube seat fabric. I do think the standard adult entry free is expensive - you can come back as many times as you like in the year, but that’s of little use if you’re not local. If you’re on benefits though, bring evidence with you - some entitle you to entry for just £1!

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text:
My son had been wanting to visit the London Transport Museum for a long time, and he was absolutely thrilled to finally be here. The highlight for him was getting his dream underground train from the gift shop, which made the day extra special. The museum is really engaging for kids, with plenty of interactive displays, and it’s also a must-visit for anyone who loves transportation and London’s history. A wonderful family outing!
Maybe parents might get bored but kids are not

title: Hackney Museum
review_text:
Hackney Museum is a small museum in the Hackney area of London focusing on local history of the area. Considering the history of Hackney, a lot of the exhibits focus on the influx of people over the centuries and decades. The story starts with the Roman period, but the story rapidly shifts to the last 200 hundred years with a particular focus on immigrants and by extension the poorer members of society.

There's also a small exhibition room which is currently dedicated to "black" music. Truly amazing to see some old school equipment. A real gem of a museum.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: Small but cute cafe. Huge seating area and wide range of food and drinks . Their coffee is outstanding if you compare it with the coffee shops around the area. It’s great to go with children as they also cater for them. Homemade cakes and different hot wrap of the day every week. They also serve fresh salads and toasted sandwiches. One of the few places I have seen they sale baby food. Staff is very friendly and always willing to help.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text:
Co-created with children from early years to teens, the venue is intended to be the “most joyful museum in the world”, offering young people a space to imagine, play and design. It features almost 2,000 toys, characters, objects and artworks on display from around the world and across history.

The museum offers three permanent galleries, Play, Imagine and Design, a temporary exhibition space, restored Grade II listed architectural features, improved retail and café spaces, new accessible routes and facilities, including a Changing Places Toilet, and an enhanced and expanded Clore Learning Centre and Creative Studios.

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
My Visit to The King’s Gallery, Buckingham Palace

I really enjoyed my visit to The King’s Gallery at Buckingham Palace. The gallery is beautifully designed and showcases a stunning collection from the Royal Collection — including paintings, sculptures, jewelry, and historic artifacts. The displays are well-curated and informative, giving a wonderful glimpse into royal history and fine art.

The staff were friendly and helpful, and the overall atmosphere was calm and elegant. It’s a great place to explore if you love art, culture, and history. The location next to Buckingham Palace makes it even more special, and the exhibitions are regularly updated, so there’s always something new to see.

Highly recommended for anyone visiting London — definitely worth a stop after seeing Buckingham Palace!

title: IWM Cafe
review_text:
Not a bad selection of goodies.
Cakes, biscuits, hot and cold drinks, and a few hot food offerings for both kids and adults. (We had tea, coke & 2x slices of cake 👍🏻😋)
Expensive - yes, but
1. It’s London
2. Entry was FREE, so come on….
Great afternoon out 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

title: IWM Cafe
review_text: Very pleasant space, lots of seating. Staff very friendly and efficient. Chips were nice, the Spitfire sausage roll was rank - texture extremely rubbery and I’m assuming very cheap meat- we’re taking it home for the dog. They had no mayonnaise when we asked, and we got given about 1/2 inch of ketchup with our chips. Would return and try some coffee and cake or pastries :)

title: IWM Cafe
review_text:
Paid nearly £30 for 2 bowls of chips, a cheese toastie with barely any cheese and no actual toast. A cheese and ham baguette that was really cold (like fresh from a fridge) and 2 okay enough teas.

Staff seemed like they didn't really know what they were doing if it was outside of their normal tasks. I asked for mayonnaise, as we requested it when we placed our order and they asked 'any ketchup or mayonnaise?', and the staff member looked as if I had him to build me a rocket. I then had to ask another staff member who went and got some but brought back a tiny bowl with a tiny dollop of mayonnaise.

title: IWM Cafe
review_text: Spitfire sausage roll was grotesque had an unknown brown/black meat inside. On top was seeds that tasted of liquorice which I like but no not on a sausage roll. The vegan sausage roll has pork in trust me I’m married to my wife I know what pork tastes like. Atmosphere was like a care home and I was charged £4 more than I should. ABSOLUTE TRAVESTY

title: IWM Cafe
review_text: The cafe is located on the lower ground to the right from the main entrance. The fare is limited but we'll cooked and presented by a helpful and friendly staff. I had a chicken burger and chips for £10.50, very slightly dry but nicely broiled.

title: IWM Cafe
review_text: The queue of self-catering recalled the time in the militry I think😂and the size of dish is very big. The coffee and food is normal but it is still a good place to enjoy more time beside the war.😉

title: IWM Cafe
review_text:
A nice place to stop and rest when you are browsing with your elderly father.

The menu was limited by the door itself was tasty and the service friendly and the coffee was amazing.

title: IWM Cafe
review_text:
A nice place to stop and chill inside the IWM. When you need to take a rest from all the gallery browsing. Good food and they sell alcohol as well as coffee and soft drinks. Free wifi and access to the park when the weather is good. Reopening 01.08.2020.

This is just a small update. The museum is now open, but because of the virus I'm afraid food is off the menu.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text:
I did afternoon tea.

We got two sets, so we had 2 of each sandwich and pastry, 4 scones, and 2 types of tea. This is 84 pounds for 4 people.

The sandwiches were good and the clotted cream and jam for the scones was good. The pastries were too sweet and I would have skipped them.

The service was a bit slow in my opinion.

I think this was alright, but probably better to go elsewhere for a sandwich and tea.

Because of the number of items that come, you should have an even number of people

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: I came across this dental surgery a few years ago, looking for a dentist who could look after me, as I am a very nervous person when it comes to dental work.  I needed advice and help as my teeth were in need of corrective protection.  I have been going there ever since, and Dr. Wahab Shakir, my dentist, not only gave me practical advice but, also, with his professional knowledge and kind emotional intelligence, has completely cured me of being a nervous wreck about dental work.  Today,  I had 4 composite resin fillings done without any problems and did not need an injection.  The nurses there and also the reception staff are highly trained and are all courteous and helpful.  I recommend this dental surgery highly and I would never go anywhere else!!

title: Waterlow Park
review_text:
Great park I got there Greenwich park vibe but strange enough I like it more then my local Greewnich park!

Lots of area for picnic, great design and variety of plants and trees.

It is a must to go if you decided to see Highgate Cemetery.

10/10 stars:):):)

title: Queen's Park
address: Queens Park Lodge, Harvist Rd, London, NW6 6SG
review_text: Amazing local park, with a lots of space for activities, mini zoo, coffee shop and sport areas as tennis courts and etc.. Very well maintained! One of my favourite parks in London! ✨

title: Queen's Park
address: Queens Park Lodge, Harvist Rd, London, NW6 6SG
review_text:
The toilets were closed at 3:30pn on Saturday, the 15th of November 2025. The Queens Park café area and also the toilets next to the children’s playground was closed and had the shutters down at 3:30pm . I use this park because they have toilets and when you have a medical condition it is important you have access to toilets that you have research that will be available.it is extremely concerning that the toilets are closed an hour before the park closes..
If the café is open till 430pm and they are serving food and drinks, the toilets attached to the café should be opened until the café closes.
The staff in the café stated it was nothing to do with them. They were very rude and said it would be the staff that wear green in the park that would be responsible. I couldn’t see anyone around at 3:30 pm that was visible. The park itself is lovely. I have been using this part for over 40 years. It is extremely concerning that the toilets are not opened and available when you need to use them.

title: Hendon Park
review_text: This is the park we walk through almost every day. So many memories have been made here.

title: Hendon Park
review_text: A beautiful and peaceful spot in North London! Great for walks, relaxing under the trees, or just enjoying nature. Well-kept and clean, with lovely green spaces and a safe, open vibe. Definitely one of my favourite local parks.

title: Avondale Park
review_text:
Lovely park in historic Notting dale. Great play areas for children also football pitch and tennis courts. Furthermore, if you enter the park directly from the Walmer rd entrance you will come across a community Market. It's situated directly in front of the kiosk, which has not been in use for some years, and has now open.  The community Market held at Avondale Park hapoens once per month the next one being 9th Dec 11am to 4pm. Available on the market stalls is a tasty Jamaican food pitch selling tasty authentic hot meals, as well as craft traders selling knitted & crochet items to name a few. I also stumbled across African “puff puffs“ which is a type of donut that you have served with various toppings. I also tasted some Eritrean food which is located next to the Jamaica food stall.
All very surprising as I wasn't aware this market was taking place.
I would say it's worth a visit.Lots to do in this well maintained park situated in the Royal Borough of Kensington & Chelsea.
Ps whilst at Avondale park, you can tour the locality hippodrome place, where horses used to kept and raced, the pottery kiln on Walmer rd which is listed, and directly opposite the park. This kiln was where potters from the local area who fire their wares to sell to the public.. The area of Notting dale was once home to  pigs too, hence a part of the area surrounding the park was called the "piggeries".

title: Avondale Park
review_text:
Great for all ages
Smaller playground for the little ones
Bigger playground for the older ones
Cafe not been open for a while.
Loads of seating around.
Also has tennis courts
Toilets could do with regular cleaning, not very clean and smell badly

title: South Park
review_text: Such a lovely local park, got everything we need, playground, tennis court, basketball court, etc. So many ppl are playing sports there in the Summer, feel so vivid. There’s a cafe at one of the corner, can get some rest indoor when it’s getting too cold in the Winter.

title: South Park
review_text: I live so close, and love to play football here. I’m so sad when they take it down in the summer for cricket. But Ik some people are going to have a good time doing that. Used to ALWAYS come to the playground when I was younger. God seeing a video of it gave me nostalgia. Injured myself once kinda badly but that was me being stupid. I’ve gone doing tennis with my dad and brother a few times. But football will always be the best part. There is also a basketball court and 2 playgrounds.

title: South Park
review_text: Good place quiet during this lockdown. Found just a handful of people for some regular workout.

title: South Park
review_text: It is a lovely medium-sized park with the amenities of a children's playground, exercise weights, and tennis courts bundled into it. I personally mainly avail myself of the walking path to get some good steps out in fresh air, and the tennis courts, which are hard courts, which is not my preference, but they have some good trees there for shade during the sunny season. I think it's a very nice park and it directly connects to the local community centre, which is also very nice.

title: Hurlingham Park
review_text: There are a few cherry blossoms at this timing, quite beautiful. The park got all the desired facilities, football pitch, tennis court, basketball court, etc. There are 2 playgrounds for children, a bigger one and a smaller one, on 2 sides of the park. Great place for the locals to enjoy the afternoon.

title: Hurlingham Park
review_text: The zipline is amazing. Attended an Easter camp of a local football club here. Kids enjoyed it. The cheery blossum was surprising too.

title: Millfields Park
address: Chatsworth Rd, Lower Clapton, London, E5 0AR
review_text: This nice, reasonably sized park on the Green Link walking route across London has the river at one end and local shops and street art at the other.

title: Marcus Garvey Park
review_text: Small neighborhood park and playground, very popular with locals. As part of a celebration for children, Boris Johnson visited in summer 2015 and spray-painted a pink pigeon on the wall.

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text:
Nice urban park with modern equipment and climbing frames. There's an on-site cafe serving hot drinks and cakes/pastries and ice-cream.
Security guards regularly patrol the park giving parents assurance of it's safety. There are emergency SOS buttons around the park, but not in service.

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: This place was great, superb and very convenient for our stay on Kilburn High Road, very secure with friendly staff and the prices are very reasonable for central London, we were always greeted with a friendly smile! It was perfect! We will definitely return! Highly recommended.

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text:
Rude rude rude,  they think you are unable to understand a word they are saying to you.  Pick up your jaw from the floor when you reply to them.
As for the car wash
It used to be very good. Not anymore  lazy. They  refused to go near my car when I took my sons assistant dog out, refused a refund.  All the same arrogant and lazy.

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: Parked in the evening. Came back and they were closed (like after 6:30pm or something like that). And they aren't just closed for cars coming in but closed for cars coming out too. So I had to pick it up the next day. I had to pay £26!!!

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: Can park your vehicle at an affordable rate.

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: They had three people asking me what I wanted, all I wanted was to park, they need to stop over staffing. I ended up parking on the street cos each one had something else to say. Um-ing and ah-ing about if there was space. Also it was cheaper for the 1 hour to park on the street outside.

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: Great value for £5 but be warned,spaces not guaranteed get there early and you could be in with a chance, as for the market upstairs you'll probably find a bargain but on the whole its pretty grim..

title: Kilburn Market Car Park
review_text: It is located in a good place outside the congestion area. It's not the cleanest car park, but it is worth the price.

title: Finsbury Park Basketball Courts
address: 94 Endymion Rd, Finsbury Park, London, N4 4LX
review_text:
The best outdoor court in London, located in a nice park and most hoops have nets!!
I moved out of the area, but I'm still coming back;)
The only downside is that the park is closed for vehicles and it can be difficult to find a parking space nearby.

title: Regency Car Park - Swiss Cottage
review_text: I use this car park on a regular basis. £10 for a whole day of parking in Central London is very rare. The car park attendant is also very friendly and is willing to even park my car for me! Can't go wrong with this business, fully recommended!

title: Emslie Horniman's Pleasance Park
review_text:
Lovely local children park with plenty of activities for them on a protective floor and fully enclosed part.
Taking 1 star off as the public toilets are filthy!

title: Guy Street Park
review_text: The park used to be a burial ground for the nearby Guy’s hospital. Guy Street Park has been developed thanks to a local friends group and volunteers with a multi-use games area for 5 a side football and multi-sports and a childrens' playground.

title: Sumner Road Park
review_text: Local park mainly used by residents living on the perimeter

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text: Like many others I wish I read the reviews for an NCP car park! My car was broken into yesterday and nothing valuable to them taken but my lashes kit was stolen, a few hundred pounds at least to replace plus the window not to mention that I can't provide this service until it is replaced now so loss of business! The bag is small and black, hidden in the footwell and the car has black tints. I've always checked it before as the boot is full of my other equipment and it's not easy to see from outside so they definetly use lights and seem to be very quick as multiple cars broken into! I had no reception to call anyone except near the lifts and told a couple and they ended up moving their car. At that time I saw the patrol guy go past and told him and he said yes I patrol the floors every 30-60mins and this happened an hour or so before I got to my car and they broke into 3-4 other cars too. He had already reported it to the police and gave me the reference number. Whilst I was driving to the exit I told another couple and they said yes they know so they always leave their back windows open as it's happened to them there before. The man also explained that a  group blocks open the fire exits and comes via that way. From the reviews it looks like NCP know that this is a regular occurrence and have only put up "high theft area" posters near the lift and hired one patrol man for the whole car park. Until they invest in proper security such as cctv or patrols on every single floor I would AVOID this car park. NCP clearly doesn't care about customer satisfaction and can't be bothered to invest money to make things safer!!

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
Terrible company. Euro Car Parks are clearly only interested in making as much money as possible, not taking into account human error or fairness. Their systems are rigid and designed to profit from mistakes rather than allow people to rectify them.
