If this company wants to aggressively go after people for a human error, then they should ensure they don’t make errors themselves. Their outdated signage in the car park proves they think they can get away with mistakes - but the moment we do, they’ll slam us with a £60 fine.

Unfortunately for them, that mistake was their downfall, resulting in them having to withdraw action against me and back down completely. I’ll never be using or recommending this company again.

title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
place_title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
review_text: My car got broken into robbed all the luggage that was in the boot and stuff that was underneath the passenger seat... items worth a good few thousands. Looks like nothing is being done in this place they been robbing it for long time proper organized gang. Police literally not doing anything?! How come ?! It is a complete joke but this is not gonna end like that.. NCP you better do something about it I'm  going to expose this everywhere..

title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
place_title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
review_text: Car broken into after a matter of hours. Nobody was there to help, and I chose this car park for its so called ‘security’. I then had to pay £65 to get the car out of the car park. All I can say is to anybody… DONT PARK HERE. Your car is safer parked on the road. This car park is a regular target.

title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
place_title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
review_text: Beware do not park at this car park. I’m sure they have break in every night. The cameras do not work. It’s has been happening for a long while. We parked overnight thinking it’s a secure car park. It’s gated and you can only enter with putting your car registration in. I spoke with a contractor the morning after the break in and he mentioned it happens regularly.  They do not even respond to my emails about the incident.  They smashed the boot window and took some items of clothing. Nothing was on display and the windows are tinted and there was a parcel shelf covering the items. I do think it’s an inside job!

title: Park West Reception
address: 4 Park W Pl, Edgware Rd, Tyburnia, London, W2 2QJ
review_text:
Didn't want to even give one star. Booked this through booking.com. Turns out this is a residential flat in a block ,NO sub letting through any holiday app is allowed. There are signs up in all the differen blocks .We had abuse from residents whilst waiting for the key. Initial contact was at 3pm ,went to put our stuff in the apartment as we'd come to London on an emergency ( my son is in intensive care) and booked last minute.  Was told we could keep our belongings there but couldn't have a key till later as they only had 1 key and were waiting for cleaners. Against our better judgement, due to a highly emotional situation, we agreed and went back to the hospital.  So to cut a long story short we called at 7 pm ,nobody would answer the phone, got onto booking.com who were as helpful as a chocolate teapot!!! Luckily for us the security let us stay with them for our own safety!
Finally after contacting a few numbers that kept being sent to us from booking.com and the answer phone of the initial number we were given,a woman let us in at around 10pm we didn't stay as we felt very unsafe. At this moment we are still waiting for our money back, which booking.com said they would pay the difference if we could find another place to stay. At 11 pm we managed to find a lovely hotel nearby. The Met police have also been involved as are Westminster Council.
Apparently this is a scam and is happening quite often .
DO NOT BOOK any apartments in PARK WEST,  as you have no comeback if they take your money!!!

title: BAYSIXTY6 Skate Park
review_text: Waiting in the queue, I got queue jumped by I guess a regular, staff didn't bat an eye after I told him to wait his turn. Then yelled at by a coach on another occasion in the beginner session after a collision. This happened in the *beginner session* - I'm still finding my feet myself and didn't feel very welcomed. Otherwise the skatepark is nice for what it is, and being covered for when it rains. Would appreciate a better progression for drop ins seeing as they orient this park around lessons.

title: Nora Cafe
review_text:
I stopped by this lovely cafe for a quick Sunday brunch recently. It’s a cosy space — a little small but very comfortable — and when I got there around noon, it was bustling with people (seems to be popular with the locals). Fortunately I was able to snag a table that had just opened up, as I was dining by myself that day.

I ordered the eggs benedict with bacon for my meal, and it was amazing! Bacon was juicy and crispy without being too dry or oily (and came in a healthy portion), and the eggs were perfectly cooked to a nice runny-ness. The side salad was a nice, lighter touch that complemented the heavier/creamier hollandaise sauce and bacon. Portion size was sooo large, too — I could barely finish.

I also had a hot chocolate, which was yummy. It had a nice depth to it and warmed me up, without the chocolate being too heavy or artificial/sickly sweet.

It was a nice surprise that they didn’t add the ubiquitous 12.5% service charge :)

Service was so warm and friendly, and I had a wonderful experience throughout. I’d love to come back to try their classic English breakfast (seems to be one of their specialities).

title: Nora Cafe
review_text: Very cosy little cafe that serves alot of different types of breakfast. I got the full English breakfast and it was delicious! The portions are generous as well and I was full for so long after. They dont add in the optional service charges so what you see in the menu price is what youll pay. This i really appreciated. There were alot of locals there as well.

title: Nora Cafe
review_text:
I went for a weekend breakfast with my family during my stay in London after seeing their rating on Google. Their full English breakfast isn't trying to win any awards, and it's quietly average. Instead, it's a reliable, comforting and satisfying plate of food that does exactly what it sets out to do: fill you up and set you right for the day.
My personal favourite was the Mediterranean omelette, which was worth trying for its fresh and filling ingredients.
The ambiance was nicely done, though the space was very congested. Service could be better.

title: Nora Cafe
review_text:
After seeing Nora Cafe on Google maps, I thought I'd give it a try, and i wasn't disappointed.
I had the full English and it was really yummy. A great sized portion, very tasty, and a good price too. The cafe was cosy , and the service was very good.
The toilet was lovely and clean.

title: Nora Cafe
review_text: Woke up hungry at 6am on a Saturday, and not in the mood for hotel breakfast again. Found Nora Cafe just 2 mins walk, only place open. Pleasant surprise! Had the Haloumi and sucuk (beef sausage). So good. Staff were super friendly too.

title: Nora Cafe
review_text: Lovely little spot tucked away behind the main street. Delicious food, lovely service, and the music/vibes were so calm. I had the Mediterranean meal and it was perfect. The price is also amazing for the amount of food (and being London).

title: Nora Cafe
review_text: Had a lovely breakfast in here, Spanish Potato Hash, a nice find in the city and good value. Very nice atmosphere and great service. Highly recommended

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: The food was so good! The staff are so lovely ! There were some halal options, you just need to ask the staff. We had the Mediterranean breakfast and the morning turkey breakfast. Love that you have options of sweet and savoury breakfast! And it comes with a good variety. We just walked around and found this cute little place close to Islington for lunch. The food is also decently price - cheap for a central London brunch spot and it actually tastes good and fills you up. Found our new fav little hideaway cafe ! They have the cutest decor around!

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text:
Absolutely amazing! The breakfast was outstanding – every little detail was carefully thought out. The quality, flavor, and presentation were simply perfect, and the prices are unbeatable.
I’ve traveled a lot around the world, but I’ve never had such a delicious and lovingly prepared breakfast for this kind of price.
A special shout-out to the waitress, who was incredibly kind, attentive, and genuinely sweet – especially with children. She treats them with such warmth and even gives them a lovely little surprise that makes the experience even more special.
I can highly recommend this place – it’s the perfect way to start your day!

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: This place  honestly saved our mornings while we were in London. Great breakfast spot with lots of options. The food is really tasty, the staff are super nice, and the place is clean and welcoming. Would definitely recommend.

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: As we arrived in London this was our first breakfast. Since, it's been our favorite while here in town. The food has been consistently excellent, delicious, quick and gorgeous presentation and garnish. The atmosphere is so cute and the staff ŵarm and welcoming. I enjoyed the vegan breakfast first day, the veggie breakfast the next; both were delicious and similar to full english breakfast. We also used barbican café as our morning latte/cappuccino fix and they are artists with the coffee!

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: We were around the Angel/Islington area and were looking around for a good English breakfast. Had the Morning Turkey breakfast alongside the Mediterranean breakfast and loved them! Fresh fruits, pancakes and a great selection of savoury items too. Big menu so theres plenty of things to choose from. Highly recommend if youre in the area!

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: Amazing breakfast spot with professional and super nice service. The atmosphere here makes you want to sit down and relax immediately. And the food is top tier! So much variety in the menu makes it a great spot to come back to and the taste speaks for itself. Highly recommend :)

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text: The food is so good!! I ordered the full English breakfast but I overestimate myself😂😂. I couldn't finish them all. But it tastes so good so I took away the rest of them for my dinner. Really delicious! Highly recommended🥰 Maybe next time, I will order the normal Set breakfast

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text: Halil is a constant friendly face we’re always happy to see — welcoming, attentiveand genuinely lovely every time. The food is consistently delicious; you honestly can’t go wrong, but I especially recommend the mixed kebab or any of the breakfasts. We’ve been coming here for years and it’s become a real family favourite. We’ll definitely continue coming back for many years to come.

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text:
Food was so good. I ate here two days in a row. Sunday brunch at noon and I ordered the fish and chips which were out of this world. The reviews online were very good, but the portion was huge, crispy, and better than I imagined. It was great!

This morning, I had a mushroom And cheese omelette, along with my typical vanilla oat latte. Yum!

Service is amazing. I’d give it 10 stars if I could.

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text: My friend suggested coming here for breakfast/ brunch and I can see why! The food is amazing, so filling and tasty! Pair it with the fresh orange juice and you won't be disappointed! The staff are so so so friendly and the atmosphere is inviting! Definitely will be coming back!

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text: What a find - great little caf, so much on the menu covering every option... whether all day breaky or a decent lunch/dinner option. Tasty chicken escalope - very generous portions... this is what I appreciate as London food - a working person's option.

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text: Cosy, simple café with a really authentic, family vibe. Small dining area, but it just feels nice and welcoming. Halil, the host, was a fantastic guy—chatty, friendly, and cracking jokes while serving. Had the mixed döner platter; massive portion and super fresh and tasty.

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: Popped in during the Fujikina Photography Festival & was really happy I did! Very warm & welcoming staff/atmosphere, great food and drinks. I really appreciated the initiative that this café represents - learning difficulties don't mean unemployable or uncapable.

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text:
We sat in this small cafe for a coffee. Unfortunately they only serve in takeaway cups even if sitting in (which is not enviro friendly nor homely). The coffee was quite nice however and the service fast.

Don’t get me started about the toilets. Since I’ve started about the toilets, I’ll finish. Tight as anything in there. Never been in such a tight cubicle. There is about 10 inches from the edge of the toilet seat to the door. Barely any space to drop your drawers. Manoeuvrability is non-existent. However, they were functional and clean.

Overall, good. They also employ folk who otherwise may struggle for work, which is important.

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: absolutely gorgeous coffee shop! the staff was so friendly and kind, when i first walked in i had no idea of their initiative, but i was pleasantly surprised to have supported such an important cause. I got a mocha and a cinnamon bun, both absolutely delicious. Overall great service, very cozy and warm ambience and great food!

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: Great quiet location. Comfortable seating but can be limited during peak hours. Friendly staffs and cafe comes with a good cause, from their branding. Coffees are pretty good. Recommend to come here for a break in between your daily activities.

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: The coffee is so so good and such a great place for a quick break. The staff is incredible, can’t rate it enough. I usually dislike the coffee in London because of its bitter taste, but here it’s fab!

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: Absolutely no word of a lie fantastic. Great service. Great coffee. Really great food! We had the BLT and cheese and leek toastie.

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text: Incredibly well brewed coffee from Peruvian beans that add extraordinary pallet of flavor. Highly recommend to indulge in the locally baked donuts that accompany the taste of coffee to its fullest. No need to add that the service is top-notch. Overall greatly recommended

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text:
This new place is great, and sells great donuts and coffee! I saw it while I was in a rush, but I am so happy I went out of my way to try it the next day when I had time. Pierrot is a really friendly owner, who seems to care and know a lot about the products he sells!

The space itself is really charming, tucked into a nook at the entrance of Maida Vale Underground station, which itself has lovely old-fashioned mosaic decor.

The donuts were nice, fresh, and chewy. They tasted delicious, and were not overly sweet like many store-bought donuts. I tried the coffee (I got an espresso macchiato, so I really tasted it) and it was great as well! It was brewed really well, and the bitterness paired with the donut to make it even better!

I get really excited about charming, personable small/independent local businesses opening up, and I am really chuffed that this place has established itself in Maida Vale! I highly recommend going here if you fancy a donut, or want a coffee on your way to the tube!

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text:
Horrid donuts. 4.50£ for a dry, barely-filled donut. Not sure about the other reviews, but the chocolate hazelnut donut was the worst purchase I made in London. Other bakeries nearby bake with much more love. Never again.

To reply to the owners comment:
This was my second big bite, I did not find this mysterious chocolate you speak of. And love can be easily measured in the care of the texture and filling. This tasted like any generic supermarket donut, nothing special about it. A half spoon of Nutella does not a loving donut make.

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Tucked away in a quiet corner of the neighborhood, Small Square Coffee offers a warm and inviting spot—perfect for a cool British summer morning. The croissant sandwich paired with a smooth, sweet pistachio coffee sets a refreshing tone for the early morning. With its cozy yet stylish atmosphere, it’s a lovely place to enjoy a peaceful moment.

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text:
Highly recommended!
The staff were incredibly warm and attentive, and every single dish was absolutely exquisite — bursting with flavor and prepared with outstanding care. An unforgettable dining experience that left me genuinely impressed. I can’t wait to come back!

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: What a brilliant find, we stumbled across this cafe whilst looking for something to eat and we weren't disappointed. Very friendly owner and excellent food. The plate was massive and very good value for money especially for London! It all tasted lovely and fresh and it was a very pleasant dinner. Definitely recommend 👍🏻

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: i had a very good experience at Cali Cafe . Staffs are very nice and friendly. 😘

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: Very affordable, delicious and generous English breakfast! I didn’t even pay 10£ for it + cappuccino. Also very friendly and nice to sit outside. 😊 10/10 can recommend!

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: I'm typing this as I sit in this excellent cafe. Very warm friendly atmosphere and brilliant service. The staff are friendly and so is the owner. Try the toasted croissant with a cappuccino ☕ They also serve a full English & other hot meals. If you're in this part of town totally check out Cali Cafe 😃

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: Absolutely lovely full English breakfast! Could not fault it at all. Very nice staff also! Highly recommended 👍🏽

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: I’ve been coming to Billy’s Cafe for over 6 years, and it truly is the best cafe ever! The atmosphere is always warm and welcoming, and the staff make you feel like family every time you walk in. The food is always fresh, delicious, and beautifully prepared. Whether I’m stopping in for breakfast or lunch, the quality and service never disappoint. It’s rare to find a place that maintains such high standards year after year. Billy’s Cafe absolutely deserves 5 stars — highly recommend!

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: classic and warm atmosphere, hit the spot for a quick and delicious fry up

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: Great little hidden gem off the Hackney Road. I always get a Turkish breakfast when I am in the area. Top notch!

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: Billy’s Cafe is the absolute best place to go in E2. I started coming here ten years ago and would mostly have the breakfast options and always coffee! Then as the years have rolled on had more of the lunch options as well when working from home. It is such a well run, family and fun community. You cannot bee order anything that isn’t super tasty and really good value for money and freshly prepared. Billy’s cafe family are the best such wonderful and warm people and the best food and laughs ❤️

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: Don‘t get me wrong, am not giving 5 stars because this an exceptional dining experience but because everything is authentic and fully met or exceeded expectations - solid food, rustic atmosphere, friendly staff with a sense for humour and accepting german accent and limited language skills with a smile. A bit hidden but worth to take a few steps to get there. Kudos.

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text:
Banger of a full English in a local cafe. This is a no-frills place that does breakfast right. The guys were quick on service, with good chat, and brought out good food.

Good toast with egg, tasty beans and mushrooms, and nice slices of bacon. The portion size was perfect for a hungry guy and enough to get me going for the rest of the day.

Great prices, too, which will make me come around more often, too.

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: Billy's Cafe is an amazing place to go. I have been coming here years, and the food has stayed consistently amazing all the time, the inside is warm and relaxing with comfortable seating, good for early morning breakfast or brunch with the family. I highly reccomend them!!

title: Billy's Cafe
review_text: If you are looking to have some lovely food in a friendly authentic atmosphere,  then Billy's is your place! Always genuine, always helpful and polite, all staff members will make you feel special. Thank you everyone!

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text:
Love the venue - many things on their menu are amazing (salads/sandwiches/omelettes).

On this occasion, ‘Pancake Heaven’ (£12) was ordered. Sad to say that it was not heavenly at all. The assortment of fruits on the plate were cute and added to the overall presentation - but the promised ‘fluffy pancakes’ seemed to be no different than the small scotch pancakes you can grab at your local Tesco. Their texture was not appetizing.

All in our group who had pancakes with our individual meals were left disappointed - and the pancakes remained mainly untouched.

The Turkish Eggs tho, unmatched. Deffo recommend.

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text: Very tasty breakfast options. We had a traditional full English and a Turkish breakfast. Both were very good. Good quality ingredients. Service was quick and friendly and food arrived to the table in just a few minutes.

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text: Amazing breakfast, they have a rich menu with so many options and the vegan breakfast was so good as well alongside with the regular one and egg benedict. The coffee is good, staff is nice. The portion is huge and they also have a nice turkish tea.

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text:
Excellent in every way!!
Great coffee, great food and prices too good for the portions and the quality of the dishes!!!
Unfortunately we didn’t found it earlier , only our last day in London.
Best place to try English breakfast , definitely recommend it ❤️

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text: Everything from a full breakfast to cake and coffee. Great service. Very clean but when busy quite tight between tables. We would definitely return if in the area.

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text: I came here recently to meet a friend who works in the area. Oh, my gosh, the Penne Arrabiata was gorgeous and so much better than I have had elsewhere in London! Fresh cherry tomatoes, delicious, rich tomato sauce, olives, herbs, chilli. Definitely made with love and all for the princely sum of £9.50. Unbelievably good value. My friend's omelette was enormous and came with chips and salad. We both had lemon, ginger and honey tea and it came in generously sized cups! I will definitely be back for the Penne when I'm in the area. Very efficient and friendly service too. A real gem!

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text: Perfect little gem, conveniently situated, friendly and efficient staff, beautifully presented tasty food.  Vegan options, milk alternatives.  The staff happily adaptated my meal choice to suit my dietary requirements.  Thank you 😁

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: Cute little cafe by the park entrance, good outside space when it’s sunny. The breakfast is fine but hollandaise too runny for my taste (not sure if it’s made on the premises). Wrong item was served but quickly remade so all was good.

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: Popped into this lovely little cafe in the park on the recommendation of Michael’s the Cobblers. Ordered a tea, flat white and a Maritozza cake. All delicious. And total cost under £10 so a lovely surprise for London. Staff super friendly and cosy Christmas atmosphere. But too early for a mulled wine but nice touch.

title: Parco Cafe
review_text:
A hidden gem.
Called for breakfast, instead of eating at the hotel, and we were glad we did.
Full English breakfast for me and granola with Greek yoghurt for the wife.
Great coffee to wash it down in a lovely park setting with great service.
Spot on.

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: This is our absolute favourite local cafe, it’s run by a lovely family who are super lovely. Food is consistently delicious, we normally get the full English and extra fries, it’s always well portioned, fresh and tasty. Their tea bags are the posh Birchall ones, none of the cheap stuff. Great seating both front and back patio, especially when it’s sunny.

title: Parco Cafe
review_text:
Food always so nice, fresh and tasty. The recommended lentil soup was DELICIOUS!
The pastries are also devine. Service with a smile. The customer service was warm and friendly. Looking forward to my carrot cake next time folks 😁

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: A little secluded cafe on the edge of a quiet park. Veggie breakfast was tasty, and fairly priced, and you can’t beat a freshly squeezed orange juice. Staff were polite and friendly - great place to start the day!

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: Amazing location. Quick service, good food, several healthy options available and fresh orange juice. Friendly.

title: Becks Cafe
review_text:
I’ve been coming to this restaurant every Tuesday during my university break since 2022, and it has become one of my favorite places. The food is excellent, with very reasonable prices and generous portions. Every plate looks like a main dish, and everything is truly delicious.
The waiters are always polite, professional, and have a great sense of humor. They respond kindly to our jokes and make the atmosphere very welcoming.
I will miss this place a lot when I finish university. All the staff are wonderful, and they really make the experience special.

title: Becks Cafe
review_text:
We have now been to beck’s 3 times and have been impressed on every occasion.
We have a family member at great ormond hospital, so are having to make lots of visits to this area at the moment and are not local.
The staff are so friendly and kind, on pretty heavy emotional days the atmosphere, good food, good music and kind staff really cheer us up.
Would highly recommend!!

title: Becks Cafe
review_text:
Went for breakfast after having heard someone saying how good it was here.
The food was average, the cappuccino was huge but quite bland, and overall I wasn’t impressed. Prices are on the affordable side, though, and there is outside seating where you can smoke if that’s something you care about.

Note the hygiene rating of this place is 3/5, which doesn’t surprise me. In any case, I will definitely not go back any time soon.

title: Becks Cafe
review_text: The food was absolutely amazing we had previously had breakfast here at our last stay at London and as frequent British travellers this is the best breakfast we have ever had our hotel was not far away from here so it was easy to get back and too. I want to specially mention a young man named fatin (apologies if I’ve spelt your name wrong) he was so lovely and polite and when a friend had ordered food it came with beans that we didn’t order. Fatin our server was very apologetic about this and it wasn’t something you normally see so great food service and we are definitely coming back our next stay :)

title: Becks Cafe
review_text:
I  dined here and was genuinely impressed by both the food and the service. Every dish was prepared with clear attention to detail beautifully presented, perfectly seasoned, and absolutely delicious. You can tell the kitchen takes pride in what they do.

The service was equally outstanding. The staff were warm, attentive, and knowledgeable without ever feeling intrusive. They anticipated what we needed before we even asked and made the entire experience feel effortless and welcoming.

From start to finish, it was a memorable meal. Excellent food, exceptional service, and an atmosphere that makes you want to return. Highly recommended

title: Becks Cafe
review_text: I ordered the Rib eye steak, mash and mushrooms and all I can say is after one bite of the steak that I spat out was honestly one of the most disgusting and disappointing foods I’ve ever tasted. I sound harsh but it’s the honest truth. The whole meal especially the steak just had no flavour, not seasoned properly and I just can’t eat this. Very disappointing as I’ve spent £35 on this and couldn’t even get a refund. Honestly don’t waist your money on unseasoned, lack of flavour and overall disgusting food!

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text: Had such a cozy and pleasant time here I had to leave a review. For London prices and such a fresh and buttery sweet croissant, £1.80 eat in is unheard of. I got my cortado super fast and the empanada was so delicious - they asked if I wanted spicy sauce and it was the cherry on top. Sat outside on a sunny spring day and was so delighted to see how many friendly faces came in. The staff are just incredible. They are so warm and welcoming to everyone, always share a small and exchange plenty of conversations with so many regulars. Really haven't felt the community in a coffee shop for a while. I will definitely be back!! Muchísimas gracias ♡

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text:
A Hidden Gem for Comfort Food Lovers

I stopped by this charming little café with my son on a Saturday, and we were in for a real treat. While they usually serve a solid selection of paninis, cornbread, sandwiches, and great coffee during the week, Saturdays are extra special. That’s when they offer a Colombian homemade special meal — we had the most amazing soup, served with a warm cornbread on the side. You could taste the love in every bite.
It’s a family-owned and run spot, and that warmth shines through in both the food and the atmosphere. If you're looking for a cozy, welcoming place with real comfort food, this is the place to go.

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text:
Inexpensive friendly café with freshly prepared food. Lovely coffee, soup of the day was delicious, and the empanadas, too.
Recommended if you are in the area.
Many thanks.

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text: The Empanadas my friend...the Empanadas. I honestly cannot recommend them enough. Combined with the amazingly friendly staff and delicious smoothies you MUST go here.

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text: I loved this place, it has such a homey community vibe and the food was amazing 🥹💕

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text: The empanadas were absolutely delicious! This little cafe is cute and the staff is amazing, coffee was great the egg&bacon sandwitch was huge and full of flavour. Absolutely recommended! ❤️

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: Hidden Gem! Jamie and her team are so welcoming serving up amazing coffee, fab food and great music!

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: Located in a nice quiet area. The cafe is decorated quite cute and is regularly kept clean. Reasonably priced food.

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: CALLING ALL HACKNEY LAPTOP COFFEE SHOP TECH BROS — put down your overpriced laté and go check this place out!! It’s super friendly, has a view of the park, great coffee, food, internet, record player, and it’s warm and comfortable. And you’re funding a great local cause. Totally underrated!

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: Welcoming staff and a very warm environment with a brill view of the park :) ! Absolutely love your ethos too :)

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: Brilliant service and friendly staff. Had a lovely toasty and it was reasonably priced. The Victoria sponge was to die for!! They weren't shy with the buttercream and Jam. Can't wait to go back.

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: The staff were attentive, friendly & welcoming. We were having a meeting with 9 people and the staff adapted very well with us as a group with chairs & tables pushed together. We will use this cafe again.

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text: Superb, warm service and finally cafe that serves a flavoursome side salad!! The refreshing air-con also didn't go a miss on these very hot days. Loving the work of Tower Project 📍

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Simply the best. Quality food, great prices. Staff are all super hard working and friendly. A landmark for any Peckham local.

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Lovely little no-frills kinda cafe. Friendly staff and great food plus great prices! My bacon and egg sandwich had the softest freshest bread. Delightful! Definitely recommend 👌

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text:
19/02/2026

I had a Fabulous time at
this cafe

there's so
much to choose

from on the menu
board

I couldn't make
my mind up but

I chose a spaghetti
carbonara with

grated cheese on
top with a black coffee 💯😋

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Lovely and helpful staff, and great options for a classic English breakfast so you’ll definitely end up with a massive plate of all your favourite trimmings! We got to sit outside with our dog which was great and the general atmosphere was really warm and had a nice community vibe. Great value for money too.

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Really wanted to try out a different cafe instead of our usual one. So glad we did! I ordered on deliveroo Chicken Escalope sandwich and was hoping for the best! In the photo it only shows one half of the sandwich. So glad I did as it was fresh and delicious!! Will be ordering from Crossroads again for sure. My husband ordered a Sausage roll with 3 hash browns which he enjoyed. Cravings satisfied.

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Friendly staff, popped in for a breakfast and a tea, wasn’t disappointed. Recommend to get your morning going.

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text:
This place is great! I have been a regular customer for over a year now. And Crossroads never disappointed.

Whether it was a regular week day and you want to start the day off with a strong breakfast, or you are hungover on the weekend and need the perfect Hangover-cure, Crossroads has you covered. On top of that the staff is incredibly friendly and the price is too.

Couldn’t recommend this place more.

title: Crossroads Cafe
review_text: Great value, good atmosphere, protected seating out the front. Generous omelette, beans and salad. Worth the 5 min walk from Peckham Rye station.

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: Great breakfast at Mama’s Cafe! The full English was exactly what we needed, the prices are affordable. Good portion depending on what you order. Hulia was such a lovely lady, very kind and professional. We’ll definitely be back!

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text:
Early Tuesday morning, stumbling down Waterloo Road and seeing this charming cafe.

Walked on in and the owner greeted me and let me look at the menu which admittedly I spent way too long doing but it happens when one is not caffeinated.

Ordered a cappuccino and ham and cheese toasitie, spoke with the owner while all this was getting made and his a great conversationalist.

When the toastie was prepped and cappuccino made, I was delighted at how homely both were and delicious.

The bread was toasted to perfection, lots of cheese and ham but not too salty. Cappuccino definitely gor bouncing off the walls at work.

The place is pristine and cozy. Check out photos and videos. Highly recommend this cafe.

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text:
Perfect if you want a bit of variety. Fully English and veggie version went down very well - don't be deceived by the description of 'small'.

Then there were pancakes, and Turkish and a Sri Lankan curry breakfast (magnificent).

Coffee was huge and magnificent- no airs and graces.

Very good value and the style of a traditional English breakfast cafe. Probably 16 covers or so.

A proper gem.

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: Friendly, small traditional (no fancy espresso - instant coffee and tea served with the bag proudly floating in the mug) cafe, you’d walk by without noticing.  But strong positives are: super friendly service from “mama” herself, spotlessly clean, generous portions at pre COVID prices, and interesting Turkish-Sri Lankan fusion dishes together with British breakfast fry up staples.  Enjoyed the Turkish sausage (chorizo style) spicy sausage toastie with chips and salad.  What makes South London real.

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: Lovely little shop with a kind couple running the place. Food is excellent quality. Had the Turkish sausage toastie  and have no regrets. Thank you!

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: This is the place in Waterloo for an affordable Traditional British Breakfast! The food wonderful and perfectly prepared.Service is delightful as you  are served at your table by the one who prepared your food. We were searching for a quaint place to spend our last morning in London and we found a jewel of the British crown. Thanks to them for a perfect Breakfast on our last beautiful London morning. If you are in the Waterloo area give Mama’s Cafe a try! They also serve a wide variety of food with something for everyone with even some all day savory favorites!😀❤️🇬🇧

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: hands down one of the best breakfasts I’ve ever had. Every bite was perfect 🤩 Highly, highly recommend! Everythjng was perfect

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text:
Lovely cake/coffee shop that is sort of hidden because you have to walk through a shop to get to it, and I would assume most people wouldn’t necessarily notice it. There is a sign in front of the shop but when it’s busy you could miss it.

I really liked the place, it seemed less chaotic than the other coffee shops nearby, the coffee was good and the blueberry muffin so pretty I almost didn’t want to eat it.
Spent some time reading and enjoying the treats before going back to the flower market outside.

They also have a restroom but you need to ask the staff and they’ll give you the key.

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Stopped in after I saw online that this café served cream tea. :) Tucked away in the back of a vintage ceramics and antique shop, this café hosts a unique style, excellent baked goods, and delicious tea. The floorplan hosts a number of large tables with free seating, so you might end up conversing with the stranger next to you. A lovely place to pause at during the day.

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Such a cozy spot. I tried the lemon drizzle cake and an earl grey. The cake had that sort of home feeling that I really enjoyed. The staff were friendly and attentive. 10/10 would recommend for a good chat or a date with a good book!

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Felt like stepping into a tea room up north - with the warm, friendly service to match. So happy to have found this little gem in London. Enjoyed a lovely cream tea and a relaxing break.

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Some of the best homemade cakes, scones and afternoon tea I’ve had! Great hole in the wall place, come here often after the flower market on Sundays. You can tell the cakes are made with love, can’t recommend enough. Suggest going early because they will sell out!

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Super cute and cosy cafe tucked into a vintage store. Came here specifically for the scones as I saw they were highly rated. Definitely not disappointed. So fresh and soft, comes with your choice of jam and clotted cream. We also got a pot of English breakfast tea and an Orange Earl Grey cake to share. The cake had authentic orange flavour and it was really moist too. Friendly service :) will be back for sure for more scones and cake!!

title: Regency Cafe
review_text: cafe that grew more and more on me as the day went on, had a full English breakfast here, prepared with both fried and scrambled egg, and it was the most savoury meaty flavorful dish, had all the works including my first bubble and squeak which was a great mashed potato dish along with the black pudding which was savoury and had a great texture like a burger but only slightly iron tasting and the rest of the works just tasted good along with a really surprising hong kong style milk tea Which tied out nicely and despite the long line it only took half an hour from arrival to being set with our meal. so it's highly efficient and well run organisation and tasted good. even heated up later that night. would definitely recommend trying for sure and tempted to swing back at some point in the future

title: Regency Cafe
review_text:
We went to the Regency Café on a Saturday morning at around 8 and it did not disappoint. If you are looking for a classic English fry up then this is the place to go. Cannot fault it.
I had the bubble and squeak which was potatoey and cabagey (delicious) and my partner had the black pudding and hash browns.
Overall 10/10

title: Regency Cafe
review_text:
The local full English breakfast!!
After three attempts, I finally got it!

The most important is that have to be there before 10:00am or have to wait for long😂
The English breakfast was perfect and the atmosphere of the restaurant was very typical.
I and my wife loved the “Bread in butter”!!!
You cannot see “Bread in butter” in menu!
You can add the extra side menu whatever you want! Staffs are very kind as well.

I definitely suggest it!

title: Regency Cafe
review_text:
Me and my friend ended up at Regency Café the other morning completely by accident. We were just walking around, hungry and kinda confused about what we even wanted, and the place was right there so we just walked in without thinking. It has that loud old-school London diner energy, the kind where you feel like everyone else already knows the routine except you, but in a funny way.

I ordered probably the most chaotic breakfast plate I’ve had in a long time. I had half a donut (literally just half, don’t ask, I wasn’t in the mood for the whole thing), some raw salmon because I somehow convinced myself it was a good idea, eggs, beef bacon, and those little biscuits. It honestly looked like I picked items from five different menus, but weirdly it kind of worked. The salmon tasted fine, eggs were exactly what they should be, and the beef bacon was actually really good. The biscuits were a bit dry but I just dipped them into whatever was on my plate and it made sense in the moment.

My friend was laughing at my food choices the entire time. The staff were quick and didn’t fuss around, which I liked. The whole place has that chaotic but comforting London vibe. I’d go again just for the experience honestly.

title: Regency Cafe
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Proper London brekkie, mate!

Ain’t no place like Regency Café, innit? Proper old-school London vibes — eggs, bacon, beans, sausage, toast, the whole lot. Tastes bangin’, and the place looks straight outta a movie.

Staff’s sound, prices fair, and the atmosphere’s just pure London. If you’re after a proper English breakfast, this is the spot. Can’t go wrong, bruv!

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: Popped in here as we were around the area and it was such a delicious brunch. It is halal and everything was cooked to perfection. My cappuccino and daughter’s babycinno was also perfect and I would definitely come again. Their lunch menu looks great! Service was warm and fast

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: We stopped for breakfast out of the rain and this place was perfect. They have some lovely cooked options, which my mum and sister thought were great. I had trouble deciding between all the cakes and pastries, decided on the almond croissant and it was amazing. It was huge - plenty big enough for breakfast, warmed and oozing with an almond filling. The juices were also lovely and although a small cafe, there were enough tables and service was great thank you!

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: Really lovely small cafe well located. The staff was very kind and clearly cared about the regulars who came in often (prime people watching). I had a plain croissant and a pumpkin latte which was very good--not too sweet or fake tasting. I wish I were staying more locally to return.

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: This cafe offers a highly enjoyable experience, featuring a full English breakfast that is both delicious and satisfying, accompanied by quality coffee. We were fortunate to meet the chefs, who were very courteous and inquired about our satisfaction with the meal. It is a welcoming establishment with a friendly atmosphere. We look forward to returning. Our gratitude extends to the staff and chefs for their excellent service and hospitality.

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: Really cute café. The staff are ridiculously nice and helpful, and the selection of food and cake is great. My mum also said her carrot cake was the best she's ever eaten. And, well, mums know everything.

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text:
It was a really good breakfast.
The place has a really nice atmosphere 👌
I highly recommend it.

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text: Great food, I love that you just pick what you want rather than having to take off what you don’t and great service. Easy to park outside also very cheap

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text: What an incredible café! Friendly, polite, and professional from the moment you walk in. The team is knowledgeable and always happy to guide you through their amazing menu. The food is truly second to none—every dish is prepared to perfection. I would highly recommend this café to both locals and visitors to the area!

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text:
A little hidden away from the main road, made a visit because of the good reviews.

Plenty of options, so I took a little time before I settle with *Grilled or breaded Chicken*. Didn't read properly, because I was confused due to lack of sleep, server didn't have much patience to explain to me that there are options. Grilled or breaded chicken; chips or salad. At the meantime she was busy serving the other customers. Well... To them, there was amazing service; to me, there was neglecting. So to speak, regular customers might have extra care.

Whatever that is, not the best experience I had, will not be going there again. Though the food looked great, was a shame that the portion was too small for me or I had no luck. They ran out of chicken. If I was told so when I made the order, would have been cool to switch. Overall, I felt unwelcome.

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text: Hidden gem. Amazing place for breakfast

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text:
Greatest English Breakfast in London!

Congrats to the chef, keep going we need good quality food and such an authentic place full of  character and great menu options and smiling faces 😄

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text: Friendly staff, great food and very reasonable price wise!

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text: Only got drinks here, but will definitely need to come back for the food. The hot bar and variety of breakfast and lunch dishes look very good and well priced. The iced strawberry matcha was very good and reasonably priced. The staff was also very polite. Definitely plan on returning!

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text:
Nipped in for a quick breakfast while staying at the nearby Travelodge.
Had a lovely vegetarian breakfast. Friendly service, and a nice atmosphere.

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text:
I had a great experience at this café. The prices are very reasonable and affordable, especially considering the quality of the food. The breakfast and lunch options are excellent, and the buffet is absolutely amazing. Everything was fresh, tasty, and well presented.
The atmosphere is welcoming and comfortable, and the staff are friendly and professional. It’s a perfect place to enjoy a relaxed breakfast or lunch with friends or family.
I highly recommend this café and I will definitely come back again!

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text: We were so glad to find this little gem for breakfast. The salmon breakfast was superb and my husband found his English breakfast first class. Great coffee, freshly squeezed orange juice- all perfect and it a decent price. £33

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text: So lovely! Service was warm, friendly and very quick. Food was delicious. I had eggs, but saw the buffet afterwards which looked really fresh and varied. Good veggie options. Prices also really reasonable, especially for London

title: Cafe 101
review_text:
The cafe was light and airy and the atmosphere quiet - an oasis of calm in a busy city.
I am a wheelchair user and staff went above and beyond to assist me. My flat white was perfect!
There was lift access enabling the cafe to be accessible /  step-free despite the sign outside which stated that the cafe was down steps (I nearly rolled on by before deciding to ask if there was a lift). The tables were high enough to fit my wheelchair right under like a chair which really helps with eating! And plenty of space between tables so it was easy to move around

title: Cafe 101
review_text: Great place to meet friends for fellowship or have a quick bite to eat. Affordable prices in this part of London. Sustainable good quality foods.  Spacious, hygienic, WiFi, friendly staff ..... I  observed one of the staff going round  cleaning all the tables meticulously. Very clean toilets too.  In addition, I know they train the staff in a supportive environment so they are work ready for the big wide world. They run out of fish and chips. But I enjoyed my big portion of fresh mushrooms soup and tasty chunky bread and tartlet.

title: Cafe 101
review_text: A wonderful spot for anyone who loves good coffee and a peaceful environment. The café has a cozy charm with comfortable seating clean tables and soft background music that makes it perfect for both work and relaxation. I tried their iced latte and club sandwich and they were both outstanding. The latte had the perfect blend of flavor and strength and the sandwich was fresh well seasoned and nicely presented. The staff members were friendly and efficient making the whole experience even better. A truly impressive place 5 stars without hesitation.

title: Cafe 101
review_text: Delicious food, tea tasted wonderful (shout out to  the tea bag supplier NEMI). Staff smiley and helpful. I love this place. If I lived in London I’d eat here everyday!

title: Cafe 101
review_text: Limited menu with fairy basic meals but quite good value and a nice bright and clean cafe

title: Cafe 101
review_text:
London’s best kept secret for a fresh lunch offer that doesn’t cost the earth!
My go to destination if I’m in the area. Something for everyone, great breakfast lunch and afternoon tea offer, in a great location.

title: Cafe 101
review_text: In the center of London it's a quite and lovely place to dine in or for takeout. It's a  wide and open space to enjoy your meal

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text:
Very cute and wholesome experience. It did take me a while to be served but I'm not too picky on it. The food was quite mid in my opinion, overpriced. But I think you pay for the experience and vibes really. I got mako and hina as my servers. Mako was the one attending to me mostly and the one I got a cheeki with! She was very sweet and reserved in comparison to hina who was very bubbly and energetic. I really liked the dynamic!
For a total of one hour, one drink, one main course, a desert, and a photo it cost me 39.50£
Be warned that there is also a service charge of 12.5% on top of a minimum order per hour charge of at least one drink (or 7£) per hour.
I do believe it was worth my money and I left with my heart feeling lighter. A wholesome fun experience and just so darn cute!!

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text: Came here yesterday with two of my friends for my birthday. At first, when I tried opening the door, I realised that I had to ring the doorbell and so then afterwards seeing everyone else do the same thing made me feel less awkward. The café is small but still felt cute and whimsical, playing Japanese hyperpop, and when I was there the place was packed for the most part. I ordered the magic soda, the omurice, and the parfait, and each thing was amazing and made the wait feel completely worth it! Me and my friends all got to do a magic chant with the maids which at first I was nervous if I would like doing it or not but NO I absolutely loved it and the chants were a world of fun! We then got Happy Time with one of the butlers and played this card game called Hally Gally with her and we had so much fun we ended up spending so much time there that we were almost late for our tube! Absolute 10/10 experience, would absolutely go again at some point in the future!!!

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text:
Absolutely fantastic cafe. Really brings the vibe of Japanese maid cafes to the UK. The staff were amazing and made you feel welcome, I went for my birthday and the vibe was spot on for how these places are. My maid was amazing and I enjoyed powering up and putting magic spells on my good and drink. She draw an amazing hello kitty on my dinner as well.

If you want to maid cafe experience then go here and let yourself go (that's the important bit). I will be back one day 😊 😀

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text: It has the authenticity of a maid cafe in Japan but the homely feel of a nice cafe. Really enjoyed it. Would love to know what the spell translates to on english. Prepare to be called master (if male) and princess (if female)

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text: We came in out of pure curiousity, having little prior experience with maid cafés. The food was definitely worth the money, as were the drinks, and I was positively surprised. The decor of the place was nice too, a small and cozy atmosphere- not to mention the crane games downstairs. The maids were polite and good at their jobs. Overall a nice experience, if a little pricey overall, but quality does make up for this.

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text:
Though I had been to Japan twice before, I had never been to a maid cafe, so me and a friend thought it was a good idea to reserve a table here at the Usagi Anime Maid Cafe. It was a really cool experience, and the maids were super cool! I was also really surprised at how nice the omurice was.
Overall a very cool experience, I recommend it!

title: Double six cafe
review_text: Great place to have a meeting over a good breakfast before catching a train at Euston Station nearby. Service is prompt, gracious, and quick.

title: Double six cafe
review_text: After walking past this cafe many times, I finally had the opportunity to give Double Six a try and I wasn't disappointed. Quick service, food was hot and fresh. I was most impressed with the fried bread - not greasy, nice to have rather than hash brown. Plenty of seating. Clean and well maintained. Just round the side of Euston Station.

title: Double six cafe
review_text: Quick service nice food good value for Money. Toast Bit on the fatty side but lovely. Clean too.

title: Double six cafe
review_text: Lovely very clean café with very nice creamy coffee and a delicious breakfast. The staff were very professional and truly stand out — incredibly friendly, attentive, and always smiling, making you feel genuinely welcome from the moment you arrive. Their warm service creates a relaxed, comfortable atmosphere that makes you want to come back again.

title: Double six cafe
review_text:
Great service,  meal is set. Choose what option you want. Excellent value for money. 5 mins from Euston station.
Come here every year. Friendly lovely staff.

title: Double six cafe
review_text: Staff were polite and professional excellent choice of breakfasts. Fried bread a bonus hot bacon crisp egg cooked but yolk runny. Perfection on a plate.

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text: Had a lovely experience at Pottery Cafe, dog friendly. We booked in advance. They gave us a demonstration when we arrived and assisted when needed! It was a really fun and relaxing activity. Perfect for date ideas or girls day. There was a separate party room too for big groups. The cafe options were also incredible. We got a red velvet and carrot cake which were both delicious! Bonus to allow dogs in also.

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
I had such a lovely time at Pottery Café in Fulham! The atmosphere is warm, welcoming, and perfect for spending a relaxing few hours. The staff were really friendly and explained everything clearly, which made the whole pottery painting experience easy and fun, even for beginners like me.

What I enjoyed most was how calming and creative the whole session felt — it’s such a refreshing change from the usual cafés. The selection of pottery pieces was great, and they had a wide variety of paints and stencils to help spark ideas. It’s also a fantastic spot to enjoy with friends or even just some quiet time for yourself.

The café side was just as good — nice drinks and snacks to keep you going while you paint. Overall, it was a wonderful experience that I’d definitely recommend if you’re in Fulham and looking for something unique to do. I’ll definitely be coming back!

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
A very cosy place in London and it's such a cute spot with so many pottery options ranging from different prices✨️ There's a good amount of tables and the service is amazing. They give a simple demo on how to use their tools and paints to make the best out of your experience! The drinks are amazing and the cakes are delicious
They glaze the products and you can collect it within a week. It's so worth it, 100%

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
Girls’ Night Vibes 🎨✨ @pottery_cafe #fulham So me and my bestie spent the evening painting teacups… and honestly, it was way more addictive than we expected 😍 Mine’s The Little Prince, hers is Crayon Shin-chan x Tohato Chocobi 🍪💫 picking colours and patterns was half the fun, and the staff give you little tips so even if you’re not “artsy”, your cup still looks fab.

We enjoyed red velvet cake & iced mocha + iced matcha with cookies, everything are freshly baked by London’s top bakeries, and their coffee is roasted by @origincoffeeroasters ! all while chilling in their super cosy space with candles and music 🕯️💖Plus, the cups are exclusive Emma Bridgewater, only paintable in the UK — total little treasure!

Perfect for uni catch-ups, girls’ nights, or quirky date nights.

🎨 Teacup reveal on 21 Oct! Can’t wait to see our Little Prince & Crayon Shin-cha cups!

By fishenjoylife

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
My sister and I booked a session to celebrate our father’s birthday.
It turned out to be even more special than we expected.
We’ll take with us a piece of that moment as a ceramic keepsake.
The space is very well equipped, with great guidance and a fun, extensive menu.
I’d like to give a special thank-you to Simone, because when my dad was almost finished painting, he accidentally dropped and broke his mug, and Simone from the team promptly offered him a new one so he could complete it. Thank you so much for your kindness, Simone.
I will definitely be back and will recommend it!

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: Lovely place, staff is always friendly, food is lovely, enjoy their coffee as well. It is great to go there during a lunch break and read a book with a cup of coffee:) I love that they support charity as well, we need more mission driven businesses! 💓🙏🌸

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: The food was good, especially the vegan cami sandwich and the chocolate Chip Cookie! However, the strawberry matcha tasted pretty bland unfortunately. Also there are no toilets available:(

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: Sensational pastries, great coffee and a friendly service !!! The pastry was so good that I had to get a second to takeaway!  Absolutely loved everything about this coffee shop. Really cheap prices too and a real hidden gem!! Highly recommend!

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: Love this place!! So nice that it has opened round the corner from Guys hospital. We hospital workers were looking for somewhere cosy with independent coffee and good vibes. This place donates all proceedings to the community. The salad was absolutely delicious and the tea was tantalising. The staff were super friendly and accommodating. I felt so relaxed here. Will definitely be back!!!

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text:
I recently stumbled upon Clubhouse Cafe 2 in London Bridge, and it was a delightful experience. The cafe is cozy and cute, with friendly and attentive waitresses. I highly recommend trying their salads - they are the best I have ever had! The cafe also has plenty of vegan-friendly options. The flat white I ordered was perfect, and they offer every alternative milk under the sun, which is a big plus for me. I had to come back for one of their toasties, which was hands down the best toasty I've ever had in my life. The portion sizes are generous, and the prices are very reasonable. Another great thing about this cafe is that all profits go to a charity that supports the local community.
Overall, Clubhouse Cafe 2 is a fantastic spot to grab a bite and support a good cause.

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text:
I own a cafe/ coffee shop with a 4 star food hygiene rating. I've never seen a place with such abysmal food hygiene standards. Really shocking. I'm surprised NHS staff eat here. Atmosphere seems nice but I wouldn't risk eating here. Some of the 5 star reviews they've got seem fake.

Just to respond to your comment, I am not your competitor, I'm based in Forest Gate, definitely not competition. It's a very lazy excuse. You should respond maybe but cleaning up your act and following food safety and hygiene regulations. Maybe take constructive criticism on board rather than deflect and call me your competitors. We are no way competitors.

title: Green Cafe
review_text:
Green Cafe is a charming, family run spot where you’re always welcomed with a smile. The service is friendly and attentive, making it a pleasant place to start your day.

The food is freshly made, and most dishes stand out in terms of quality and taste. However, the pancakes seemed microwaved and didn’t taste as fresh as the other breakfast options. Despite this, portion sizes are generous, ensuring you get good value for your money.

The atmosphere is calm and inviting, though it can get quite busy during the morning rush. If you’re a coffee lover, their freshly brewed coffees with added flavors are a highlight smooth, aromatic, and satisfying. For something sweeter, the vanilla milkshake is absolutely delicious and worth trying.

Overall, Green Cafe offers a delightful dining experience with just a few areas for improvement. It’s definitely a great spot for breakfast or a relaxing coffee break!

title: Green Cafe
review_text: Killer breakfast. Service was fantastic, food was super quick and everything was delicious. Partner had the best poached egg she’s had in years.

title: Green Cafe
review_text: Had a good breakfast here. Excellent cappuccino!

title: Green Cafe
review_text: Quality tasty food by family-run amazing Turkish lady chef, excellent value, generous portions and lovely sunny seating outside.  Freshest food in Mayfair/Victoria.

title: Green Cafe
review_text: Very friendly staff staff. Enjoyed a proper English breakfast. And the price was also very affordable for the location.  Would definitely come back when I go back to London. Is a 5 minute walk from the Victoria coach station.

title: Green Cafe
review_text:
Excellent place where you can have a  tasty British breakfast.

A bunch of local people use to eat there so good place to avoid tourist scam. Furthermore, the Brazilian waiters are very friendly. I recommend it.

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text:
We had a wonderful brunch at Pear Tree Cafe in Battersea Park. The location is fantastic, surrounded by greenery, and the park itself is absolutely beautiful. It creates a relaxed yet posh brunch atmosphere that we really loved.

We ordered the scrambled eggs with salmon, and it was both very filling and delicious. Exactly what you want from a late morning meal. The cappuccino was the same size as the latte, which felt unusual at first, but we were pleasantly surprised and very happy about it!

It can be a bit difficult to find a seat because the cafe is quite popular, but once you settle in, the experience is definitely worth it.

If you are looking for a stylish brunch spot with great food and a lovely park view, this place is a perfect choice. Highly recommended!

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text: Serene location overlooking the lake. Good quality coffee and food. Was not to busy midweek so service was quick. Large outside seating area.

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text:
£ 8.40 was a bit steep for what turned out to be 2 cups of milk froth with a very subtle flavour of cheap coffee, especially when I had ordered 2 cappuccinos. I appreciate many cafes and bars are feeling the strain these days but if you are charging this kind of money for coffee served in a paper cup, at least make it taste good and use ingredients. It's not as though there isn't enough mark up.
Sorry if this sounds harsh but no one likes being ripped off.
On the plus side, staff were pleasant enough but definitely not a place for coffee lovers.

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text: Love it here !!!! Scrambled eggs is 👌🏼👌🏼 don’t be put off by the long Que it moves really quick. Lovely seating area outside

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text:
This is a really happening spot, perfect for catching up with friends. It’s a lovely daytime café offering great vegan and vegetarian options. I tried the smashed avocado toast, which was delicious, hearty, and filling, and the prices are quite reasonable too.
In the evening, they open up the outdoor bar, and I even noticed a BBQ station. The music, outdoor seating, and beautiful lake view create such a fun and relaxing atmosphere. Definitely a place I’d recommend if you’re looking to chill, eat well, and enjoy the park!

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text: Lovely lakeside location in Battersea Park with a spacious, dog-friendly café that’s great for people-watching. My scrambled eggs with smoked salmon on sourdough were nicely done, though my husband said that his bacon & egg roll was ‘average’. The only odd thing was having tea and coffee served in paper cups when dining in — I’d have preferred proper mugs. Overall, a nice spot for breakfast.

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: If you're looking for a relaxing break near the shopping hub of Regent Street, this is it. It's a proper traditional cafe tucked away, offering a calm respite. The staff is genuinely friendly, and the classic menu is executed well. We enjoyed an excellent cappuccino, a tasty chocolate malt, a satisfying hamburger, and a delicious tuna melt toastie. Grab a table outside on a crisp fall day and enjoy the quiet pleasure of watching the world pass by. A real local find!

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: Brilliant café, so friendly and everything is fresh and delicious!! Highly recommended. So great having somewhere like this just a stone's throw from Oxford Circus. I would come here all the time if I lived in London!

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: Nice place to sit and feel like a local. The décor is simple and traditional, and the staff are very friendly—especially Anna, who gave us such warm service. The Big English Breakfast is a must, and we also had the pleasure of trying a Pastel de Nata made by a Portuguese baker, as well as a delicious apple pie with custard.

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: Arrived in the area early for a meeting so I wandered around and found this place. I had the breakfast. Every item was really delicious, from the bread to sausage. The whole lot. 10/10. I love cafes and I can honestly say this is one of the best I’ve been to in London in years. Others of note are Terrys near Southwark , and Bar Bruno on Dean street.

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: A good place to have a  full English breakfast!! I loved the bread slices with salty butter on it !! Big portion that i couldn’t even finish ! Recommended

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: A small cafe with a varied menu. The food was very nice, but get there early as seating scarce.

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: Had some amazing food, such a good spot to get brunch and great value pricing! Recomand!

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: Such a warm and welcoming café! The food was absolutely delicious and the staff super friendly. We were kindly suggested to have the lentil soup, the spinach and feta wrap and the halloumi wrap. All of it tasty and perfect for a rainy day in London. We also had the best suprise with a complementary Azerbaijan tea by the lovely staff. Would definitely recommend!

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: A cozy little cafe! It has a small sitting area with comfy cushions and a counter with barstools. The people running this place are extremely friendly and helpful. The coffee is great and the food delicious. The chicken wrap was one of the best I’ve ever had! Thanks for your hospitality!

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text:
Love this little local coffee shop😍, I always try to go there when here for work. Very decent prices vs big brands, better coffee and food.
Liked the bacon butty 👌

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: One of the best café experiences I’ve had in a long time. The staff are incredibly welcoming and the place has a very homely feel. You can tell they put genuine effort into everything from the presentation of food to maintaining cleanliness. I ordered a hot chocolate and garlic bread and both were outstanding the hot chocolate was rich and creamy and the garlic bread was crisp and flavorful. The environment is peaceful and perfect for conversations or a quiet break. Definitely a spot worth revisiting.

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: Think it has just changed hands - never ate here before so I can't comment on that - but the takeaway chicken wrap I just had was fantastic. Lots filling, juicy chicken, delicious herbs and vegetables -  good coffee too and a very friendly chap behind the counter. A big thumbs up from me! Skip Pret and give these guys a chance!

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: Amazing coffee and food. Huge plus that the service is top class, it’s rare in London where you actually feel very welcomed.

title: Amar Cafe
review_text:
Cozy little spot with a warm, local vibe. The espresso was strong and well balanced, served in beautiful rustic cups that make the whole experience feel intentional, not generic.
The place was busy but relaxed – a mix of locals grabbing coffee and people staying a bit longer. Service was quick and friendly, even with a queue.
If you’re looking for specialty coffee without pretension, this is a solid choice. I’d definitely come back.

title: Amar Cafe
review_text:
Really nice cafe with nice seating and good coffee/bakes.

The cafe is quite small inside, not the best for larger groups. There is some outdoor seating though which is lovely.

The coffee is very well prepared. Brownie is also excellent. Great for a pit stop.

title: Amar Cafe
review_text: Hype is real, guy used to compete in coffee pouring. Will ship you the most delicious cappuccino and if it’s not busy you’ll get a fun design. Vibes are impeccable, great outdoor seats

title: Amar Cafe
review_text: It’s really great coffee and I am pretty picky being a home barista myself and world coffee traveler! The baked goods looked delicious, the music and atmosphere feel fun and mostly welcoming; it’s very small inside maybe seats for 8 people give or take. Outside more seating and in a charming area off main Chelsea shopping. I did find the staff to be a little dry and not super friendly, they were also very busy. I think it’s a cute cafe and I think it has excellent coffee.

title: Amar Cafe
review_text:
Lovely and hidden coffe shop.

Decent terrace

Good quality beans and amazing final product.

Vegan options available.

We will come back for certain

title: Amar Cafe
review_text: Great Coffee, beautiful place to sit, relax, and enjoy your bite. Would have been better if they had toilets :)

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe

review_text: I hopped into this cute cafe for breakfast and had the most delightful experience. Mansour was very welcoming and the food was delicious. I had breakfast then got a take-away brownie for my long flight and it was amazing. I highly recommend breakfast here then wandering around Notting Hill.

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe

review_text: This place is lovely!! The pancakes were sooooo fluffy and tasty, and the mushrooms and eggs toast was really really crunchy. Also, the service was fast and kind, we highly recommend it :))

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe

So glad to have stepped in.
Ps. The bathroom is so pretty and I loved the sink 💜

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe
place_title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text: Florentine - Avocado - fruity pancakes - breakfast tea - cappuccino and avo smoothie. EVERYTHING DELICIOUS and super sweet and precise service. A 5 star experience. We loved it! Thank you!
