Great spot for a brekkie! Our poached eggs weee delicious! Albeit a bit smaller than I wished but I was hungry so not their fault. Also the plates came warm so it kept the temperature for longer.

The coffee is decent

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text: Friendly staff, good food and it was also ready quickly eventhough it was quite busy in the morning. Nice music playing as well :)

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text: Such a lovely restaurant! The staff are incredibly kind, generous, and welcoming  truly adorable. Everything is very clean, the food is delicious, and you can really feel that everything is made with love. We had a great time and would definitely come back.

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text:
We had an overnight stay in London and came to this cafe on a whim and I'm so glad we did. The staff are so friendly and actually welcomed us back when we returned the day after!
I had fruity pancakes and my partner had the full English breakfast. The food is amazing quality as are the drinks. The cafe is decorated lovely and cosy and is just an overall good experience. Would definitely recommend and we will definitely be back!

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text: Had a full english  brakefast which was tasty and cheap. Am 6,4 tall and one plate was more than enough for me. Fair prices. Waited 5-10 min for a food. Food tasty and the lady was so nice to us. Thank you!!

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text: The best choice of London, Amazing employee with an excellent service and a wonderful menu ! Thank you for having us explicit to the Two Sisters, Kind regards of Amsterdam

title: moko made cafe
review_text: The cafe itself is cosy and cute. I had the tuna mayo onigiri (this was very salty and was majority rice), matcha latte (really good) and matcha roll cake (the matcha flavour was nice but the cake was a bit dry). The staff were nice and friendly.

title: moko made cafe
review_text: Such a cute cafe and the food was so good. I got the Iced Matcha float and Strawberry Shortcake. Not a matcha fan but will definitely be going back for it; so delicious and creamy. And the cake was soft and not too sweet. The mismatched dinnerware is adorable and the decor is so cosy. You can’t always guarantee a seat but was lucky to be there when it wasn’t busy. 11/10

title: moko made cafe
review_text: When I saw this as the number one spot for matcha in the Beli app I had to go and see for myself and was not left disappointed. The vibes of the place are lovely with a traditional Japanese cafe look that provides a relaxing vibe. Also being a bit away from central London definitely has its perks as there was no queue when we went on a Saturday after lunch. A bit crowded but definitely adds to its charm. The service was very good as well as the waitress was lovely. Now on to the important stuff. The float I thought at the first the matcha tasted really strong (so you know the quality is high) but also when the ice cream started melting it was definitely one of the best matchas I’ve had. But the most unexpected suprise was the matcha cheesecake which was the creamiest and most tasty dessert I’ve had that I had to buy another slice to take home. This is must place for matcha lovers or even someone who just wants a nice dessert in a more quiet cafe in London.

title: moko made cafe
review_text:
⛾ matcha latte、kinako latte
𓐐 matcha swiss roll、salmon onigiri

the place itself is so cozy and cute. i tend to avoid ordering matcha because it’s so overpriced for something i can make better at home, but my friend ordered it; i was impressed!! kinako latte is not something i see often, and luckily theirs is delicious. matcha swiss roll paired so well with the drinks. i would skip the onigiri here, but my standards are the perfect and cheap ones from convenience stores in japan LOL

title: moko made cafe
review_text: great service, amazing matcha quality. i got a matcha float and it was so yummy! only thing was i was studying there and i was eating a small pot of yoghurt as my breakfast to have with my matcha and i was told to put it away, so just bare that in mind to not bring any of your own food. aside from that i will definitely be back

title: moko made cafe
review_text: I love this cafe. The ambience is comforting and the interior design is immaculately curated. Went on a Sunday at 10 am and within 30 minutes the cafe was full. Their matcha was really good and the gluten free brownie was quite dry (but that’s how gluten free baked goods usually taste). I also like their onigiri only thing is the rice to filling ratio is off. I wish they added more salmon filling inside but besides that the rice is well seasoned. Such a safe, cosy spot for girls 💖

title: moko made cafe
review_text:
Ive been to this place twice. Great great matcha cheesecake and miso soup, good food in general and great authentic vibes. I’d always go there again.
The only bad thing is that it gets crowded easily and you may need to wait in the queue to get a table. I went there just 5 mins after it opened and there were already people inside, got a table though.

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text: A gem in the back streets, literally a stone's throw away from Waterloo station. I was at the station and wanted a light lunch. I looked at Google Maps and found this cafe just 100 metres away. All the staff were lovely and the food, a BLT sandwich, was simple but delicious. The cup of tea was perfect. Both cost just £7, much cheaper than I had expected so close to a mainline railway station.

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text: Lovely place if you need a hearty breakfast or just a quick stop to refuel. They have portugues delicacies such as pastel de nata (custard tart) and bolo de arroz (a DELICIOUS sponge cake topped with sugar, my favourite!) I had a scrambled egg with smoked salmon that was wonderful. Definitely recommended this place.

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text:
I walked in absolutely starving at 8 a.m., just looking for any place that was open — and Waterloo Café totally saved my life. The man who welcomed me was super friendly, got me seated right away, and in less than five minutes, my coffee, orange juice, and breakfast were already on the table.

I had the “deluxe breakfast”, toast with scrambled eggs and salmon — the portions were big, everything was fresh and delicious. The coffee was great, the juice tasted natural, and the whole meal hit the spot.

The service was fast, warm, and genuinely kind. It’s not easy to find a place open that early with such good energy and food.

Highly recommended. I’d definitely come back.

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text: Our first breakfast here in London. And it’s amazing all. Thanks for all the staff

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text: I really enjoy coming here to have breakfast as the staff are very friendly, food is tasty and seating area very comfy. I highly recommend this place. Come and enjoy a Portuguese pastry (Pastel de Natas). 🤤

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text:
Wow!
It opens at 5am!!! A quiet, unassuming quaint cafe with a small sized seating area upstairs (more than enough seating at non busy times!)

I opted for an Omlet with salad. The salad was clearly very fresh and had half an avocado and tomatoes as part of the salad. The Omlet was enjoyable and the salad was more than just a garnish. This small breakfast was clearly made with passion.

The staff were very polite and I didn’t feel rushed to leave which can be an issue  in some cafe’s in central London.

A lovely, peaceful experience.
I gave 5 stars for the atmosphere as sometimes, it’s nice to have a little bit of peace in away from the hustle and bustle of central
London.

Wheelchair accessible downstairs with a couple of seats just near the entrance

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text: proper grub, lovely service, value and taste for food and decent coffee

title: Waterloo Cafe
review_text:
Nestled on the unassuming Wootton Street in Waterloo, Waterloo Cafe is a haven for weary travelers and hungry locals alike. Stepping inside, the aroma of freshly brewed coffee and sizzling bacon instantly put my stomach at ease. In search of a hearty breakfast to fuel my day, I opted for a classic – the omelette, baked beans, fried tomato, hash browns, and buttered toast combo. And let me tell you, it was a symphony of comfort food that hit all the right notes.

The omelette, nicely cooked. The baked beans, simmered to a perfect sweetness, danced around the omelette in a comforting hug. The fried tomato, juicy and bursting with sunshine, added a touch of acidity to cut through the richness. The hash browns, crispy on the outside and soft within, provided a satisfying textural counterpoint. And the buttered toast, a canvas for golden butter, was the perfect base for mopping up all the deliciousness.

The charm of Waterloo Cafe extends beyond the delicious food. The friendly staff, buzzing with the energy of a well-oiled machine, added to the warm and inviting atmosphere.

As I left Waterloo Cafe, my stomach full and my heart happy. The combination of nicely cooked food, friendly service, and a welcoming atmosphere made it an experience that went far beyond simply fuelling up.

If you're looking for a hearty, no-frills breakfast, look no further than Waterloo Cafe.  Just be prepared to share your table with the locals, because this Waterloo gem is definitely a neighbourhood favourite.

title: Cafe OTO
review_text: What a gem! Artsy music café with an eccentric and informed lineup. Will be coming back - here you see Víg Mihály & Balaton. They were so great, please do invite them again 👌

title: Cafe OTO
review_text: Love this place. It’s so welcoming. Independent, quirky, and full of character. The staff are genuinely lovely and remember their regulars, which makes it feel like a community. The coffee and pastries are great, and the space is welcoming for those working. Just the best vibe. Thank you.

title: Cafe OTO
review_text: One of the best venues for live music in London. Great shows every night from stunning artists putting on unique performances. Lots of good places to have dinner nearby too.

title: Cafe OTO

sells lots of records ,jazz,experimental music and it holds this same kind of live events. I didn't know how well known it is in experimental circles it is. Friendly staff . I can imagine the night time events will be very atmospheric there.

Yea top boy put number 1 cafe on the map but it’s been going on 30 years in business  so props to the family holding this spot down! I will be back!

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text:
A Notting Hill Gem: Exceptional Coffee and Heavenly Treats
​If you find yourself wandering through the charming streets of Notting Hill, there is one spot you absolutely cannot miss: Mawi Coffee. From the moment we stepped inside, we felt an immediate sense of warmth and hospitality. It is one of those rare places where you don’t just feel like a customer, but like a welcome guest in a friend’s home. We truly felt super comfortable throughout our entire visit.
​The highlights, without a doubt, were the baked goods. We had the pleasure of trying a variety, and each was more impressive than the last:
​Orange Cake: This was a standout—perfectly moist with a vibrant, citrusy zing that wasn't overly sweet.
​Apple Pie: It tasted like a nostalgic, homemade masterpiece with a crisp crust and beautifully spiced apples.
​Blueberry Muffin: Often a simple staple, but Mawi’s version was excellent—bursting with fresh berries and featuring a perfect crumb.
​To wash down these treats, we ordered a Coffee and a Matcha. The coffee was expertly brewed, boasting a rich, smooth profile that satisfied even the pickiest caffeine lovers in our group. The Matcha was equally impressive—vibrant green, silky in texture, and possessing that perfect balance of earthy and creamy notes that is so hard to find.
​The atmosphere at Mawi is the perfect escape from the hustle and bustle of London. Whether you are looking for a quiet corner to relax or a friendly spot to catch up with friends on a first-name basis, this cafe delivers on every level. The combination of excellent food, delicious drinks, and a cozy vibe makes it a must-visit.
​We left with full stomachs and big smiles. If you are in the neighborhood, do yourself a favor and stop by Mawi Coffee—you won’t regret it!

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text: The staff was very friendly and welcoming. The iced coffee was fine, but the lox bagel felt a bit too simple for the price. Maybe I’m just used to NY–style bagels, but for me it seemed a little overpriced. 11.94💷

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text:
This cafe is a haven! From the moment you walk in, you're greeted by the warm aroma of fresh-baked goods and the inviting hum of conversation. The music creates a perfect ambiance for relaxing, catching up with friends, or even getting some work done.

They offer a surprisingly wide selection of food for a cafe, and the quality is top-notch. I had the toasted bagel with eggs and avocado, and it was delicious. The omelette was perfectly cooked, and the crispy bagel added a great textural contrast. My flat white was excellent – you could really taste the coffee (no burnt beans here!).

My partner, who has a sweet tooth, loved the blueberry bread but with salted butter (got it at home).

Beyond the delicious food and drinks, the staff truly shines. They're friendly and welcoming, and it seems they know many regulars by name and their preferences. It creates a warm and familiar atmosphere.

I'll definitely be back, and I highly recommend this cafe!

Pro tip: If you visit in the morning, they have a special offer on coffee and treats for takeaways!

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text: Love this place – good coffee, really nice and cozy atmosphere, friendly staff, great place to work with WiFi! A good escape from a busy street.

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text: Incredible full English breakfast! Very fair price for the huge quantity of food. Also highly recommend getting their orange juice which is freshly squeezed! Service was fantastic despite us having a bigger party and the restaurant being busy.

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text:
The food was amazing! I had a bagel with salmon, avocado, and egg, and had a hard time finishing it for how much food there was (lol). However, the hospitality needs some more refinement, and not from everyone, but rather by the short-haired blonde lady 😅 (who may be in charge?).

As soon as this said lady made me seat at the tall table, I expected she would clean the mess and perhaps arrange the cables located on the table (as you can see in the pic). No, instead she just told me to look at the menu and she never really cleaned the table for me, and then eventually came to ask for my order.

Now here is the funny part, which might just be a cultural shock (but I’m still giving one star to the service nonetheless 🥲). Because I was undecided about what to order, I kindly asked her “what would you recommend me getting?” She responds “Well, that’s not my responsibility, you are the one eating, not me.” Hahahahahahah. First mental reaction “how rude!” And second one “are all British like this?”

Here the lesson is, even if your culture is not big on small talk or even giving a brief recommendation, maybe try a little more to be warm to the crowd!

I wasn’t expecting her to feed me or something hahaha I just wanted a recommendation, but she was dismissive!

On top of that, she kind of micromanaged the cooks and the rest of the waitresses and was loud while doing so, which didn’t help the atmosphere. I was by myself, so I could hear everything with more care and pay attention to the environment, and let me tell you it wasn’t a nice, relaxing environment lol. I get it, there’s a line of people outside, and you have a bunch of customers in the coffee shop. Still, it doesn’t justify the fact that you are rude and you don’t even properly prepare the table for a new guest.

Apart from that, once again, the food was phenomenal.

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text:
An easy mission to return at least once every two weeks. Ordered the Salmon, Eggs & Avo & requested extra salmon without an additional charge!

The portions are ideal, with lovely employees and a Diner-like atmosphere. Can't skip on the quick service and free sweets at the end :)

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text: Just opposite to the Notting Hill book store. Quite energetic vibe! Very near the square / market. It’s an Extremely nice restaurant. French toast with lots of fresh fruits. The egg salmon toast is delicious! Reasonable price. Most importantly, the service and the view is wonderful. Such a relaxing one.

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text: Food was ok. Poached eggs were overcooked. Overall, it tasted fine and plate presentation was nice. Shame that they allow recurring customers using their cafe as their own living room. A dog was sitting on the couch while eating food from the table. The customers stared at us the whole time they had another people joining. Very rude and appalling that cafe’s staff allowed that behaviour

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text:
I sat at an outdoor table.
It’s right across from the Notting Hill Bookshop, so you can really enjoy your time here.
The food was delicious, and the staff were very friendly.

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text: Fantastic breakfast spot in London! The portions were generous, prices were very reasonable, and the staff were genuinely friendly and welcoming. Suzie was an amazing host — she even gave us a discount, which was a lovely surprise. Highly recommended if you’re looking for a great start to your day!

title: Mike's Cafe
review_text: Top, looking for a full English breakfast as it should be this is the place, genuine food and atmosphere. The sausages were perfect 😍. Proper toast with butter etc. They make you feel welcome , had time to chat with the staff and even offer toasted marshmallows by the fire, which we did. Thankyou for the best breakfast in London that doesn’t leave you feeling ripped off.

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text:
What can I say that hasn't been said about this great institution? I love hip hop and the jazz cafe always brings the talent. I've been here so many times, I know this place intimately.
If your favourite artist is in town and they're performing in the jazz cafe. You simply have to come, it'll be a sin if you don't.
Jazz cafe is nice and intimate. From any vantage point, you will be able to see the stage and the acoustics are also great.
If you're posh and don't want to mix with the plebs, you can sit upstairs where I'm sure you'll have an excellent view of the stage and performer.
I've always enjoyed my time at the jazz cafe. I can't emphasize enough how lovely this place is. If you live in London and you're a lover of life music. You have to come to the jazz cafe.

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text:
If you’re looking for the coolest venue going in London with insane vibes, chilled crowds, great food and amazing drinks — look no further than The Jazz Cafe in Camden. A true London institution since it opened in 1990 (over 35 years in the same iconic Camden Town spot!), this place consistently delivers unforgettable nights of music and atmosphere that keep me coming back again and again.  ￼

From the moment you walk in, the energy is electric — it’s intimate, vibrant, and buzzing without ever feeling chaotic. Over the decades, this legendary space has played host to some of the biggest and most respected names across jazz, soul, funk, hip-hop and world music — from Amy Winehouse, D’Angelo, Roy Ayers and The Roots to Bobby Womack, Adele and countless other icons.  ￼

But it’s not just history — it’s experience. The crowd is always relaxed and engaged, the acoustics intimate and immersive, and every show feels like you’re part of something special. Personally, I’ve had the pleasure of seeing two Nujabes tributes performed by the insanely talented Stompysplayground here — and each time it was a beautiful, almost religious experience that I’ll never forget.

The vibe upstairs is perfect for dining and watching the show from a table, while the downstairs standing crowd gives you an up-close and full-on gig experience. On the food and drink front, don’t sleep on the triple-picked chicken and the jerk chicken — absolute must-orders — washed down with a Burnt Pear Old Fashioned and a Spiced and Stormy for the perfect soundtrack to your evening.

In short: highly recommended for locals, visitors, music lovers and foodies alike. Whether you’re there for the next big act or a deep-cut tribute night, The Jazz Cafe is hands-down one of London’s best live music venues and social experiences. Get down there you won’t regret it! 😎🎶🍗🍸

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text: Jazz Cafe is a fantastic cozy venue to see great artists up close. The intimate setting makes every performance feel personal and immersive. If possible, book a seat in the restaurant area, as there are very few seats downstairs and it fills up quickly. Great atmosphere, great sound, and a brilliant spot for live music lovers.

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text:
If you thought it couldn’t get any better, the new menu launch last week proves otherwise. Every dish we tried felt thoughtfully reimagined — bold flavours, beautiful presentation, and combinations that genuinely surprised us in the best way. The kitchen has clearly raised the bar again, and it shows in every bite.

The updated selection strikes that perfect balance between comforting favourites like miso cod and exciting new additions prawn dumplings and tacos. There’s a real confidence in the cooking vibrant, well-seasoned, and plated with care.

The atmosphere remains just as special  warm, lively, and effortlessly stylish and the service continues to be polished yet welcoming. Tommy was really great with my dad considering he is deaf and his English isn’t perfect Tommy still managed to make it super enjoyable for us.It all comes together to create an experience that feels fresh but still familiar.

If you haven’t seen what’s new yet, this is your sign. The latest dishes deserve to be tasted  and sooner rather than later.

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text:
I booked an accessible area ticket for my mum who is a wheelchair user - the staff couldn’t have been more helpful. A ramp was produced to get us in and a small area had been roped off beside the stage, with a fully accessible toilet less than 10 steps away.

There was also a friendly security guard in our area which was reassuring as the event we went to was sold out so it was very busy. If you have a physical disability you can be confident that the venue will take care of you. Just make sure to book everything in advance via email and arrive when doors open so they can get you in safely.

Thank you to all at Jazz Cafe.

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text: Great music and fantastic service. Not only we had a great time, but the stuff went the extra mile to make sure we had a great experience while listening to the gig. Will definitely come back

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text: Had an amazing experience. A little hiccup at the beginning but they fixed it by giving me a drink for free. I would definitely go back.. 10/10 for Vibes 🥳

title: Jazz Cafe
review_text:
Awesome venue with great pubs close by. Fortunate to see Newcleus here recently. Friendly staff and quality sound system.

Always good acts here so keep an eye out of what’s coming up.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text: Lovely little lunch spot in the high street. I enjoyed my healthy feta and avocado salad and my son said the oreo milkshake was delicious. Nice efficient service. Good value for London.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text:
Hearty food in a cozy and comforting location. I’ve already been here twice in the span of a week. Such a good and fulfilling experience after a long walk.
Will def be back again and again 👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text: I’ve been coming here since 2019. The staff are very friendly, the breakfast is excellent, and the prices are reasonable. I especially love their pancakes and fresh fruit!

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text:
Great brunch with great mushroom, tomatoes.
Lovely staff.
And you dont want to queue for a long time.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text: Amazing English breakfast. Perfect eggs, bacon, hash browns, eggs Benedict’s and so on, topped off with a freshly squeezed orange juice and a nice cop of coffee. All for a reasonable price. Nice staff too. Cozy place, hyggelig vibe as we say in Denmark.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text:
Was planning on going to the popular cafe next door but the line was over 2 hours and so we decided to to give this place a go as the food looked good and it was busy but no queue.

Very happy that we did as it was a lovely breakfast. The full English had everything you could want and was fresh. The hollandaise sauce on the eggs Benedict was lovely too. Amazing bubble and squeak.

Don’t bother waiting in the queue for next door. Come here!

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text: Lovely, warm and inviting owners. Incredibly kind and hospitable people. I was treated to a delicious soup and iced tea. They love where they are from and they invite you into their heritage. This place felt like a second home, thank you guys :)

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text: Delicious Kurdish restaurant! Great options for both vegans and vegetarians. The food is fresh and authentic, the staff is friendly, and the atmosphere is cozy. Definitely recommended!

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text: Possibly the nicest café I’ve ever been to! The food was delicious and so fresh and absolutely not too expensive for Camden and the people working had a really lovely vibe. The decoration and ambiance created were really amazing, so comforting and fun, and the beautiful cat walking around was the cherry on the cake. I cannot recommend this place enough!!!

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text: probably my favourite cafe ever. came in here randomly after stumbling upon it and was so impressed. such good drinks, gorgeous decor, lovely staff…10/10 would recommend

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text:
I’ve been to this place twice now, and it already feels like a hidden gem in Camden Town. The vibe is super relaxed and welcoming  you instantly feel at home. The staff and owner are incredibly friendly, always with a warm smile and great energy. The food is fresh, tasty, and satisfying especially the falafel!
What really makes this spot special, though, is the atmosphere. It’s casual but full of heart. Oh, and they have the cutest cat named Lalo who just chills around and adds so much charm to the place.
Highly recommend if you’re looking for authentic Kurdish flavors in a cozy, down-to-earth setting. I’ll definitely be back!

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text: This is an amazing hidden delight right in the middle of Camden. It's quirky and intimate. The service is excellent and the food is fresh and yummy. We called in by chance and will definitely be back. It's a rare gem. We intended to pop in briefly and we're so enamoured that we stayed all afternoon .

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text:
A wonderful neighborhood cafe with all the comfort food one could wish for.
all day breakfast, burgers, english specialties and middle eastern treats - it’s hard to choose.
the staff is friendly, the location busy but cute and welcoming.

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: Went for a Boxing Day full English breakfast, and it did not disappoint! The food was delicious, everything cooked to perfection, portion size value for money and then this was all topped off by fantastic warm efficient customer service. It’s a must go. Highly recommend.

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: My favourite cafe in the area. English breakfast is delicious especially the sausages . They offer varied all day breakfasts. And other meals.

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: We got the full english breakfast. It was a good portion, and the food was delicious and fresh. Coffee is not included, you can add it by 3.45. They added without asking a 12.5% per service, which I consider a total excess.

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: Not fine dinning and definitely a homely place for a hearty meal.

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: Love this place. It is a hidden gem. Really good generous portions. Good nice English breakfast / you can have some American style as well. The eggs, beans, mushrooms, beef sausage, hash browns, the French toast are all top notch 👌. The coffee is ok though.

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text:
Extremely average. Classic style over substance. Your typical yummy mummy, leggings & white socks with new balance clientele.

Orders the courgette fritter breakfast after seeing several plates in the restaurant. Presentation was great, food was extremely under seasoned, luke warm & ingredients need to be of a much higher quality for the price. (£15-17). The poached egg was extremely watery and slightly undercooked too. Complained to the server about the food and was told “they’re in the process of changing the kitchen” whatever that means. Wouldn’t recommend.

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text:
Sunday has become a go-to local brunch spot every time I visit London. I cannot stress enough that the porridge lives rent-free in my mind. It's both a hearty dish with beautiful flavours and fresh ingredients. The English Breakfast has great portions with gorgeous beans, sausage, black pudding, mushrooms, and bacon. The poached egg was a bit runnier to my liking, but the rest was perfect.

The service can take longer than expected once the order is placed, but everyone here is lovely and extremely friendly. Just build in some padding when scheduling around a visit!

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text:
We had a beautiful brunch today at Sundays. It has such a homely, welcoming atmosphere. We had the avocado toast and the pancakes and they were both incredibly delicious. The fresh raspberry lemonade was the perfect compliment and it was sweet yet refreshing. We will
Definitely be back to try more of the menu.

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text: Really nice place. 3 of us went for breakfast and had the welsh rarebit and were talking about how great our breakfast was all day!! Also the best hot chocolate i've had in a while, and great fresh orange juice, would definitely reccomend!

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text: The buttermilk pancakes are insane! Thick, American style fluffy. Lovely little café

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text: I can finally say I’ve found my fav brunch place in London! The menu is simple but everything is done so well! The queue can be a bit long for a bigger group but it is well worth the wait

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text: I had a great experience at this restaurant. You are truly treated as a valued guest here, unlike some places in the city center where you often feel like just a number.

title: Café Millennium
review_text:
I recently visited an affordable local cafe located on Chapel Market, near Angel. It’s just a 10-15 mins walk from the Underground Station and very easy to find. The staff were warm and friendly, creating a nice atmosphere in the cafe, which also offers outdoor seating.
We ordered a Mocha, an Omelette with chips, and an English breakfast. The quality of the food and drinks was good and matched the prices.
I definitely plan to return for another visit!

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: Such a great and hearty full English breakfast - and super reasonably priced too! Service was lovely and food came out so quickly. Yummy vegetarian option as well

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: I have chosen my own breakfast from the list of items, and I'm was impressed with the quality of each item. Each item was cooked to perfection and most of them have been cooked on the grill, making them much healthier. The service was good, and the price was cheap for what we got. I totally recommend this place and definitely will he going back.

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: Rose cafe is a old style builder's cafe and not many are around anymore. We had a sausage roll and an English breakfast set 4 with a coffee. Very reasonable priced and lots of choice. The people serving were friendly.

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: I recommend this lovely Cafe:) It seems a family business that is run very smoothly at very affordable prices, but most importantly the food is a superb quality and is served very quickly, the tables are nice and the atmosphere is warm and welcoming! I would especially recommend it to travellers travelling to/from airports like London Stansted or Luton, and also who use intercity bus and coach routes, as well as locals and Londoners:) Wish best of luck and success to your business!)))

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: Rose Cafe does what is says in the tin - no frills, reasonably priced, comfort food. Located between the 02 shopping centre and Finchley Road + Frognal station it is a great early morning breakfast stop with doors opening at 6.30AM. The interior is large but dim, for the exception of a table next to a large window. The menu is fairly standard to most most British cafes - breakfasts, sandwiches, salads, omelettes, jacket potatoes. The health breakfast (£6.90) - two poached eggs on one brown toast, halloumi, avocado and mushrooms - was freshly cooked with a  reasonable portion size. Run by a Portuguese family, the service is warm and friendly.

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: Nice casual breakfast place.  Tried the rose special. Portions were generous and food was fresh and tasty.

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: Found this place by accident and it's like winning a lottery. Very good quality at a good price!

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: The three times I’ve tried a coffee here it’s been really bitter. Maybe it’s just so good that I don’t have the palate for it, or it’s really bad. Either way, the service is usually haphazard and unhelpful - I’ve waited over five mins for the crew to stop gossiping (about some party) while watching me standing at the counter waiting for service and saying they’ll be there in a minute - OR -  asking if I can buy a sandwich and being told ‘I don’t think we have any to sell today’ - ok then go check please and let me know - when you have a moment in your busy day of course …

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: Nice hidden place for business meetings or a calm espresso. Nice a quiet and classy looking with great espresso. In the lobby of a business building, so a bit hard to find from the street, but definitely worth it.

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: The coffee from any of the three Alchemy branches is always great. Alchemy Cafe Spitalfields provides a great place for meeting a colleague or hybrid working!

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text:
Barista has the balls to say I’m having lunch at 12.40, so you have to wait for 15 minutes.

Please close your shop hours accordingly.

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: Coffee is great!

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: Had a very nice drip brew here, would recommend.

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: fair

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: This place is special. I don't just get a coffee or a sandwich, even thought the toasties are the best. I get a human who truly cares and who has helped me find my place to work at in Illiffe Yard that I now have the delight everyday. Thank you for your generosity and curiosity. Can't wait to come back next week.

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: Really lovely vibe and excellent food - everything was delicious and from scratch and coffee was lovely

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: What a lovely place with lovely owners! It's quirky, artsy and really pleasant, full of old-school furniture and machines. Good coffee, juice and chocolate brownie.

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text:
This café is really cute ☕💫 and the person who runs it is incredibly kind 😊. I highly recommend stopping by and trying the food 🍰🥗 — it’s totally worth it! 👍💖

Souhaites-tu que ce soit plus

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: This place has all the rights things to be a great place. Ordered a cappuccino, a late, a brownie and a lemon tart. Sat down and waited for the coffees to be made. I noticed the barista smell the milk container and he is immediately put off. He comes around and says that they are fresh out of milk. We ordered them to be replaced with soy milk. The coffees were extremely disappointing. There are fruit fly's in the shop and it is full of clutter. The baked goods were decent. Definitely had high expectations from the reviews. It looks like the barista is new to the job. To top it off I had to ask for a receipt.

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: A hidden gem just off Walworth high st with nice homemade food, friendly staff and beautiful surroundings

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: Lovely quirky cafe and very welcoming. Wide range of food to order to meet people's dietary requirements. Very nice lady called louise who runs this lovely place. Had a nice coffee with a yummy homemade cake, will definitely be going back. Thank you

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: Such a lovely cafe! Moved into the neighborhood recently and am delighted to have Marios to pop into since after going - I know I would travel far to stop by! Cannot recommend enough :)

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: The caff has such a sweet atmosphere! Mario and the waitress were super nice and the food was delicious :)

title: Marios Cafe
review_text:
Small and cosy venue with a very familiar atmosphere.
The outdoor seating in summer is really nice in a quiet street with not a lot of traffic.
Plenty of take away sandwiches and panini.
We dined in last night and I had an amazing truffle pasta with creamy burrata. Absolutely to die for!!
We will definitely repeat!
(Dogs are welcome)

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: My lovely friend invited me to Marios  Italian Cafe for lunch - We had a ball - The food and atmosphere was just perfect. Mario served us with true love and made me feel very welcome being my first time to this vintage Cafe in Kentish Town . A hidden gem in the area ! I had chicken and Pasta and a delicious Ricotta Tart with Cherrys for pudding x I will definitely be going back to sit with all the other regular customers . Bravo Mario !

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: One of the best cafes in London. Excellent English and Italian favourites and of course puddings with custard.

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: This place offers the best English breakfast in town! I highly recommend it.

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: Amazing value. Good location. The owner is a sweet person. Great variety of food. The only "downside" is that the place is not so big, so get there soon and save your seat. Other than that, it's always a treat to be there.

title: Marios Cafe
review_text: The best cafe in North London. Mario is the treasure of Kentish Town.

title: Cafe Delight
review_text:
Cafe Delight – A True Hidden Gem

Cafe Delight is an absolute standout. From the moment you walk in, you’re welcomed with warm hospitality that instantly makes you feel at home. The owner, T, clearly takes great pride in the café, and it shows in every detail.

Food: The food is exceptional — fresh, flavorful, and beautifully prepared. Every bite reflects quality ingredients and genuine care. It’s the kind of place where you can taste the passion behind the menu.

Service: Service is outstanding. Friendly, attentive, and consistently welcoming, with T ensuring every guest feels valued. You’re never rushed, and every request is handled with a smile.

title: Cafe Delight
review_text: 10/10 Local greasy spoon serving up healthy portions of food! Had the pleasure of eating here for breakfast - staff all friendly and customers all giving off family vibes. Definitely get your monies worth dining here! Thank you!!

title: Cafe Delight
review_text:
I had the homemade chicken curry special today, made by the fab Suzan, who is a chef (hidden talent)
Have asked if there will be any other specials in the future!! 🤞

title: Cafe Delight
review_text:
Most affordable place for English breakfast. Most filling you will get after eating this meal. Largest portions. Only locals here. Thank goodness I saw all the locals coming in here cuz that’s a good sign of a good place.

Ordered the burger brunch. Follow along: Hash browns are thicccccc. French fries are crisp. The eggs are beautifully sunny side up . The burger patties are juicy juicy. Then you got hidden under the burger patties, beautifully grilled onions. Just delightful

Breakfast for 10 pounds or less. Can’t get any better. Service is very nice.

Order at the counter then find a seat.

title: Cafe Delight
review_text:
This place in West Kensington offers a truly delightful breakfast experience.

Ordered the traditional English breakfast, and it did not disappoint. Sausage was exceptional, and bacon cooked to perfection. Tangy cooked tomato complemented the runny eggs and black pudding wonderfully.

The cozy, greasy-spoon ambience adds to the charm, with friendly and helpful staff for help and suggestions.

Just a heads-up: they accept cash only. Really satisfied and will definitely be returning!

title: Cafe Delight
review_text: I’ve been coming to this cafe for almost 2 years, it’s amazing. Good quality food and at a really decent price. Lovely service, the staff are great! Perfect stop for breakfast or brunch

title: Cafe Delight
review_text:
What a delight Cafe Delight is.
Went there today with my Dad. Lovely family run cafe with super friendly staff. The breakfast are great. Good ingrediants, good portions and good price.
We will be back next time we are in the area.
Thanks
Stu and Robin xx

title: Cafe Delight
review_text: First and foremost, the staff in here are super friendly and welcoming! This is my go to spot for a full English and even on the days where I just want a coffee, they make the best oat caramel latte I have ever had! Highly recommend this place

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
An outstanding brunch experience!
From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel genuinely welcome with their warm and friendly service. The pancakes were perfectly fresh, the beef burger was bursting with flavour and the coffee was rich and smooth. The Full English Breakfast was cooked to perfection. Every detail was thoughtfully done. Highly recommended!

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
I was walking in the area for quite a while and wanted somewhere to chill, chat and drink hot chocolate. The staff convinced me that he makes the best hot chocolate in bethnal green and I have to admit, it was good 😊
This place has a mixture of items you can buy from hot drinks, salads and mains.
If you're in the area and want to kick back and relax then come to this wonderful place.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
Great cafe-style eatery with a very chill atmosphere.
Route offers a menu with a health-centric POV, including many plant based options.
This restaurant makes for an ideal relaxed bite to eat while catching up with a friend or even to simply have a coffee and pastry.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text: I come here for my morning tea’s and a croissant (currently working locally). The gentleman behind the counter (I’m assuming who is the owner) is very polite and greets everyone, whether they are a regular or not. On my first visit, a homeless man came and asked if he could get something to eat. To his surprise, the gentleman gave him something to eat out of good will. I’m not saying it’s a service they provide, but it was very nice to see people like that are still around. They treat everyone with the upmost respect.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
As we walked passed the cafe, the very fresh and interesting looking salads and cakes caught our eyes. We couldn’t resist buying some. We chose 5 different salads and 3 cakes. They were all so tasty.
I was particularly impressed with their beet root and avocado chocolate cake. It was surprisingly moist.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
Some tasty pancakes, poached eggs and ‘the hangover’ served for breakfast at with a friend. Both were flavoursome. Was expecting a bit more Middle Eastern flare to the pancakes, the flavour was subtle.

The chocolate, beetroot & avocado cake was very good, great balance of flavour without being too sweet or heavy.

Coffee was decent.

Portion was fair and service efficient if not overtly friendly.

Missed a star as I don’t like the trend of only accepting electronic payments.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
Very helpful and knowledgeable on their food I love that it’s all fresh. Every ingredient cater for especially with an intolerance they were very accommodating and able to tell me what was safe and not.

Location was great and food beautifully presented

title: Mess Cafe
review_text:
I've been coming to Mess Café for years and it has been consistently good quality. It gets very busy so you may be expected to share a table. The staff do their best to fit all customers in if they're able to. There are imitations in the area but none come close to Mess Café!

Food served quickly, and it was hot and delicious. Pancakes were especially tasty, I could tell they were freshly made. My husband's burger looked and smelt delicious. Perfect lunch on a chilly Autumn day!

title: Mess Cafe
review_text:
Lovely, friendly staff, I ordered a halal breakfast the turkey bacon was lovely as well as everything else.

Portion size was very generous, warm, welcoming and friendly atmosphere.

Shared tables encourage people to talk to each other somewhat which I found quite nice.

title: Mess Cafe
review_text: A friend recommended Mess Cafe to me, and I can say with certainty that our friendship has been elevated as a result. There is not a single aspect of this establishment I could criticise. The interior is intimate and elegantly cosy, the staff are unfailingly attentive and gracious, and the food is of such quality that it feels almost transportive. The menu is so enticing that we spent a considerable amount of time simply debating our choices, as every dish appeared worthy of selection. With only around twenty five tables in total, advance booking is highly advisable. I fully intend to return and explore more of what this exceptional kitchen has to offer.

title: Mess Cafe
review_text:
Great great restaurant!

The food was top tier and the prices were so good for how much we had! So many options which meant we both were able to order exactly what we wanted eventhough we wanted different food options!

The staff members were nice and helpful and we enjoyed our time there.

title: Mess Cafe
review_text: I was searching for somewhere to have lunch. I popped in for what I thought was a light jacket potato and salad. I was impressed by the quality of the salad and the size of the potatoe for the price. Very clean and friendly. I also had a taste of the grilled chicken my companions had ordered. Quite tasty!

title: Mess Cafe
review_text:
Had a great experience!

We were welcomed as soon as we entered and were quickly seated. It was a Sunday morning but it was almost full, with a lovely atmosphere.

The servers are incredibly kind and patient. I had really enjoyed the French toast ordered alongside the smoothie. The portions were good and filling. Have invited some friends over and hopefully it’s a hit with them.

title: Mess Cafe
review_text: Absolutely delicious food in here, been coming here for a few years now and it never disappoints me. Always go for halal english breakfast where the portion sizes and prices are decent. Tried the chicken salad once and that was tasty too.  Staff are very welcoming. Please dont change your service as I do want to come here more often:)

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: Best breakfast ive had in a long time. Comes with beans but I asked for it without and there is sourdough toast hiding under my perfect eggs. Also seemed to be the only place I saw locally serving pork bacon and sausage. Wife loved her French toast too. Would definitely come regularly if I lived local

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: I was delighted to locate this coffee shop on Google Maps, and I was keen to try their breakfast. I am extremely pleased with their food, which is rare for me to find a dish that is both delicious and clean in the same time. This discovery has been a delightful experience for me, and I will definitely return. I would highly recommend it!

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text:
Favourite place in west London!
Great staff, lovely food, top coffee and top setting in Fulham.
Full English is banging and the new lamb kofta hits the spot for a Sunday night takeaway

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: Lovely breakfast place! My husband said its best full English he can remember having - all cooked perfectly. I had french toast and it was beautiful.

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: Love Walk Cafe has always been one of my go-to lunch spots when I want to treat myself to great food and excellent service. The staff are consistently warm, welcoming, and always attentive to your needs. There’s such a great variety of food to enjoy, making it hard to choose each time. I recently tried their new salmon dish from the menu, and it was incredibly fresh, filling, and absolutely delicious. Compliments to the chef it was a true delight to eat.

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: So far, the best English breakfast I've had in London and love the chai tea too!

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text: We loved this little caff in Fulham . Lovely good value English breakfast and one of the tastiest I’ve had in a while . Will definitely be back .

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text:
The best traditional independent family run cafe in Hackney Central.
Plus were the only cafe in 4 - helpful in providing a charging lead for my phone 🙂🙌❤️

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text:
Affordable and good range of food options. Staff have been friendly. One observation was, they have good rapport with elderly which indicates genuine care and love.

Defo some quality food as I found food non greasy, and standard of coffees and teas (also speciality teas) included in the all day breakfast.

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text: A very pleasant and friendly café, ideal for a breakfast that keeps you full and energized all day. There are options for every taste, and the coffee is really nice.

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text:
I was early for work and couldn't access the building I needed. So I had a quick look for a coffee and a small bite to eat and spotted this little cafe.

It was fairly quiet for a Monday morning, which was a surprise.

I had just a coffee and croissant with cannelloni. Both were fresh and tasted great.

The price was reasonable, considering I would have paid almost twice as much at most mainstream brands for a coffee alone.

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text: Great little cafe in Hackney. Friendly staff and great atmosphere. I had an omelette with chips and salad which was yummy! And they also do 10% off for NHS!

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text: A lovely breakfast spot with a pleasant, friendly and peaceful atmosphere.

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text: Delicious breakfasts this morning, we all had different items (full English, pancakes, omelette) all great and large portions. The coffee and fresh orange juice were perfect. Thank you!

title: Cafe Barca
review_text:
Service was very good, all the staff is very nice and friendly.
Food is delicious, especially the Chicken peri with fries and salad, but you cant go wrong with any of the others plates.
The soup feels homemade and the English Breakfast is quite nice.
Quality and quantity of food is way above
average.
It's great value for money!
Located just a few minutes walk from Hammersmith Tube station and Charing cross Hospital, i can recommend this place to anyone fan of real food.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text:
If I did a one word review "wholesome" or the Portuguese equivalent of that. It's not everyday you go to a cafe which is a cornerstone of the Portuguese community in West London and this is it.
The food is generally really good. I have been there 3 times in the past 3 weeks.
The English breakfast is definitely in the top 5 breakfast in London, great ingredients and portions.
The breakfast panini...is amazing and a good hearty size.
Peri peri chicken is incredible, it's probably going to be my weekend regular amazing.
General go and eat in the back, it's really fun, with CNN Portugal playing on the TV and just general lovely customers which adds to the atmosphere.
The owners and staff are amazing too. So kind.
Honestly... I really like the vibe of this place and the food is really good.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text:
My friend took me here, and I had a lovely homemade chicken soup. I returned the following week and got spaghetti bolognese to take away. It was still hot when I arrived at my destination and tasted very good.
The meat just melted in my mouth. An excellent portion. I will definitely return so I can try more of the menu and update my review. The owner is lovely and welcoming. The prices are excellent!

I didn't see the toilet. Otherwise, I would usually comment on its cleanliness.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text: Very friendly hostess and delicious food 🍲 They even can add something special for your preferences.Clean and peaceful atmosphere.Fantastic Breakfast and Brunches ,big choice,big portions.My favourite lunch -Grill salmon with new potatoes 🥔 and veggies 🥕 .Thank you guys

title: Cafe Barca
review_text: Very good Portuguese food. Friendly staff. Good atmosphere.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text:
A Portuguese cafe was not the most obvious choice for my first English breakfast in London for 5 years, but it was perfect.

A huge plate of good quality ingredients (proper sausages, bacon etc) washed down by an ocean of tea. All for less than a tenner!

Fast and friendly staff too and they clearly have a regular clientele. Deservedly so.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text: Beef stew v nice, larger portion, with rice.

title: Cafe Barca
review_text:
Great Traditional home-made style Food, best Choc Cake ++ The cook just simply knows food, keeping it fresh, tasty and amazing too boot.. keep up the great work 😍x
I have now been back here many times and in all honesty how good can get better is purely down to their Chef who is simply amazing. Their value for flavour tops all. Love it x

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text: This place is amazing! The food was very delicious and the portions are huge!!! We even came back twice 😂. The staff is also so nice and kind ❤️

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text:
What a great little gem of a cafe in Cricklewood!
Have eaten here quite a few times with our family who live nearby.
The staff are always so welcoming, kind & helpful. The menu has a wide variety including vegetarian. Food is great,generous portions & beautifully presented.
Would highly recommend.

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text: Have been coming here for nearly a decade for good reason. The people are always so welcoming and kind, and the food is just as good value as it was when I first started coming here, enormous portions for excellent prices. Best breakfast in London.

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text:
Ive known about this place for a long time but have never gone in, until today! I had the pancakes with egg, bacon & Lincolnshire sausage, and I can say it was amazing 👏
Everything was cooked to perfection 👌 pancakes have a nice crunch thats not burnt, the sausage snapped as you cut into it 😋 even got a little bit of runny egg to dip into

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: My family and I have been eating here for a long while now. There is a vast array of food available and it must be said that everything we've tried has always been top notch, tasty, beautifully plated and always served with a smile. With so many establishments available locally, you have to ask yourself why you always end up coming back. Consistency. You know what you're going to get and that is very comforting. Keep it up!

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: I’m not even exaggerating when I say this - one of the best French Toast dishes I’ve ever had. This place is an absolute sleeper. I’ve been to a ton of specialty places in London and they never get this quite right - absolutely perfect and amazing value considering the sheer amount of fruit on this plate.

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: Food is great, taste, presentation, price! Ticks all the boxes

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: The quality of food and service is very high, busy cafe plenty of seating, staff is great, prices are reasonable - strongly recommend,  very clean cafe with lots of food and drink options

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: The food is always pretty good and reasonably priced I find the family who run it along with the waitress Zair absolutely delightful. Whether it be for a sandwich and cup of tea or a nice Sunday dinner Hamlet is always my local go too.

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text: This such a delightful 😊 cafe great food great service 😀 superb staff 😀 very attentive recommending the menu 😀 professionally the smoothies the full English 😋 breakfast oh the Cumberland sausages 😋 😀 😄 mushroom 🍄 breakfast 😋 is best 👌 but the tbone steak is brilliant 👏 too for lunch or dinner the lamb chops succulent.

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text:
Popped a few weeks ago for a takeout coffee and was impressed with what I saw so went back.

Really friendly welcoming staff, varied menu with great choice and proper good dark strong coffee.

The Hamlet Breakfast was £8.50 including coffee, which in my opinion is good value! Food cooked in open kitchen and came out in less than 10 minutes.

I will definitely be back!

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
We've been here before, but just met with friends for drinks and haven't had food before. This time we did and it was really good, I mean surprisingly good. The ribs were fantastic, I'm sure I haven't had better in the UK.
The salad was good, but rather small. My wife's spicy shrimp were good and so was the salmon. We'll be back.

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
Fantastic team and decor. Almost like a museum, with so many genuine clothes and items owned by pop/rock stars. Excellent music playing whilst you eat, and not too loud either!

Very friendly team, who were always on hand.

Food - served a little suspiciously quickly. Tasted fantastic, but for the price, expected something with more flavour and more "unique-ness", with the burgers. Have eaten many similar burgers, and spent a lot less.

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text: Good service from Marcos, very fast service after ordering. Good atmosphere with some moderately loud music. Very good nachos for starter then some epic burgers had some bone in it though. The deserts looked good but starters and main stuffed is. Extra guacamole is kinda expensive at £5 for a mini pot.

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
Just wanna say we have received the best customer service at this Hard Rock Cafe!

Restaurant was full,but the staff managed to find 2 seats at the bar for us. Loved the welcoming from an Italian staff named Marco.Super friendly!
I was so pleased that he prepared a pot of hot water for me. It was so sweet of him!

I am really not a big fan of western food.But I was so happy that I found 'Asian noodle bowl ' on the menu. It was really good and healthy! I was very impressed!

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text: Booked a table for 3, on a Monday evening (16:30). The place was only half full so no need. Staff were polite enough but with a strong accent, along with music playing in the background, understanding what was being said, deemed quite difficult. A relatively small menu choice, food was mediocre if being honest. I ordered the Tupelo chicken platter for £18.75, this contained about 5 overcooked and dry chicken tenders, with a handful of fries. Certainly not worth the price. Food came out quite quickly after ordering so i suspect this wasn't cooked to order. For dessert, we all ordered the hot fudge brownie at £12.50. Although this initially seemed quite expensive, this could easily feed two people, a large glass containing warm brownie with chocolate sauce, topped with ice cream and squirty cream. The dessert was worth having and recommended. Overall, overpriced average main menu food, good atmosphere, friendly and attentive staff. Would I attend again? Maybe for a quick drink or a dessert but not for a full meal.

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
Came for the One Direction, stayed for atmosphere. Came around 11AM for brunch, ordered burger and nachos. Nachos were very filling and fun to share. Burger tasted good but fries felt lacking.
Good place to stroll around, see iconic pictures and outfits. Good selection of merch too, had some reasonable sales, different styles for anyone

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text:
The location, cleanliness, seating, and toilet facilities of this café are brilliant. No complaints !

The problems with this café stem from there only being one overworked employee.
They do not have time to be friendly with customers because they have several orders they need to fulfil. Prepare for a 10min + wait for coffee. There needs to be someone taking orders and another member of staff preparing
food and making coffees.
The croissants do not taste fresh yet have the same pricing as a bakery. They taste like supermarket croissants. Also, the almond croissants had all been bought by 1130 and there were no replacements.

The regular coffee serving is very small. Yet still priced the same as other places. It was nothing special.

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text:
Nice, warm, and cozy. The food is delicious, with great portions.

We were 4 adults and a baby and went for brunch on a Saturday. The staff were super friendly and welcoming.

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text: I had the pleasure of enjoying an English breakfast here, and it was absolutely delicious. Not only was the food full of flavor, but it was also beautifully presented. The portions were generous and everything tasted fresh and well-prepared. On top of that, the service was outstanding — the staff was very kind, attentive, and welcoming. All in all, a wonderful experience that I would highly recommend!

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text: Delicious food, great service and good atmosphere. We really enjoyed breakfast here. The only thing is when you sit in the middle, there is little bit less space. I recommend this place.

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text: Fantastic and delicious food. Food portion was larger than normal. Great, atmosphere and quick service.

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text:
A cozy café tucked away in the backstreets of Liverpool Street.
The food was lovely, and the staff were incredibly kind and welcoming.
Thank you for giving us such a wonderful memory!

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text: very nice breakfast. Food comes in very large portion ( one liver breakfast is too full for an adult man😆) and the flavor is splendid

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text: A great plate of food -really enjoyed it!

title: Nilly's Cafe
review_text:
Delicious pancake 🥞 with a lot of fruit.
Fresh orange juice & cappucino
I went by myself. The staff was nice and welcoming.
May be have a table for 2 would be a good idea

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: Love this cafe and of course our favourite park. This is our regular spot on a good weather day. The food here is amazing and the menu is great, prices are bit on higher side for a cafe but these days its the case everywhere in London. Menu is more like a restaurant menu and so are the prices. Our bill was 50ish quid for a coffee and 3 food dishes (2 adults and one child) felt a bit too much. Cafe is always super busy hence staff is not that attentive. Although really would like the staff to be a little more kind and helpful and smiling faces. As a customer it doesn’t feel that welcoming by the staff. We still go there every single time as no park visit is complete for us without cafe food. Food and coffee on point. I always recommend people to visit here and have brought all my friends multiple times. Thank you.

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text:
The most lovely family-friendly park cafe. I’ll tell you it’s the ‘better’ park of the two in the big Hampstead Heath area of a park.

I like how well maintained the surroundings are and the abundance of benches/seating area.

Mostly the homemade sorbet and gelato looked really good. I tried the lemon sorbet, it’s very sweet and stretchy. Not too badly portioned for the price (£3.95 for one scoop).

I recommend for a good time :) ideal for families who want to have a space to sit while their kids run around or people who want a scenic spot to eat, catch up and enjoy scenery.

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: I would rate this establishment four stars rather than five due to its excessive pricing. However, I must commend the Cheese and Tomato Panini, which was exceptionally delicious, and the Iced Matcha Latte, which was quite satisfactory

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: Brilliant as ever! Delicious aubergine parmigiana and coffee. Just such a reliable, welcoming and perfect place. Thank you!

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text:
So happy with this cafe yet again- even on a dull drizzly day its a great place to go and the staff were very helpful.So sorry its lost the competitive tender.The successors will have a lot to live up to and I suspect  it will end up being more expensive or the food quality will go down.
Is competitive tendering really necessary when the current business is doing so well?We should also ask are the criteria correct for the competitive tender.Its easy for an incomer to say it can do better but the proof is in the pudding( or the lasagne!)

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text:
What a great place! Located just opposite Liverpool street station, I was greeted by several different members of the staff. Escorted to my table to be seated. Then I noticed just how cool the interior decorating was! 10/10 for the designer!!
My waitress was lovely! I ordered a Bacon sandwich and a tea. The sandwich was awesome and they surprised me with a pot of tea so I managed to get 2 cups of tea out of the pot😁All for under £8 in  central London!! Excellent value.
When venturing upstairs to the WC I realized this isn’t a small place ( as it appears) It’s like a Tardis! 3 floors of seating I did not expect. Don’t waste your money on the station meal deals. Go and try this place. You won’t regret it 😁

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text: Food was good and gf/df options available upon request. Service with a smile from our server, didnt get her name, but very pleasant. Price on par with area. The letdown in this place was the toilets, it was done in an old Victorian type way which would have been good but underneath the hand dryer is filthy, really dirty so it really detracted from the open pipe work and butler sinks

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text:
This was our very first british breakfast right after my girlfriend and I arrived in London. The place is typical and cozy, with a calm and welcoming atmosphere.
I had the traditional English breakfast, while my girlfriend chose pancakes and coffee. The food was very good and the portions were generous.
The owner was kind, friendly, and made us feel completely at ease.
Absolutely recommended, we will definitely come back next time we’re in London!

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text: We ordered a full English breakfast and pancakes with fruit. This English breakfast was exactly what I was looking for, the pancakes were also nice but not really warm. We were expecting them to be warm, maybe with all the cream and other toppings they already cool down before being served?

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text: We went to this place for brunch and there are several things to consider. The mac and cheese was too flavorful; the entire plate was impossible to finish. As for the English breakfast, the beans were good but cold; everything else was good, including the fries.

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text: We were with our guests from Poland on Sunday. As always, tasty and atmospheric, moderate prices. Apart from the traditional breakfast, I recommend the roll from my photo with jalapeno. See you next time..👌👍

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text:
Its situated inside the V&A museum which is free.
The cafe has three halls with lots of tables. One in the middle where the big ball lighting and piano is situated in and one on each side. Each room has a different feel and look and the athmosphere is amazing. There is plenty of pastry and hot drinks menu options, and the coffee is really good! The scones were really fresh and is served with usual jam and cream.

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text: Exquisite decoration. Nice tea. Lovely to make a stop while wondering around the museum. It is self service, that is the only downside. Highly recommended. Look up the ceiling to admire the chandeliers

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text:
Dating back to the 1860s, created as part of the museum’s mission to make art and design accessible to the public.

One of London’s most distinctive museum dining spaces — in fact, it’s considered the oldest museum café in the world, combining Victorian design heritage with a modern, varied food offering, It has fantastic decoration and particularly the stain glass windows.
The cakes are really good the coffee less so as it was not really very hot. Staff very efficient

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text: Fabulous surroundings to have a bite to eat it just coffee and cake. Several places to get served, can get busy at lunchtime. If you just want a coffee it is quicker to get served outside in the garden. Excellent choice of cakes on offer. Recommended.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text: Lovely relaxing atmosphere overlooking the lovely views of the park and beyond. Great portions sizes for the jacket potatoes and fillings. Lovely hot chocolate. Good service. Staff were polite. Clean, spacious area and free toilets. Would definitely recommend. Child and accessible friendly.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text: 😡£3.20 for a disgusting single espresso: when you charge that price you are NOT allowed to serve low cost poor quality coffee. You could justify it by saying there is table service but the customer has to do the job themselves and order at the counter. So the 3.20 is not justified by any account!!!!  You can’t say it’s because the cost is high: this is low cost badly roasted shady blend you are using. The place is nice, very nice actually but you shamelessly ruin the experience by blatantly not showing any sense of respect for the customer. The agency staff is friendly.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text:
I love this place for a relaxing cuppa and sit down and look out over the park with the occasional bite to eat. Today I was shocked to find an obnoxious lady having a go at an old man who dared tell her that her phone was too loud. It was too loud, she was shouting down the phone and the phone was on loud speaker, you can talk into a phone and not shout at it whilst it sits on the table. She did not like the fact he asked her to use headphones and tone it down. She said no one else complained, well he did and that is what he got, undeserved abuse. He then picked up his cup and took it outside and still she had to have a go (let it go, you are an adult, not 6) she said "is the music too loud? , are the people speaking too loud?".

This is why people did not tell you, because you would verbally attack them for trying to be nice.

The staff member behind the till did come and have a word to their credit but the lady's behaviour was unacceptable. She still kept on about it, dragging in other people who could not wait to get away from the cafe.

It was too loud and should have been brought to her attention by a staff member as they lovely man will probably never come back and not will I .

Great service here, good food and a beautiful place to spend an hour or two, as long as one does not have to put up with the rin, tin, tin of din from a loud speaker phone.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text:
Good to see that this café has not lost its chilled vibe and reasonable prices after it's revamp.The veggie breakfast is my GoTo with a mug of tea for around a tenner.

Nice comfy chairs, great views through the big windows and there's an area set aside for people with dogs. All facilities on ground level so very accessible.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text: So disappointed in what’s happening in the park cafe. I love doing work here occasionally and treating myself to a coffee and snack but I am beyond shocked they charge near £10 for a tuna melt that looks like this. I assumed this would have been on sourdough and had some salad or fun things to justify the price but literally it’s the cheapest ciabatta with some tuna and cheddar. Criminal and unethical, considering where we are having such extortionate prices!

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text: The place has changed a lot in recent years. And for the better

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Hipster cafe with the usual tribe present. Food was decent. Coffees are nice. Atmosphere is generic. Busy place though. Could do with more seating. Kind of cold outside. People broke out into carol singing whilst we were there.

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text:
Hands down the best coffee spot in east London. Also a fabulous spot for an aperol spritz with great food on a sunny summer evening.
The best little escape spot with what I can only describe as the best chocolate croissant I’ve ever had the pleasure of tasting.

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Very good range of food (and healthy), and perfect coffee! I really like the staff. In the evening, their selection of cured meat and cheese with wine have been my go-to with my work friends. Also price is very reasonable.

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Wilton Way Cafe is our chilled-out weekend spot. My partner and I are regulars here, mainly for the epic pancetta and avo baps – they're just the right mix of tasty and comforting. The pastries are a hit too, perfect with a coffee. What we really love is the laid-back, neighbourhood feel. It's always full of locals, dogs, and kids, which adds a nice, homely touch. Great place for a relaxed bite and to feel part of the community.

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Lovely vibe  music played at a gentle volume,  a fantastic play list. Coffee tea & pastries are always quality, with    staff that remember your name. The owner,  the lady running the show ,should win something to say ,best deli ,Coffee shop in North East London@♡&♡

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Very tasty food+ coffee, and friendly service. The decor could use a bit of TLC though - very small cafe so all the rough edges are very noticeable.

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text: Always lovely staff, great food. V pleased it’s my local! :)

title: Wilton Way Cafe
review_text:
I’ve been a local customer at Wilton Way Café for over two years, visiting a couple of times a week for convenience. The lunch options can be quite good on occasion, though consistency is sometimes lacking, and I’ve generally found the pastries to be underwhelming.

Earlier this month, during a particularly hot day (around 35°C), I visited for coffee and asked for some tap water with ice. I was told I could help myself to water from the Wilton Way Deli, but the water there was sitting in direct sunlight and was far too warm to drink. When I requested ice, I was told it would only be available if I purchased bottled water, which I did for £1.80 in order to receive ice. The explanation was that the café spends £60 a day on ice and therefore cannot offer it freely. Given the café’s pricing (over £4 for an iced coffee), I found this response disappointing, especially as a long-standing customer.

Additionally, the iced coffee is often pre-prepared and lacks flavour. I’ve politely requested freshly made espresso in my iced Americano on several occasions, but the staff have consistently been unwilling or unable to accommodate this simple request, even when clearly communicated.

While I’ve appreciated the café’s presence in the area, these experiences have made it difficult to justify returning. I hope feedback like this encourages a more thoughtful approach to customer service in the future.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text: Scrumptious vegetarian and vegan community restaurant. You go to their website and phone the local neighbour who is cooking this evening to book in. All costs go to maintaining the community space. It's a super wholesome place. The neighbouring houses who live on the street got a lottery grant in the 90's and all co-own the building.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text:
Coming here was like being able to eat the food you might spend all day making yourself for your friends with love and care.

Originally set up to feed the local community, one can feel these good vibes still.

The space is beautifully light in a stunning little cul de sac with pleasant trees and gardens nearby, just two minutes from a very busy thoroughfare. Wonderfully quiet and peaceful.

One got the sense coming here for the first time that everyone living local comes here to meet up and relax and eat truly great vegetarian food prepared with care and love.

Exceptionally good service and atmosphere, with some great music playing, this is somewhere I will be coming back to time and again.

This restaurant if fine with your bringing your own alcoholic drinks.

It appears from day to day the menu could change but the food will always be great.

Today had tacos and nachos and as you can see from the photos they were all superb.

I'm so happy to have found this place and hope you visit too.

Thank you so much for a great evening.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text: The Syrian food on Fridays is next level I will be bringing all my friends!
