title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text:
Great little cafe in a quiet part of vauxhall. Each night has a different chef, but food is always vegetarian or vegan. Have been twice, both times Chef Jen was in the kitchen. She made everything from scratch, including the delicious ice cream, and the result was yum.
It's not a Michelin star restaurant but a fantastic neighbourhood  cafe that serves real  food with lots of love. The outside garden is a wonderful place to sit and eat amongst the leafy trees.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text:
Delicious home style cooking very generous portions matched with reasonable prices. You can't go wrong here, and as a vegan this is perfect. Mostly everything is vegan for your pleasure.

We arrived late with friends 10 mins before closing but we were welcomed by Arfa who accommodated 7 of us. Everything we had was very tasty and fresh. Thank you Arfa and family.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text:
Arfa's food was absolutely amazing, I'm still dreaming about it, and she was so lovely to us! She's there every Wednesday.
Such a wholesome dinner and a lovely relaxed atmosphere.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: Horrendous pricing - £17 for a bit of cauliflower, greens and carrots. They should be totally ashamed of themselves for thinking that this is acceptable.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: We have frequented this cafe many times over the years. Though in the last 2 years less so. The menu is repetitive and sometimes not to appetising. On 18th Aug we tried again. The chips looked burnt (though were told they were fresh) very hard and crispy. The fish itself was beautiful, though the batter very heavy and soggy. I don't think we'll visit again for a long time.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: I went for a salad, maybe not the best option, and it was expensive - but this is central London. Hard to beat the atmosphere.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text:
Abysmal counter service where people had no clue what they were meant to be doing. You have a concert taking place in the church just above and you have no food??!! Seriously??? That place went downhill in the past few years.
A friend and I shared a very sad half of an excuse for a pizza and then went over to Coop to get some sandwiches. I will not be coming back. Definitely not for food! Best to be avoided.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: Limited food options for me due to wheat allergy but was able to get hot chicken with nicely cooked vegetables.  No waiter service.  Reasonably priced wine.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: Excellent singer called Sam Wray singing here yesterday while ypu sit on deck chairs sipping a beer. Why he's not the next Ed Sheeran I don't know. Such talent.

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: Me and misses had a good time. Very relaxed and peaceful. 😀 will be visiting again when local.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: Amazing food! I could eat here everyday. Really great spot for us as we had lots of suitcases and a small pooch. Large dining area and great selection of food.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: Wonderful food, gorgeously spiced and complex flavours instead of just burning with chills. The lentils and veg in the thali were filling and nourishing without being heavy, and the vegetables were perfectly cooked. The staff were very pleasant and polite as well, and the prices were very reasonable - for a quick, healthy, tasty lunch in London, I don’t think I could do any better! And the luxury as a vegetarian of knowing I could have anything on the menu and just order the thali and eat whatever came without stressing about ingredients was so nice.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text:
Very affordable and quiet. All vegan.
Cafeteria style in a metal tray, the mango lassi was fantastic as well

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: Yummy! I had home made lentil soup - just what I needed on a cold rainy day in London. Very reasonable prices.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text:
Cozy cafeteria like spot with a simple and delicious vegan Indian menu. I really enjoyed my vegan lassi and the dosa wrap. It’s admittedly not the typical Indian flavor profile - it’s more of its own Indian inspired thing, but it’s yummy in its own right with flavor dahl and pakora. I also really liked my vegan chai!

Most importantly, however, the vibes were exceptional at thenga, which had the most kind staff. I came as a solo diner and felt so comfortable and welcome.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: Loved it. Casual atmosphere with incredible flavoursome food. I tried the dosa wrap and it was so delicious. I would love to come back and try the roti wrap and all the other items on the menu. Staff are very kind and welcoming - service was efficient and I can tell the food is made with care. The nice high ceiling makes it feel very pleasant to sit and enjoy. I wish I had tried the juices as the combinations all sound very good. They are only open during the day which is a shame but well worth it if you're able to.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: lowkey, friendly atmosphere serving a downright scrumptious meal. the stuffed paratha is my favorite - delicate yet hearty. also the women’s bathroom is a purple and blue portal, such a vibey space in an otherwise very straightforwardly decorated location. love to bring a friend and play a hand of cards, eat great food at a fair price, and soak up the peaceful joy of the people around me. 10/10

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text: I'm having breakfast right now: a latte with lemon  cake... so delicious! The atmosphere is incredible, it's a very nice place... a great combination of plants and coffee ❤️

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text:
Highly recommend if you’re going for a coffee/cake as the atmosphere is lovely but for food we’ll never go back.

It took over an hour for our brunch to get served from when we ordered it - we ordered ciabatta toasties and a salad so it’s not like that takes a long time to make. We felt like we had to chase for when our food would arrive as we were told it would be a 30 minute wait (told after we paid), they also served one of us the wrong item.

Food was okay, fair bit overpriced, such a shame as the place is wonderful and we love walking around there, but for food we’ll never come back.

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text: Hidden gem! The place is great and the atmosphere is wonderful. We ordered the avocado smash, one with poached egg and one with feta cheese. The food was good, although both drinks were served cold. The staff were nice, but unfortunately, the feta cheese dish had a hair in the toast. When I addressed this with the waiter, she just apologised and took the plate away with no further action. I then spoke to another waitress who got us a new dish, but overall, they were not professional in handling the situation.

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text: Hidden beauty in the neighborhood. We haven't been here for brunch or lunch yet, but there is a very pleasant seating area in a magnificent nursery. We sometimes come here to sip our coffee. We intend to come for brunch as soon as possible. The atmosphere is great. You can also buy some kinds of accessories and plants for your garden. There are truly very beautiful pieces.

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text: service has been awful...we arrived and the waitress told us to sit anywhere and she would clean the table for us... we finished doing it ourselves. Since we ordered at the counter it has taken them 40 mins to get our order of a cheesecake and a tea to the table, sucks

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text:
Pros: this cafe has many repeat customers, so there is a bit of a homely feel when you enter. Staff are generally pleasant and the food is tasty and at a manageable price point. Lots of seating is available inside and the cafe does a good job of looking after elderly customers and people with disabilities.

Cons: In the last few months the cafe has had a change of ownership so a lot of the menu has changed and portion sizes have decreased. Personally I would’ve preferred if the prices were higher and the portion sizes stayed the same because now it feels like I’m overpaying.

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text:
Classic British Cafe, with a nice smiling team, that seemed they like what they were doing, interacting with customers which I love at this places where everyone looks like knows everyone.

Food was good, you can’t go wrong with English breakfast in this kind of cafe’s as they are always on demand and there will always be fresh stock bought.

Worth the try if you are around and hungry!
Thank you!

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text: Brilliant little cafe in the heart of Peckham. I wanted a genuine breakfast for my birthday, couldn't have asked for a better atmosphere for a hearty meal. Breakfast was great, cooked to perfection, and the tea was delicious. Not a bad price too!

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text: Really nice staff but serving time was awful, waited 45mins for a fry up - order was wrong and many people were served before me even though they ordered after

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text: Today I went to this cafe which I always go to I was so disappointed I asked for scrambled eggs sausage bacon and beans I never had the worst breakfast from here my bacon was under cooked the eggs just looked like they were fried first and just smashed together to look like scrambled egg the I could tell because the whites of the eggs could still be seen the beans tasted really cheap the sausage tasted like rubber and what saddens me is the sausages used to be the best I don’t what’s happened to this cafe used to be such a nice place to eat the quality of the food has definitely gone downhill I’m so disappointed And they are a bit pricey for the quality of food at this point

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text: The prices have all been updated but the business hasn't said anything. I went inside hoping to get a set 1 breakfast for £6 only to find out it's been increased to £9. That's false advertising. If you have changed the price u should say something.

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text:
Literally munching on my bacon and egg roll as we speak. Beautiful bread, nice quality bacon, nice deep yolk on the egg and a beautiful beautiful latté. Made my morning.

Will be seeing you again soon, definitely.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text:
Disappointed is an understatement. I had eaten at this cafe several times pre-pandemic. I was looking forward to getting their chia waffles again. Well, today, at 12.15pm on a Saturday, turns out that many items on their menu were not available: no waffles, only sweet pancakes, no soup of the day, no sourdough bread, and I overheard the waitress say they were out of caramel syrup too. Pastries look dry, display very sparse. No kids menu. The only high chair there was absolutely filthy. After using 5+ baby wipes on it I went to ask for some help cleaning it. The waitress took a while to come, by then our child was ravenous so we put him in the chair anyway and gave him food we had in our bag. No apologies, just "It's busy" but the chair clearly hadn't been properly cleaned in days, if not weeks. Our tables (and the ones around) were dirty too, even though the place was almost empty when we arrived. No menus, no table service. We had to take photos of the menu as the only one available a big board behind the counter, not visible at all from where we were sitting. We had to wait about 25 mins after ordering to get our lunch. The place was not even half full, and we ordered a basic vegan breakfast and a veggie one. Food was tasty but quite plain and didn't seem particularly healthy/special. No one came to check on us. Our table wasn't even cleared until we were putting our coats on to leave. Lots of disused cooking equipment close to where we ate (proper safety hazard!!) Bathroom old and a bit dirty. Barely any soap left. Our child was completely ignored by the staff.

Upon googling what happened, it seems clear that the new owners do not know how to maintain this place. Overpriced as a result. We will not be back.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text: Leafwild Cafe is a cozy and inviting spot with a calm, relaxing atmosphere and a strong focus on quality ingredients. The menu offers a nice variety of fresh dishes, and the coffee is rich and well-prepared. Their cakes and pastries are flavorful and feel homemade, making them perfect for a mid-day treat. The service is friendly and unhurried, allowing you to really enjoy your time there. Whether you're stopping in for a light brunch, a peaceful coffee break, or a casual meet-up, Leafwild delivers a pleasant and satisfying café experience.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text: It's a nice, clean place with good coffee. It looks smaller from the outside, but there's quite a lot of space inside. It was very quiet because we went after the market closed.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text:
I came here with a friend because we had seen the menu on their website and liked the options. We were told that the menu on their board is the current menu to order from (which was different to their online menu - first disappointment). I was looking forward to having the chia seed vegan savoury waffles and was told they dont have any waffles or pancakes at the moment (second disappointed!). I then went to order their vegan breakfast and was looking forward to trying the shakshuka sauce which was stated on the menu. My order arrives and I get given 2 hash browns to replace the shakshuka sauce which was again really disappointing. Noone had advised us of this change.

I do really like the ambience of this cafe and I think they have a lot of variety on their menu, however, their menu and communication feels a little disorganised.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text: Really lovely brunch — the eggs were perfect and the coffee was great. The staff were super friendly and attentive too. Definitely coming back ☕💛

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text: Delicious breakfast and a perfect iced latte to start the day! The toast was fresh and tasty – simple, but just what I needed. Cozy place and lovely vibes! Super friendly and polite staff – really made me feel welcome.

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text: Leafwilde Café has quickly become my go‑to spot in Notting Hill. The space is calm, beautifully designed, and perfect for focused work or quick founder catch‑ups. The coffee is excellent, the staff is genuinely warm  and the atmosphere strikes that rare balance between energising and grounding. It’s the kind of place that quietly elevates your day.

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text: An absolute delight of a cat cafe!! After months of walking past, I decided I needed to pop in to see the fluffy babies - and I’m so glad I did! The staff were very welcoming and thorough with their explanations of the house rules and what to expect from the cats. The cats themselves were very sweet and relaxed (which speaks a lot about their treatment!), and so easy to chill out with. Food and drink was delicious also! Highly recommend and will be coming back ☺️

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text: Incredible experience at Whiskers & Cream! Despite it being the longest-running London cat café, I somehow hadn’t heard about it until recently! Jasmine and the other member of staff were super nice and welcoming, and care deeply about the cats and their welfare. The cats were by far the most well-groomed, relaxed and happy that I’ve ever encountered at a cat café! The main coons alone, Jasper and Jack, are a treat to behold! The kittens of the group, Basil and Sage, are also hilarious and love to play! I highly recommend booking a study session as it’s the best value for money and you get to see the cats in all their phases/moods. Also, the place was incredibly clean and the hot chocolate is very rich and yummy!

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text:
I had so much fun here!

When I arrived the staff were very polite and welcoming but clearly very busy working as baristas and serving staff and cooks and keeping an eye on the kitties. I was seated pretty quickly despite this and my high tea arrived soon after.

Good portion size for high tea and wonderful variety of food. The sandwiches were delicious although one did fall apart the instant I touched it because one of the slices of bread was in two pieces. I was a little disappointed that my drink, a biscoff latte, wasn’t fully included in the price of my high tea (added 45p to my bill) but I suppose it was a premium drink. My drink did take a little while to come out (5-10 minute wait) though I didn’t mind too much since it gave me time to look around and say hello to the kitties. The cinnamon paw print on top my drink was adorable.

An hour and a half slot felt like the perfect amount of time to eat my high tea, drink my drink, and take pictures. Jack, one of the Maine coons frequented my table looking for pets and wanting to sample my scones (which I regretfully had to inform him were sadly not for him to enjoy). The other 9 kitties while visible and permitted themselves to be pet were asleep or kept to themselves the entire time I was there.

As for the shop itself the atmosphere was very calm and quiet. Definitely catered best to smaller parties (2-3 guests per group). While a calm older child content with letting the cats occasionally approach them or simply being seated near a sleeping cat would have a good time (and it seemed like the two children who were there while I was there and fit the above description seemed to be enjoying themselves) Whiskers and Cream is not the place for children who want to run around playing with cats constantly, or always have their hands on a cat. The cats here (with the exception of Jack and his favorite fish toy who put on quite the display and got the zoomies while I was there) seemed to prefer to lounge around, watch the street outside through the window, or sleep.

After my meal I took lots of pictures, gave each kitty another snuggle, and talked with the staff. I asked about how they handle allergens in their kitchen since I wanted to bring my mom here but she has some food sensitivities. The staff member I asked was very understanding and told me they separate their food as much as possible (separate storage, separate prep stations, etc) but that they don’t make everything in house or guarantee that no cross contamination happens. Their menu had a lot of options to choose from for a variety of dietary needs (gluten free, dairy free, pescatarian, vegetarian to name a few).

Before leaving I explored their downstairs (I could see why no one was seated there today since no cats were downstairs and I think that was where the liter boxes were too). The human restroom was clean and well stocked and the portraits of the kitties on the pillar downstairs were a nice touch.

Overall paid £32 for a premium latte, 3 sandwiches, 2 scones with jam and butter, 2 cake cubes, a macaron, some sorbet, a mini cheesecake, and 1.5 hours spent with cats. Left feeling full and satisfied.

As pricy as it was having high tea at Whiskers and Cream (as opposed to just coming in and having a coffee or getting a little snack) was worth it. In addition to being a fun way to spend an afternoon the food tasted amazing (and earned me a lot of attention from Jack the Maine coon, something I was told most patrons who don’t purchase high tea don’t receive). The drink  was a good size and tasted great too. I made so many wonderful memories!

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text: Furry Therapy- Wonderful Kitty’s, so well looked after and happy to hang out with strange humans oooh’ing and awwing at them. Staff were clearly passionate about this place of work a definite passion project. Loved the vibe and the oat latte wasn’t bad either! Go share in the love of the most beautiful kitty cats in Holloway x

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text:
Absolutely amazing!
My daughter asked to go here for her birthday - we are cat owners and love what Whispers & Cream stand for.
The staff where so accommodating and after a telephone call there was space for me, my daughter and her 3 siblings.

We arrived so excited and left feeling so incredibly happy.
The cats are so well looked after, and very loved, this is shown in their temperament.
The food and drinks where delicious too.

Thank you so much for accommodating us, and making my daughters birthday one to remember.
We will be back very soon♡

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text:
This place specializes in Mediterranean cuisine — refined but not flashy. Every dish is super substantial, made with quality ingredients. After eating, I felt incredibly healthy, like even my skin was glowing!

Plus, they offer a lunch set: a main dish + two kinds of salad as a meal deal, all for just a little over a tenner — feels like a great deal!

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text: This place is honestly so lovely , the food options, the amazing coffee. I loved it all. They also have outdoor seating. All staff are always so kind. Melissa makes me feel so welcomed. Her smile was lovely and she makes my experience 10 times better every time and this is why I will continue to come back. Definitely recommend this cafe!!

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text: Really like the vibeh here, it feels so chill and perfect for a casual hangout with friends. I’d recommend the Parmesan Chicken and the Baked Salmon. The salmon has a sweet chili flavor with great seasoning, and the fish tastes really fresh. The grilled chicken skewers are super tender, juicy, and flavorful.

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text:
Absolutely loved this spot! 💛 Perfect for a hot summer day in London — the menu is super refreshing and full of flavour. I tried the Chicken Skewer with Peach (£14) and was pleasantly surprised — the peach starter was actually fresh peach, not a gimmick! Juicy, tangy and paired perfectly with the chicken. Super satisfying.

The Meatball Skewer with Roasted Sweet Potato (£14) was also great — the meatballs were decent, but the roasted sweet potato totally stole the show. Soft, cool and naturally sweet — not your usual fries. I ended up stealing the last few bites from my friend 😆

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text: This restaurant has such delicious and creative salads – every dish is full of flavour and really well balanced. 🥗✨ The community vibe here is also wonderful, very warm and welcoming. Thanks so much for the lovely hospitality – I’ll definitely come back again!

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text:
Chill vibe, friendly staff, cozy wooden tables. Good spot to slow down for a bit.

I ordered the turmeric latte which just out of curiosity. It came out looking like a cup of sunshine, golden, warm, kinda mysterious. I weirdly liked the experience. Haha)

Also had the pulled lamb with barley salad, the lamb was super tender and flavorful, and the salad is way more taste than I expected. The plates looked beautiful too.

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text:
I stopped by for a late breakfast and was pleasantly surprised that they served it at noon. The cafe looked different than the photos and there was no name on the outside. Just follow the map and you’ll find it.

There is more seating in the back for groups of 4-5. The staff were friendly and brought the food out to me. The breakfast was simple but delicious and included a hot beverage. There was a lunch rush so it seems like a popular place. Would come here again when in the area.

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text:
Food was alright. Bacon and egg sandwich wasn’t very good, but the full English was okay for my first ever. Coffees really weren’t all that great

Friendly people, though, and the price is reasonable

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text: Does what it says on the tin excellent quality tea and sandwich’s gem just off Oxford Circus  no Clowns here just brilliant service and staff very friend great little pit stop for Breakfast or just a snack.  ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text: Really enjoy our breakfast here, so tasty. Very homely food👌😋... Toast is amazing.. Will be back!!!

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text: Small cafe in the one of the corners of the city. Quick service and friendly staff. You can eat in or just take away whatever you like.

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text:
I stumbled upon this gem and was blown away by the great atmosphere, friendly staff, and top-notch coffee. The cappuccino was rich and smooth, and the pastries were fresh and delicious — especially the Turkish Breakfast! You can tell they put real care into everything they serve.

The interior is tastefully decorated, perfect for both working or catching up with friends. They also have a lovely outdoor seating area that’s ideal on a sunny day.

Highly recommend if you’re in the area and looking for a relaxing, high-quality café experience. Can’t wait to go back!

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text: Cosy cute place ! Had a lovely breakfast . Service staff deserve 5 star 🌟

title: Mustard Cafe
review_text: Great fresh food, great prices. Friendly helpful and fast service. Thanks

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: Lovely nook in the corner of south end green. Prime people watching spot & a fabulous owner. We had a flat white & chicken sandwich, which were both very good. Family owned and characterful business, which you don’t need a mortgage to eat at. Will be back. Thank you ❤️

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: Walked into this cafe with a friend looking to have a snack & coffee. The man working in the shop refused to serve us because he did not like how we entered the cafe and sat down. He could not in any shape or form explain to us what we had done wrong in coming into the cafe and sitting down, he just ignorantly claimed we did something and refused to serve us. I’m assuming this man owns or manages the cafe- he provided miserable service and treatment and id clearly not a nice person. Avoid entirely

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text:
Was recommended this place by a barmaid in a local pub which didn't have soup on their menu. It was very cold outside and I wanted a heartwarming soup.
Went in and was warmly greeted by the owner Milko. We struck up an engaging and lively conversation.
I ordered the chicken and vegetable soup.
The soup was superb, and a very generous serving at that.
The bowl was huge and it took a while for me to finish eating. 10/10
Finished with a Macchiato which was very good.
Thank you Milko for such a great experience.
I will be back very soon!

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: Such a friendly cafe where the owner (Milko) knows all his regulars by name. There's a real sense of community at this place. Good healthy food as well, Milko bakes his own bread, makes his own juice. Small and cosy feel to it as the name would suggest.

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: Very nice coffee at a reasonable price, the food is also very good. The staff are excellent, respectful, helpful and friendly, always a pleasure to get a coffee there. The cafe is rather small at busy times it could be difficult to get a seat. There is some seating outside, there is a hatch in the window for take away orders or sit outside orders. Highly recommended.

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: The place caught my eye while i was passing by. I gave it a try and this place is the best. The coffee, the Sandwich all was great. The price for the area is cheap. They will definetly see me few times a week.

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text: Delicious Vegan salads, Vegetarian quiche and fish and chips food.  Excellent roof top view.  Very Good selection of Coffee and Cakes.

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text: I really love JL cafe.. especially their lunch! I love their fish and chips. Very fresh and crunchy.. their cakes are freshly baked and so delicious. Just their staff sometimes are very intimidating. They don't greet or say good morning with a smile...makes me wonder they had bad day in mid morning..

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text:
I would have to say this is a fantastic store.

The staff are brilliant and very helpful.

Disabled access is great too

The cafe is reasonably priced all in all a great example of British at it's best.

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text:
I popped to the ‘Place to Eat’ after finishing my shopping. As I queued, a courteous member of staff, offered to support with carrying my tray. (Very observant to notice my walking stick).
Excellent customer service!

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text:
A masterclass in mediocrity.

The chicken thigh arrived cold, as if seasoning and heat were optional upgrades no one bothered to mention.

Flavor? Absent.
Reheating? Not even up for discussion.

Service was a performance art piece in inefficiency, one person working while three others observed the growing line like it was an exhibit at a museum,

occasionally making eye contact just to heighten the awkwardness.

As for the cherry drink, it was a triumph of syrup over substance, overwhelmingly sweet and mostly ice, as if hydration were a secondary concern. A truly unforgettable experience, though not for the right reasons.

Could be amazing with better oversight.

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text:
Very helpful friendly staff,
Good selection of food either Breakfast or lunch lovely tea in pot, lovely latte
You can always reheat as a microwave available. A good break wile you shopping in Oxford street

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text: Went to have a Sunday morning English Breakfast at John Lewis only to discover that this restaurant no longer serves up an English breakfast on a Sunday morning! Only poncey food. I’m sure it’s nice but no not on a Sunday morning in London England. Try going to Tokyo’s big Dept store restaurant and not getting a Ramen. Not good. There’s no picture because I couldn’t stay.

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text: SCRUMPTIOUS. JUST ABSOLUTELY DIVINE. I had the lotus biscoff cake and it was quite possibly one of the most delicious things I've ever eaten. Such a generous portion size too! I wasn't able to finish it all but was kindly given a box to take the rest to go. And it was vegan????? WHAT'S MORE LOVE!!!!

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text:
Beautiful cafe to work from. I had the clementine and chocolate cake and it was beautiful, my friend had the pumpkin one and it tasted amazing.
Coffee was also really good!

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text: Very good tea and coffee, which we went to a nearby park to drink. They also sell a variety of pastries and other baked goods - the sausage roll was fantastic. There was a friendly atmosphere, and lots of options for people with allergies.

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text:
I'm so glad I stumbled upon Lumberjack Cafe during my walk. This coffee shop offers not only excellent coffee but also a selection of baked goods, skincare products, wine, and groceries.

The atmosphere is fantastic, with plenty of seating options, including cozy wooden tables downstairs and a charming garden area.
The latte I had was smooth, flavorful, and thoroughly enjoyable. The staff was welcoming and friendly, making the experience even better.

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text: Cute coffee shop serves Assembly coffee, nice pastries, also some nice lunch items. I was in the area so stopped off for a latte a pain au chocolate they also sell a few grocery and gift items.

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text: A great place to work and catch up with friends with a great coffee. I love the atmosphere and the team, they are always friendly and nice. We've been buying coffee beans from them for 2 years and they have the best choice in the area. I will be moving to another area soon and this place I will miss a lot!

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text:
Fantastic coffee (best flat white for miles )and overall a real hidden gem  (especially with the outdoor seating) 💎
Fantastic quality coffee and lovely chatty baristas and staff team! Bike and vegetarian friendly! Can’t wait to come back
Love the selection of locally sourced and high quality products everything from wine to chocolate!

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text:
Nothing special, just a cafe inside a church! Pretty good latte and carrot cake and very friendly staff here the day I went. The atmosphere would be even better without the kids’ playpen and with a little better music but still worth coming to for the novelty.
Supposedly it gets busy with moms and kids (for said playpen) but it was nice and quiet when I went.

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text: Nice cafe in the church with plenty of seating. They have many different snacks, food options and milk alternatives.

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text:
Niiice
Church , coffee , booze .

There’s a kids playground so it is a perfect place for those with children .

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text:
Great time here with my son and friends. Great place to catch up 😁
If you have older children, they have a large soft play area. They have high chairs for feeding and toilet facilities.

Only negative is that they dont have hot food, only snacks but they did say they are working on this so fingers crossed. Definitely recommend !

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text: This cafe is excellent. The cake is always *delicious*, and there is a wide range of hot and cold drinks and sandwiches. Lots of seating, both at tables and on sofas, and you will always find somewhere to sit. Great wifi makes this a good place to work if you don't mind kids running around/music playing in the cafe. It really is a sanctuary - and extra lovely if you can bag a seat next to one of the big heaters! Thank you St James Church for offering the use of your beautiful space.

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text:
A multi-functional place located inside a church, a great place to go if you have small children as there is a slide / ball area (extra cost).  Nice coffees and snacks (mainly cakes).  There is also a small book lending  library.

The local post office is also located at this site.

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text: Great place to go with kids. Can have a coffee and have them enjoy the play area (not without direct supervision though if they are under 5). Only thing it the play area is rented out to group half the time so you really have to be there at the right time or you cannot use it.

title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
address: St James Church, 2 Sherriff Rd, London, NW6 2AP
place_title: The Sanctuary Cafe N W Ltd
review_text: Nice little place inside the church. Popular with mums given the childs play area. Nice coffee and interesting setting inside a church. Adjoining post office is very convenient. LGBTQ friendly.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: Lovely cafe in the woods. With good selection of drinks and desserts. And big outside sitting area. Next to the stadium, or something like that. They have toilets ;)

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: Love this place, the most friendly and lovely people work there. The food is tasty and the portions are huge, great value for money.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: Beautiful surroundings, magical feelings with all flowers around. In the middle of the park there is such a relaxing feeling to sit and have some bites. I recommend falafel. Very good. Great service.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: We ate here today for lunch, three adults with our three four year old kids. Things were pleasant enough until a woman staff member with long dark hair came over to us and told us to make our children be quiet. Yes our kids were excited to eat at the cafe, but were just behaving as kids do. Also we were surrounded by other families with young children who were also making noise. We happened to be seated at the table closest to the kitchen, and the staff member gestured to the kitchen as she told us to be quiet, implying that we were too noisy for the kitchen staff! I was surprised that a cafe situated in a park would be so family unfriendly. Have eaten here a few times before, but won't bother again. I don't like writing negative reviews, but hopefully the management will take notice of this feedback and retrain their staff on appropriate customer service skills.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text:
The coffee has never been great but the last time I went the service was bad and one of the waitresses pretty rude as well, which was honestly the most unpleasant thing about the experience. I was with my two children who ordered mac n cheese. After 20 minutes and after seeing that a table who ordered long after us was getting their food, I politely asked the waitress (a girl with long dark hair) to check if our order was coming too. She dismissed my question and just pointed at the kitchen. So I went over and said that we had been waiting a long time, and the “manager” quickly apologised and brought out the mac n cheese promptly.
I hope that Daisy Green will do a better job both in terms of service and quality of the coffee.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: When of the best spots to relax amazing customer service variety of food very friendly staff

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text:
What a blessing having a cafe in middle of woods in freezing day. Os nice location and atmosphere. Not a very impressive service without smile early morning i would say. Coffee was ok.
But its my day so let me jog on to spend it in my way 😊.

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text:
I just love the warm and charming atmosphere here. I usually come here for their amazing carrot cake, but today I decided to try breakfast and it was absolutely brilliant!
I had the crushed avocado, salmon and scrambled egg on sourdough toast and I have to say I loved every bite. The portion was generous and really satisfying too. A big thanks for this lovely Tuesday morning treat, can’t wait to come back again!

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text: I love these little places, they are real gems and stand out from the crowd. I entered by chance, on a rainy Monday while I was waiting for my motorbike to be serviced. The place is large, welcoming and the background music creates an atmosphere from another era. A working Monday morning but excellent, I think even on the weekend, I was tempted by a delicious turmeric latte accompanied by a ham and cheese croissant. The service is friendly, without frenzy. It reminds me of a university café in Bologna Italy. I recommend it.

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text:
This is a go to cafe and for once feels worth the money!!

They had so many options on the menu - I went for a southern style breakfast and lemonade with mint and was very impressed!

Generous portions (the lemonade comes with its own small jug so you get at least a few refills out of it)

Lovely fresh produce it was an absolute banquet with a selection of jams and delicious bread.

We also had excellent service from Jessie who was extremely patient and lovely to our group of 6!

Also seems to be a very popular spot for people to do work on their laptops

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text: Good food, great location, varied menu , nice coffee. Nice atmosphere- I’ve been to this cafe various times of day and on weekends and weekdays . During the week it’s a nice balance of parents with young kids, groups of adults and people working quietly. And during the weekend it’s a lot louder and fast paced with families and friends meeting for breakfast and brunch. They have lots of books for kids to read

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text:
This is my favourite cafe in the area. Good decently
Priced food, and nice vibe to work in

I wish they'd bring back salads they had on the old menu!! I go there to work in the afternoons, and the salad bar is often half empty / finished by the time i get there .

It also used to be open late, which gave it an edge over the other cafes in the area, but recently it's been closing early - hopefully that's temporary?

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text: This was probably some of the worst customer service we've ever experienced in our lives. The staff was awful, no warmth and  a condescending attitude that too was atrocious. The only possible reason for them to be employed there is if they're somehow related to the owners of the space. The space was cozy and the sweets were good and that's about it. A pity because the service tarnishes the overall experience.

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text:
Love this place, the vibe is so chilled. Food is different but great.
Took someone who hadn't been before and they said they'd go back

title: Cafe Z Bar.
address: 58 Stoke Newington High St, London, N16 7PB
review_text: This is one of my favourite brunch places in London. The menu is huge, which can often lead to disappointment, but here they get it right every time. The vegetarian breakfasts are great, and the Menem is truly amazing. Great coffee and friendly staff. Winner every time 🏅

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: I found this place quite nice overall. The food was tasty and well-prepared, though it was a bit frustrating that my order was initially forgotten, and I had to remind them about 2-3 times. Still, once it arrived, it was enjoyable though would've preferred not to eat alone.

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text:
Had a mushroom omelette, with chips and salad, and it was absolutely delicious. The omelette had plenty of mushrooms, and the portion of chips, was very generous. The salad was plentiful, and absolutely gorgeous. The prices are very reasonable also.
I would definitely recommend this cafe. Try it, you’ll be glad you did..

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: Such an amazing place for breakfast! I came here five days in a row for their Turkish breakfast and sujuk yumurta — everything was perfect. The staff are super friendly and make you feel good!

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text:
This place is a no-frills, no-nonsense greasy spoon in the best possible way. The kind of spot where the coffee is always hot, the grill is always on, and nobody’s pretending it’s anything other than comfort food done right.

The menu sticks to the hits—eggs, bacon, hash browns, burgers, melts—and everything comes out fast, filling, and exactly how you want it. My breakfast was cooked perfectly: crispy on the edges, generous portions, and seasoned like someone actually tasted it. The bacon had a proper crunch, the eggs were spot-on, and the toast showed up already buttered (a small detail, but it matters).

Service is friendly in that easy, familiar way—efficient, chatty if you want it, and happy to keep your coffee topped off. You get the sense a lot of regulars eat here, which is always a good sign.

Is it fancy? Absolutely not. Is it honest, satisfying, and worth every penny? 100%. If you’re craving real English breakfast without the gimmicks, this place delivers. Come hungry, leave happy, and don’t overthink it.

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: As a complete stranger to London, I happened upon this lovely cafe on a busy street corner in Islington near Moorfields Eye Hospital 's private wing. From massive breakfasts for £12, mixed grill (£16.50) to homemade 'Sucuklu Yumurta' (Pan fried eggs and Turkish sausage), they really do offer an eclectic selection. I opted for the very tasty cheese on toast (£6.50) and an excellent cappuccino (£3.50). I thought the prices extremely reasonable for London. Nice clean loos and free WiFi.  A great find!

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: An upgraded greasy spoon - decent food, relaxed music, but average food and terrible coffee. But service was very friendly and efficient, possibly a great place to enjoy with friends. Pricing a bit high - as you would expect given the nice atmosphere - but it’s shocking that “breakfasts” don’t include tea or coffee

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: This place was so close to being 5 stars for me, good prices, big portions, the mixed veg omelette and fries was really delicious, the beef and onion pie was so so. Mocha was big and delicious too. A really nice casual old fashioned diner at a street junction. Took one star off because the waitress somehow couldn't really understand us, and also couldn't understand the kitchen because she told us they ran out of pie and then we cancelled it, but then they just serve it to us even though we already decided not to have it. One of the item we ordered never arrived too.

title: Shepherdess Cafe
review_text: Great breakfast. Had to rush off half way thru, they very kindly kept it warm for me until I got back. They even put it on a new plate with fresh eggs and offered a fresh tea - Excellent service

title: The Coffee Bar
review_text: Didn't use it  bloke behind counter looks really happy

title: The Coffee Bar
review_text: Friendly staff

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
What a lovely restaurant dedicated to Princess Diana! From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere felt warm and welcoming, with beautiful photos of Princess Diana displayed all around the space. It was so special to learn that she was actually a regular here, which made the experience even more meaningful. The décor really pays tribute to her elegance and charm, and you can feel how much care went into making the restaurant a place that honors her memory.

On top of that, we brought along a friend who was named after the princess, which made our visit feel even more special and personal. The food was delicious and well-prepared, with great flavors that matched the unique and thoughtful setting. Overall, it’s not just a place to eat but also to soak in a bit of history and admire the legacy of someone so beloved. Definitely worth a visit!

title: Cafe Diana
review_text: This place is absolutely hideous there was hair in my eggs also my eggs were not cooked properly and it was raw and uncooked. Also the bacon is salty and the mushrooms were oily deep friend instead of grilled. Bacon was salty and the cooks weren’t wearing their uniforms instead they are wearing what they came from outside hygiene is absolute disgust I wouldn’t recommend for families or anyone to come here by the way the waitress was kind and nice to us

title: Cafe Diana
review_text: The food was very mediocre - chicken kebab was dry and luke warm. When we told the waitress she laughed. Cafe food at restaurant prices. Toilet was dirty and had no toilet paper, also smelt like a urinal.

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
Every year I’ve been attending this cafe especially for the occasional event Nottinghill Carvinal.

The breakfast menu is the best & the staff are so friendly, welcoming & very efficient with understanding your order.

I am very impressed with how nice & clean the decor is & everything is halal based which is really nice as well.

5 stars are well deserved for this & I will definitely be back here again very soon!

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
The staff is not too friendly and there is no music at all. There are pictures of Princess Diana on the walls and that's about it. Espresso costs the usual amount of money for the center of London - around 3£ ,but the coffee is good :)
*There are 2 rooms actually and the other one is the restaurant that looks much better than the cafe part!

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text:
Nice looking café
3 café workers, one, a barista in training
Saturday afternoon ~ 12.30 midday
Café not too busy
Lots of plates & cups still on tables
Barista trying to do orders
One attendant very busily wiping the same point on the counter… repeatedly… for a very long time

Was looking to get a toasted sandwich, and maybe a coffee (?)
But everyone here just looked completely disinterested in their job, so I left

And went to the Korean restaurant next door, and was served instantly. Nice.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text: I love this café. The food, service, and location are great. The A/C is also amazing here, which is great given how hot it is out at the moment.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text: Very nice place with good coffee and plenty of type of pastries, very kind service and an incredible selection of books and games in the shop. We didn't have the time to see the exhibitions but we'll definitely come back.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text:
Incredibly pleasant staff,
Wonderful hot chocolate made to accommodate my dietary needs,
The atmosphere is very lively, the place is beautifully clean,
Very minimalistic in the best way,
Service with a smile and incredibly efficient
Could not fault my experience ✨

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text: Great service. Good food. Comfortable environment.

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text:
Fantastic food which was Cooked and presented perfectly.
Delightful cup of tea. Of which I had 3 (witches brew ☕️).
Very cosy cafe and the staff are lovely.  Friendly atmosphere and everyone is happy with their food.
I highly recommend this cafe. Perfect for sitting in, outside seating or just to walk in and have a takeaway Xxxx 😁👍💋

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text: Found this little cafe whilst waiting to check in to our hostel. We ordered sausage, egg, chips and beans and tomato omelette. We was all satisfied with our meals and how much we got for the price. The egg, chips, sausage and beans was £6.10 and tomato omelette £6.20

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text: Great Cafe, reasonably priced food.

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text:
Disappointing, spent £60 on four breakfasts for myself and colleagues.
3/4 orders wrong, when questioned made out to be in the wrong myself, staff extremely rude, and confrontational.
Very over priced and poor quality of food!

If no stars were an option I would give them that!

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text: I remember coming here, I had work in the morning and a meeting soon after, so I had no breakfast, this place was close I came in tried it and it was very satisfying. It's a small place worth the stay and eat

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text:
I'm always astonished that when I come here it's not packed with customers. The family who own it are wonderful, the food is greasy spoon (however well done) and the decor is from the 50's.

Our family love it here. And the Spanish omelette is my favourite.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text:
A quiet, cozy cafe near Kentish town station. I stopped in for breakfast. It looked closed from the outside because the windows are so tinted but it is open 9-4:30 (4 on sundays).
Soft jazz was playing in the background. I had a croque madame. I liked that it came with some salad. The sandwich was decent but needed salt (as did the fries and salad) but luckily there were salt and pepper shakers on the table (my pet peeve is when they make you ask for this). Latte was just ok. Service was good. They have a kids menu.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text:
Local family run restaurant cafe that's been in business for years. Reasonable prices and fresh juices, smoothies to choose from!
The team are very accommodating here, not to mention the amazing choices on their menu, kudos to the chefs for keeping up with demand!
I love going here when I'm in the area and it's great to see they're going strong following the pandemic and rising costs. Worth a visit if you're in Kentish town.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text:
Wow! Sure, I was hungry getting there, but this is one of the best breakfasts I had in a very long time!

I went for the American breakfast for £10 and a cappuccino. Blueberry pancakes which had a great texture, eggs, bacon, syrup, sausage and some potatos. Man, this was great! This is my new go to place for breakfast on weekends!

The guy who served the food seemed really nice and took time to chat with the regulars without letting anyone wait for service.

Five starts without a doubt!

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text: A lovely bistro serving the most fantastic breakfasts! Not cheap but you get what you pay for, massive portion and perfectly cooked/prepared. So many breakfast options to choose from too.  Very relaxed environment,  beautiful decor and great service.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text: Came in with my girlfriend and had a perfect pappardelle con polpette and an excellent cappuccino. The service was lovely, I would highly recommend and I will definitely come again.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text: I love ❤ this place. It's a proper restaurant amongst so many cafe's in Kentish Town. It's a REAL gem where I regularly dined when I worked in the area. Fine wines, excellent menu, delightful staff, first class service and a cool, Boho vibe 😎 I no longer work in the area but still visit.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text: Reasonable prices and cute decor. French Toast is more like thick pancakes than sliced bread but tastes amazing. Dined there twice for brunch and both times delicious.

title: Cafe Renoir.
review_text: Cute little restaurant at a good value for money! The croque monsieur was delicious, although the crepe with chicken and peppers was not to our taste - too heavy on peppers. Would come again overall!

title: Market Cafe
review_text: It was an okay experience here but I can't say it was good. No problem with the staff or customer service they were all lovely but the food wasn't it. Croquettes tasted really dry and it only made it nearly good with the sauce next to the croquette, however the chicken was really nice. Just dont think the blue cheese dressing was a good fit for the chicken.

title: Market Cafe
review_text: I was recommended this spot by a friend and I've just had a wonderful lunch here. The burger is DELICIOUS and I couldn't recommend it enough. They offered me some hot sauce with my meal which I later found out was home made by the owner!  It was amazing! What a touch! Really lovely staff, quick service and very nice vibe. (p.s. they sell the hot sauce in house...I bought two bottles)

title: Market Cafe
review_text: I had a coffee on the patio and the staff we had was not very friendly at all. It felt like he was annoyed that we were asking for service. He was inpatient with us when we took a second to respond to what we wanted. He got us to pay right when he brought us the drinks which was totally fine. We asked to each get our own bills and he was visibly annoyed to do so. I got my bill and the total was written and there was a line that added an OPTIONAL service charge. The menu had stated that the tip was discretionary. Given the level of service I decided to give a 10% tip , which I still think is generous given the service I received, instead of the 20% it suggested on the bill given the attitude we got from the waiter. I gave him the money. Then the server came back and forced me to pay the full discretionary tip which I thought was soooo unprofessional and rude. When it said it was optional to pay that amount! I would never return here or recommend anyone to do the same. I also say him sternly telling other people off for moving the patio chairs to be in the sun. The whole situation just put me off. There are plenty of other places around and I would suggest going to any  other cute cafe.

title: Market Cafe
review_text:
A staple of the community. Market Cafe is one of those places that truly gets everything right. The service is consistently top tier — warm, attentive and genuinely welcoming.

I’m always a little hesitant now that we’re travelling with our four-month-old baba into social settings. Not all London restaurants are accommodating when it comes to little ones, but these guys get it right every single time. The demographic of the cafe is a testament to that.

People meeting parents. People on dates. People with babies. Girls’ lunches that have started with their first Prosecco. It’s all here at Market Cafe. They cater to the community in a way that feels genuinely unique — and uniquely Hackney.

The menu is varied, the quality of the food is outstanding, and the price point is incredibly reasonable for the area. And if you’re into a boozy brunch, their cocktails are delicious.

The atmosphere is relaxed and inviting, making it a true neighbourhood favourite and a key part of Broadway Market and London Fields. Couldn’t recommend it more.

title: Market Cafe
review_text: Horrible attitude from staff, this place is everything wrong with what Hackney has become. No sense of community, respect or kindness. So many better places in the area, spend your ££ at other (cheaper) venues w better vibes, better food & no 20% service charge for horrible service lol

title: Market Cafe
review_text: This awesome hangout is a longtime neighbourhood institution in Hackney. It's a great, relaxing place to gather with friends, either outside in the sunshine on the pavement in summer or inside during the winter months. Staff are absolutely fantastic and easy on the eyes! The locals are super cool, too. A great, unpretentious place for drinking and eating. The fry-up is recommended!

title: Market Cafe
review_text:
Slow service and coffee is not good. It's worth if you just want a quick break.
Great natural light coming from the big windows. The guy who welcomed us at the entrance was nice.

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: Brilliant shop! Went  as I collect the pins from all different Hard Rock locations. Went on Saturday and was busy but well worth it! Even explored The Vault and learnt so much! Was super cool to see all the memorabilia and the guy telling us everything was funny and informative. He even took pics of us to mimic Ringo and have fun exploring round answering any questions. Eould deffo go again and even grabbed more merch there! My brother even loved it as his 1st time in one and got some shirts and bandanas even blanket deal! Brilliant day out! 💜💜

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text:
Came to the vault to see the memorabilia. Not a very big place, there was a lovely guy there telling you all about the things and history of the Hard Rock cafe it was very interesting. It's definitely worth going

Keep rocking!!!

title: The Park Cafe
review_text:
Always a lovely experience at this cafe, it does get busy at the weekend but the staff work hard and the queue goes down quick enough. The coffee is delicious, especially nice to find good decaf coffee, and there's always a great selection of homemade food.

Its so nice to see thriving local businesses and would be such a shame to see anything about this cafe change!

title: The Park Cafe
review_text: Always excellent at any time of the day. Great staff and great food and drink. We love this cafe and definitely don't want it to change hands!!! Sign the petition to save our locely park cafe from the hands of another overpriced and generic chain.

title: The Park Cafe
review_text:
Shame, shame, shame. We attended Christmas Concert on 6/12 outside the cafe. Concert finished around 3:45 so we decided to grab mulled wine and a treat from the cafe. At the till I got drinks, mulled wine, cakes, ice creams. I asked if they are still serving food but there was a short answer ‘no’. Fair enough, they are closing soon and there is a massive queue still. I moved to the table area and the same man who served me shouted straight away: excuse me, no sitting down, we are closing in 5 min. I said: well you haven’t said that when I was just buying a bunch of food and drinks and I was asking about hot food!
Now either I need to wait until I eat at least half of it or I can refund it cause clearly I cannot carry it. He didn’t say anything, carried on serving on the till but nearly immediately another worker (Asian woman) came to tell me they are closing in 5min so I cannot sit down and  eat.
Honestly- disgraceful. You had a Christmas concert, you would benefit a lot from being open for another 30min. You should inform customers before they purchase food that they cannot eat it inside. All you wanted was to cash the money and kick people out as soon as they leave the till- not a great example of customer service. I ended up eating my cake in a rush, standing up and telling my child to hurry up.
By the way would be a great gesture for the choir members to at least treat them with a coffee- they brought you customers. But they were told to leave too!

title: The Park Cafe
review_text: I was very pleasantly surprised by this place. THE COFFEE IS AMAZING! Nutty, delicious, perfect. Very rare to come by in general, let alone in a park cafe. I tried a mushroom and emmenthal toasty, decent, not greasy, got the job done. The place has hipster, east London vibes, nothing fancy. Lots of Middle Eastern food options on the boards, which I’m looking forward to trying.

title: The Park Cafe
review_text:
The toilets were closed at 3:30pn on Saturday, the 15th of November 2025.

I asked the kitchen staff as he was visible at the side of the toilet area,could he open the toilet as I have a medical condition and need to use the toilet. The cafeteria staff member was so rude he said he worked in the kitchen and it’s nothing to do with him and to ask the park staff. if the park Café is open till 430pm they should be access to the toilet when having a café that serves food and drinks. The staff should be more enabling to be helping get the toilet open when it is attached to the building of the cafeteria.
This café is not cheap, and if you can’t even use the toilet when you’re having a drink, I would say this is a issue that needs to be addressed.

title: The Park Cafe
review_text: Loved this little cafe. Well-lit and warm service. Delicious breakfast options and good coffee!

title: The Park Cafe
review_text: Such an amazing location, and actually quite like the food. But it’s a 25*c day and the doors are locked because “the kids will all run through”. Honestly most ridiculous exclude from the till person I’ve heard for not opening doors to get a breeze on a hot day. Staff not open to feedback, should be better

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text: Excellent espresso, and the decaff machiato was very good too. Nice staff as well, and plenty of seats and space for a comfotable break.

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text: I love this place, I come here everyday to have my coffee, it’s so tasty. The staff is super nice and helpful. Even when it’s busy Francisco and the staff will always make sure you get the best service!

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text:
This used to be a part of the park, with trees, green grass, outdoor tennis courts -- a home to birds, to beautiful (intelligent) crows; a beautiful home to many many dogs throughout the day and early evening -- UNTIL it was covered in concrete, making profit for few.

We, the residents, need to protect such places because in poorer areas there is generally more corruption. It is not that easy to ruin parks and homes to wildlife where richer people live -- they have better resources to protect their homes

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text: The place is generally nice. Around 2pm kids come after school so if you want to work is better to get there in the morning. Food is ok. Coffee, however, is really bad, especially the flat white. I’ve tried it several times and it doesn’t improve - and you’re paying £3,75 for it! They need to start making better coffee.

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text: While I do wish the menu offered more in the way of lunch options (I go there after swimming class with my daughter), I have to give them five stars because the staff are incredibly friendly and helpful and they do their best with what they have. Moreover the seating area is very spacious and comfortable. We love having it there I never we are at the Britannia centre.

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text:
Over priced and the sandwiches taste stail and crusted over (had a cheese sandwich). Service very slow and once I had a panini again tasted stail but I had it in and the lady said she would bring it over. 25 mins went by and went up and still on the presser with the lid lifted up. No one to be seen. 10 more mins just standing there like a lemon then  someone slowly came out rolling her eyes and dragging her feet.  Plated my panini up and chucked it on the counter. Then another time I asked for a pot of tea and the man in front of me wanted his drinks in but all served in paper cups and then he pointed out he wanted in cups as staying in.  When I got mine the lady asks if I am staying in and I said well I have asked for a pot of tea so yet I am staying in.

Also very messy. Seems to be enough staff in to come out and clean as the place at times is disgusting but they seem to be more interested in hiding away in the back.

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text:
So slow. It took over 40 minutes for them to make a cheese toastie that they press in the presser for 2 minutes. Never eating from
here again.

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text: Superb brunch breakfast and lunch. Good place for snacks brunch. I enjoyed an eggs benedick and mocha and was pleasantly surprised. Good service and tables outside that they regularly disinfect between customers due to Covid19. Overall good quality and service. Very good fresh juices too

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text:
One of the nicer cake 🍰 shop in the area. The flat white is amazing, it’s smooth and well balanced coffee. The cake portions are however too big. I wish they had smaller or half portions. We were feeling sad about leaving some cake. The apple pie was pretty crunchy and delicious. A bit pricy but makes for an amazing Saturday evening coffee.

As expected, I came back here!

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text:
Let me start with the concept. A chef that's up and coming, is enabled with the facility to fulfill his or her dreams and have a restaurant to impress the public. Fantastic idea ! We were treated to the Greek odessey.

The food was good. Not spectacular but good. Interesting, different and a nice experience.

Our staff, big shout out to Lizzie who was our waitress for the night. Absolutely impeccable. Friendly, attentive and funny. Impeccable service. Callum the barman was great too and kept our glasses topped up and put up with us through the night.

Thoroughly enjoyed the experience and loved the concept of the restaurant . Will be intrigued to see what comes next and hope to return.

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text:
The best cafè in Primrose Hill.
Cozy and welcoming inside, wide range of food and drinks to choose from and constantly delicious.
This has been our favourite spot in the neighbourhood and let me assure you, it never fails to amaze us.
The owner is always cheerful, funny and polite, and always find the time to have a chat with us and really make us feel welcome.
Whether you're after breakfast, brunch, lunch or just a coffee, this is the perfect spot❤

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text:
Super nice place for the breakfast , nice menu and bakery .
The omelet was amazing , especially the taste , has been served with toast and salad.
The pace has perfect alternative coffee , V60 was really nice.
Fresh tasty croissants.
Defiantly will recommend this place for the breakfast time

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text: Chamomile cafe never disappoints. We've been visiting frequently since we moved to the neighborhood and every time the quality of food is incredible. Very good spot to have a brunch with family or a catch up with a friend over coffee. Staff is very friendly and attentive

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text: Really friendly and helpful staff. The coffee was great too. I also had two of their energy balls which were nice, though one would have been enough. You also need a larger coffee to wash them down or they get a bit cloying - I only had an espresso.

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text: Tay Do serves some of the most authentic and flavorful Vietnamese food around. The bun thit nuong, banh xeo, bun cha, pho and Hai Nam Chicken Rice are all top-notch. The staff are incredibly friendly and attentive, and the owner’s passion shines through in both the food and the warm, welcoming atmosphere. A true hidden gem — we’ll definitely be back!

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text:
The food is cheap and banging
1️⃣bun rieu
soup base is sour and slightly sweet, the crab meatball is even bigger than my spoon😂crazy!!!
And it is packed with flavour and super juicy
Staff are friendly and always smiling
2️⃣Summer rolls(Pork and Prawns)
Authentic and yummy
Dipping sauce - I can’t taste any peanut in it , it is like only hoisin sauce

Honestly, it tastes like something straight out of a Vietnamese home kitchen.
Definitely coming back!

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text: We were curious about this place as it's been advertised on social media a lot, but it was a disappointment. Food looked better than it tasted. Very plain and tastless Pho, same as the traditional Vietnamese soup we ordered. Service could be a bit nicer too, not very welcoming...

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text:
a Loyal customer，but treated with clear discrimination and disrespect！

I have been coming to this restaurant regularly for a long time, easily more than ten times. To be honest, the service has never been good, but I accepted it because it is extremely close to my home and the prices are cheap. The food is average, nothing special, but convenient and affordable, so I kept coming back.

Usually the service is poor but tolerable. I have noticed clear differences in how Asian customers, Black customers, foreign white customers and local British customers are treated. There is one older gentleman who is always polite and kind, and he is honestly the only reason I still return.

However, my last visit was deeply upsetting.

There were plenty of empty tables, yet the older woman who I believe is the owner insisted I sit by the door where someone had hung their coat on the chair. I explained at least three times, but she ignored me and spoke in a very unpleasant, commanding tone. Eventually she moved us to another table without any apology.

We have supported this restaurant many times, sometimes spending up to £80 in one visit, and even recommended it to friends. I do not understand how regular customers can be treated this way.

From the moment we sat down, we were rushed constantly. During a 40 minute visit, we were asked to leave and had our plates taken away at least five times, even though we were still eating. Finally, the same woman came over and said “I need this table.” No “please”, no “sorry”, no smile, no thank you. Just rude and dismissive.

We were eating for around 30 minutes, which is completely normal. If we had stayed an unreasonable amount of time, I would understand. But being pressured and rushed like this was extremely uncomfortable, especially when the nearby table of three local white customers was being chatted with and treated warmly. As an Asian customer, this felt like clear discrimination.

I grew up in an Asian family in Dublin. My parents always taught me to treat everyone with the same respect, regardless of race, background or status. They worked their way up in professional fields such as law and accounting by being fair, honest and respectful to every client. That is how we earned our place in this society, and that is why I was able to study for a Master’s and now a PhD.

Seeing someone from the same background behave with such unfairness and bias towards others is honestly heartbreaking.

This experience was disappointing and upsetting. I will still return if the kind older gentleman is working, but if that woman is there, I will turn around and leave. I will also no longer recommend this restaurant to friends.

Respect costs nothing, but it makes all the difference.

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text: as a local this is where we go as group and is our food spot. The food, the portion, the people, the interior just makes you feel like you’re eating home cooked food in grandparents kitchen with friends. Good for the soul. Highly recommend !

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text:
Went here on a whim and no regrets. The restaurant is fairly large and the people running it are very friendly.

Food arrived quickly and the portions are large. I had the shaking beef while my dining companions ordered pho and the Vietnamese pancake. We also ordered a papaya salad to share.

The papaya salad was refreshing and the veggies nice and crisp. The shaking beef had a really yummy sauce. I wish the beef was just slightly more tender. The char on it was delicious and the sauce was great with the rice on the side.

I look forward to coming back here and trying more on the menu!

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text: We used to visit this restaurant regularly in the past, and we went back a few weeks ago. For sure, it was our last time. Shocking service!! The waitress was very abrupt, and she didn't have any customer service skills. We ordered many dishes, and they tried to bring them all at the same time!! Unbelievable!!

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: Stopped by for a quick coffee break, had a white americano. Although is a very small place, it is very nice and the service was also great. The coffee, it did not look the great but actually had a nice taste!

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: The hosts are very polite, the food taste really good and the proportions are quite filling as well. The place is hygienic and well maintained. Overall a good, budget friendly place in London for breakfast/lunch.

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: Have to say this place indeed has the best coffee. Will go back again for coffee and some other dessert next time. The staffs are also great!

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: This little cafe is amazing! The food was delicious and the staff was so nice. Definitely the best place I ate during my stay in London

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: This is the best coffee shop I’ve been to in long time. Their organic coffee is delicious and cakes to die for. The blasting music puts you straight in the mood and guys there are just amazing.

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text:
This was such a lovely coffee shop that I am so glad I stumbled upon during my work trip to Barbican and East End area. The woman who owns it is truly so kind and made me feel so welcomed and invited. Her coffee is delicious, the food is so fresh. She bakes many of the goods in the shop. I had a flat white and Mediterranean sandwich and they were both very fresh, affordable and tasted great.

I came in here twice during my trip that's how much I enjoyed it. Overall a great place if you need to get some work done on your laptop and enjoy a coffee. Warm and inviting place and locally owned. Highly recommend.

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text: Three of us came here in February on a Saturday afternoon, which was unusually quiet in this normally bustling commercial area. We consulted with the owner and had sandwiches and an omelette, all freshly made according to our tastes and wishes. The food and service was exceptionally good. We thoroughly enjoyed our food and stay.

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
Where to start!
Came here for my dogs birthday - they had a great menu for dogs - we did the make your own bowl and she loved it !
Came so pretty. Then they treated her to a little birthday desert platter! So cute and really made our day.
The human food was great, both food and drinks are so fresh.
Beautiful decor and atmosphere and the staff are so so kind!
Overall a fantastic experience and will be recommending to anyone and everyone

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
The cafe provides a safe and engaging environment for our four-legged friends to let loose and have fun, making it a perfect spot for dog owners looking to treat their pets to a day of play.

As for the food offerings, Pawsitive Cafe doesn't disappoint. The menu boasts a wide variety of options for both humans and dogs, catering to all tastes and preferences. From freshly brewed coffees and delicious brunches for the humans to gourmet dog treats and pet-friendly meals for the canine companions, there's something for everyone to enjoy. I was particularly impressed by the thought and care put into creating a menu that caters to both species, ensuring that no one is left out of the dining experience.

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
I absolutely love this place! They have great staff, plenty of food options for humans and dogs, and the atmosphere is popping. They play good music and every patron is friendly. Pawsitive even has a resident Dalmatian named Domino (the sweetest dog ever) that greets everyone upon arrival.

People can bring their dog or not, the food is great! This restaurant is family owned and you can tell they put some much love into running the place.

Thanks again and I will return with my doggy, she loves the tea service.

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
My shih tzu had an amazing time in this cafe, and so did we!

Which makes this place so special is that there is a menu for the dogs, I ordered the beef steak for my dog and as you can see he pulled out all the tricks with excitement to eat his meal, he devoured his food with such delight to be involved in the experience.

There is a small interactive play area at the back of the restaurant which includes a ball pit and a mini obstacle course, which my dog master by the end of his time here.

As for humans, there are a variety of food options, from brunch, to burgers and surprisingly Malaysian dishes. We had the Nasi Lemak and Buttermilk Chicken, with the mocktails London Calling and Passion Paradise, which was all amazing and very tasty.

The staff are super lovely and friendly with the dogs coming in, and very helpful.

Would definitely recommend coming here for a doggy day out

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
Brought our Shiba Inu for his birthday brunch and it was great!
We received a warm welcome and we had a great experience (we were the only customers present).
Our pup was the main guest and the staff went above and beyond to make him feel special.
We ordered the steak with veggies for him and the staff was so polite to celebrate him also with a birthday pupcake! Very much appreciated!

The human food was ok, cocktails too..
Recommended for a fun afternoon!

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text: Nice ambience, friendly staff and good service. The food was delicious especially the truffle fries and mushroom roast. My dog enjoyed his scrambled eggs too!

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text: Celebrated my Pomeranian’s birthday at Pawsitive Cafe and we had the best experience! I ordered a birthday cake for him, and it was honestly the cutest thing ever. My pup very much enjoyed it and as for me, I thought their brunch menu was pretty decent as well! Thoroughly enjoyed the salt & pepper squid.

title: Pawsitive Cafe
review_text:
Had a really nice time here with our pup, never normally one to leave reviews but this place is exceptionally good. The staff are incredibly friendly and asked many questions about our pup. Was a great place to meet and talk to others as well as allowing our dog to socialise.

Will definitely return. The place was clean and very well thought through with domino (the owners Dalmatian) dressed as Santa giving all the dogs a treat as they passed by. Food and atmosphere was amazing, with reasonable prices.
